observations.recommendation: We recommended and the CDRRM Officer agreed to coordinate with the concerned offices to submit the necessary documents for the implementation of PPAs and problems encountered that caused the non-implementation or delay, so that the former can bring up these issues and concerns to the CDRRMC for immediate resolution.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the CDRRM Officer agreed to include in its LDRRMFIP all PPAs identified by the CDRRMC, including the list of PPAs to be charged against the unexpended balance and submit the same to CPDO for incorporation into the AIP.
observations.managementAction: and the CDRRM Officer agreed to include in its LDRRMFIP all PPAs identified by the CDRRMC, including the list of PPAs to be charged against the unexpended balance and submit the same to CPDO for incorporation into the AIP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management to direct the City Engineer to prioritize the immediate implementation of the projects as the design provided under Section B, paragraph 10 of the related MOA for the constituents to enjoy the benefits from the corresponding infrastructure improvement and equipment upgrade.
observations.managementAction: and Management directed the City Engineer to prioritize the immediate implementation of the projects as the design provided under Section B, paragraph 10 of the related MOA for the constituents to enjoy the benefits from the corresponding infrastructure improvement and equipment upgrade.
observations.reason: The design of the building structure.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management to instruct the City Accountant to send letters and demand letters to the concerned contractors to exert more effort to collect the advances to contractors by exhausting all legal means for recovery. In addition, strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth in Annex E of Revised IRR of RA 9184 in the granting and recoupment of advances to contractors.
observations.managementAction: and Management agreed to instruct the City Accountant to send letters and demand letters to the concerned contractors to exert more effort to collect the advances to contractors by exhausting all legal means for recovery.
observations.reason: Contractors did not respond to the demand.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management follow up the status of implemented PPAs to ensure timely implementation and the realization of benefits to the constituents as envisioned in the Annual Investment Plan.

observations.reason: There are still PPAs that are unimplementable due to problems like road right-of-way.

observations.recommendation: We recommend and Management to revisit the MOA and ensure the compliance of both parties to the terms and conditions contained in it to ensure the realization of the goals and objectives of the said program and to confer with the recipients’ obligation in the MOA and remind them of their obligation set forth under the MOA.
observations.managementAction: that Management revisit the MOA and ensure the compliance of both parties to the terms and conditions contained in it to ensure the realization of the goals and objectives of the said program.
observations.reason: Recipients’ misconception concerning the terms under the MOA resulted in non-settlement of their obligations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management retrieve the documents from different focal offices to establish the probability of collections of the long outstanding receivables including the determination of the location of the responsible persons or known address: wit: City Agriculture Office, City Cooperative Office, CSWD Office and NGOs/POs. Issue demand letters, if applicable, and if collection is nil, request the write-off of the dormant receivable accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005. Finally, disclose to the Notes to the Financial Statements to present fairly and free of material misstatements the financial statements of the City of Bislig.
observations.managementAction: Management to instruct the City Accountant to retrieve documents from different focal offices and issue demand letters, if applicable, and request the write-off of dormant receivable accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005; and disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.reason: Absence of known address of NGOs/POs and responsible persons.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the City Treasurer to finish as soon as possible the verification made relative to the outstanding PDAF funds which is in the possession of said LGU-Bislig City and thereafter, instruct the City Treasurer to immediately deposit the said funds to the account of the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: Direct the City Treasurer to finish as soon as possible the verification made relative to the outstanding PDAF funds which is in the possession of said LGU-Bislig City and thereafter, instruct the City Treasurer to immediately deposit the said funds to the account of the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.reason: Unknown source of the outstanding PDAF funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the misstatement of the PPE accounts accordingly by rectifying the error of capitalizing amounts that should be expensed/charged to the prior period adjustments so that the PPE accounts are presented at their correct carrying amounts and to ensure strict compliance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS I and Section 4 of COA Circular No. 2022-004, dated May 31, 2022.
observations.managementAction: Determine
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has yet to finalize the closure of the capitalization threshold.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant intensify the reconciliation of their records, one fund per accounting office for quarter for Cash-Local and the Treasury and one bank account per month, and effect appropriate adjustments in their respective records/cashbook, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliati on of the records
observations.reason: Difficulty in tracing the discrepancies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: a. Municipal Engineer to take immediate action to settle the aforementioned deficiency and avoid incurring the same deficiency in future projects; b. Municipal Accountant to monitor the actions taken by the former on the rectification of the deficiency and if warranted, effect consequently settlement; and c. Bids and Awards Committee Technical Working Group to review the program of works or the basis of the Approved Budget of the Contract before proceeding to the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented
observations.reason: No justifications were given.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to: a. Immediately prepare the list of all real property tax delinquencies and establish a proper monitoring mechanism for the subsequent collection of delinquencies; b. Intensify collection efforts on delinquent Real Property Taxes and institute appropriate administrative and judicial remedies, if necessary, to enforce tax compliance from delinquent taxpayers; and c. Henceforth, strict adherence to the provisions of R.A. 7160, specifically the enforcement of administrative and/or judicial actions for the collection of Real Property and Special Education Tax.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2023 the updated list of current accounts and accounts in arrears will be prepared; percentage of collectibility per year of delinquency is yet to be determined.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to: Direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Ensure the setting up of RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year, supported with a certified list from the Municipal Treasurer showing the taxpayers’ names and amounts due based on the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/Taxpayer’s index card, and the proper recording of subsequent facilitation of RPT/SET collection; b. Make necessary adjustments by recognizing the RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Income based on the list of delinquent taxpayers. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to: a. Provide the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the name and the amount due and collectible for the current year based on the RPTAR as the basis for setting up the beginning balance of RPT and SET Receivables, respectively; and b. Furnish the Provincial Accountant with the summary of the JEVs showing the breakdown of the amounts Due to LGUs pertaining to the share of the Provincial Governments and Barangays and the Abstract of Real Property Tax Collection. c. Direct the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal Accountant to collaborate efforts in generating accurate/reliable data on RPT and SET receivable balances by indicating the names of taxpayers and amounts due from them, including penalties therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Accounting Office will set-up the percentage of collectibility per year of delinquency once the certified list of RPT/SET accounts is handed over.
observations.reason: Waiting for the certified list of delinquent taxpayers; preparation of necessary entries and adjustments pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Direct the Municipal Engineering Office to employ necessary measures to expedite the implementation of stalled PPAs, such as but not limited to a rendition of overtime and hiring of appropriate personnel to do the lagging duties; b. Advise the Municipal Development Council to properly screen the PPAs and prioritize those that are readily implementable and procurement-ready. In the same way, thoroughly plan the suggested PPAs in relevance to the capacity of LGU to implement such to prevent wastage of resources due to delay or non-implementation; and c. Henceforth, strict compliance to the provisions set forth under DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented
observations.reason: Several PPAs still not implemented as of current year; reprogramming of unexpended 20% DF pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to comply with the following: a. Set up a reliable means to log attendances, e.g., use of biometrics, and require the approving officials in the DTRs to review and make sure that the time entries indicated therein would match those in the attendance log maintained. b. Compel all JO/COS Personnel to individually prepare and submit accomplishment reports which are accurate and detailed enough to depict actual duties and activities performed. c. On the subsequent renewal, kindly indicate on the contracts or memorandum of assignment and ensure that the JO/COS know their duties and functions, including the office/location of assignment, before the rendition of their services. d. Strengthens payroll processing controls by ensuring that payrolls are only prepared and processed if duly supported with corroborating documents, such as the DTRs and accomplishment reports. e. Require the submission of PDS, a copy of identifications, and other government-issued clearances to validate the identity of hired JO/COS personnel.
observations.managementAction: Approving officials reviewed DTRs together with the accomplishment reports; Government issued clearance is yet to be complied.
observations.reason: MOA is subject for revision and the updated PDS submission is yet to be complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management religiously enforce and adhere to the prescribed procedures as stipulated in COA Circular No. 2020-006 to properly conduct inventory taking of its PPE accounts. Thereby, the prepared reports thereon would be trustworthy enough to substantiate the said accounts in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The new property officer will be sent for trainings and workshops.
observations.reason: The new property officer is still familiarizing her duties and responsibilities; also, there was no proper turnover of documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to require the MEE Administrator to strictly impose the collection of the balances from the delinquent stallholders, and if the delinquent stallholders failed to pay after due notifications, revoke the contract of lease, in consonance with the MEEDO Code of Barobo.
observations.managementAction: MEE Administrator imposed measures in collecting the delinquent stallholders, and if the stallholders failed to pay after due notifications, revoke the contract of lease, in consonance with the MEEDO Code of Barobo.
observations.reason: Leniency in the collection was imposed; collection still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create an Inventory Committee and conduct a physical count of PPE in accordance with the guidelines and procedures as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006, dated January 31, 2020.
observations.reason: An Inventory Committee was already created, but it was unable to conduct a complete physical inventory taking, as reiterated in AAR 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to comply with the required mandatory positions set in the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CS Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014, to institutionalize the MDRRMO's organizational structure and manpower to carry out the DRRM plans and programs in the long run.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. If a particular account had no more specific purpose, return immediately to the source funding Agency. Henceforth, always ensure that financial transactions are well accounted for and sufficiently disclosed to assure reliability on the Financial Statements.

Further, direct the Inventory Committee to complete the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) and submit the same to the Appraisal Committee to determine the appraised value of the said properties.

Refrain from entering a similar kind of transaction in the future, c. Effect payment only to the contracted and rightful supplier, and Follow strictly the provisions of RA 9184 regarding the payment using the letter of credit.

Consequently, direct the Municipal Accountant to compute the corresponding accumulated depreciation expense of the above-mentioned accounts and record thereafter in books of account of the LGU. c. Henceforth, observe inter-office coordination and communication for a cohesive and organized record-keeping of accounts.

observations.reason: Non- coordination between the Accounting Office and the Engineering Office

Direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the amount pertaining to the road lot as part of the reported book value of the Road Networks account.

Ensure that the Property Officer, together with the Inventory Committee, conduct an actual physical count of PPEs, annually. c. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer or designated personnel to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and cause the immediate disposal of unserviceable assets following the pertinent guidelines in supply and property management; d. Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that properties are properly recorded and capitalized, thereafter, apply appropriate systematic depreciation of assets.

observations.reason: There is still the collector who doesn’t consistently comply with this requirement.

observations.reason: On-going implementation

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to properly enforce and adhere to the prescribed procedures stipulated in COA Circular No. 2020-006 to properly conduct a one-time cleansing of PPE accounts so that the prepared reports would be trustworthy enough to substantiate the accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to the following: a. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify collection efforts on delinquent Real Property Taxes, and cause the posting of Notices of Tax Delinquency on the LGU’s conspicuous places and serving the same directly to delinquent taxpayers to enforce tax compliance; b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer, in cooperation with the Municipal Assessor, to update the listing of taxable real properties within the jurisdiction of the Municipality and seek the assistance of the Provincial Treasurer and Assessor, if necessary; c. Provide additional manpower in the RPT collecting division and instruct the Municipal Treasurer to provide the necessary number of personnel required for the effective RPT collection operations of the LGU; and d. Henceforth, strict adherence to the provisions of RA 7160, specifically the enforcement of administrative and/or judicial actions for the collection of Real Property and Special Education Tax.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower

observations.recommendation: We recommended: a. Require the appropriate office to ensure that all Lands owned by the municipality are duly registered under the name of the LGU Lingig and governme nt center copies of the corresponding Torrens Certificate of Title (TCT). b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert best efforts to trace the acquisition of the unrecorded real properties and determine the appropriate cost to be recognized in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Lack of proof of acquisition

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Assessor secure copies of the certificates of title (OCT/TCT) of all land owned by the LGU and the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer, obtain a list of real properties owned by the Municipality, and take appropriate action to determine the appropriate cost to be recognized in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Verification of land ownership and reconciliation of property records remain ongoing due to incomplete supporting documents and The volume of historical records and competing responsibilities of key personnel have contributed to delays in full implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer submit a justification for charging the ABACA Festival expenses to the Establishment of Cultural Development Facility appropriation under the 20% DF without proper authority and adhere faithfully to Section 4.1 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Still looking for the minutes during the 2023 ABACA Festival meeting.
observations.reason: Still looking for the minutes during the 2023 ABACA Festival meeting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant fast-track the reconciliation of records and then remit the correct amount per reconciliation to GSIS, HDMF, and PhilHealth to adhere to the provisions relative to GSIS, HDMF, and PhilHealth and prevent any fines or additional costs that the Municipality and its employees might incur as a result of late remittances.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation remains ongoing
observations.reason: Reconciliation remains ongoing due to the extensive validation of GSIS, HDMF, and PhilHealth records against HR service records dating back to 2007. The volume and complexity of historical data continue to affect the pace of implementation

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the concerned personnel on the following: a. Municipal Accountant to return the excess funds from completed projects upon receipt, and communicate with the Source Agencies on what to do with the non-moving funds; and b. Municipal Health Officer to utilize available funds to provide better services to the public.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office was not furnished with copies of the relevant MOAs and receipts upon fund receipt making it difficult to determine specific conditions and allowable uses. Efforts are ongoing to retrieve the necessary documentation from concerned offices and source agencies to enable full implementation.
observations.reason: Not implemented due to difficulty to determine specific conditions and allowable uses. Efforts are ongoing to retrieve the necessary documentation from concerned offices and source agencies to enable full implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to communicate with the Source Agencies on what to do with the unutilized/excess funds.
observations.managementAction: Subject for reconciliation & liquidation.
observations.reason: Subject for reconciliation & liquidation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agree to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the documents containing adequate information as basis for the recording of the Other Investment Account.
observations.managementAction: Supporting documents could no longer be traced as the transaction dates back for more than 15 years.
observations.reason: Supporting documents could no longer be traced, as the transaction was back for more than 15 years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure accurate assessment of business taxes by following the proper procedures as specified in the Local Treasury Operations Manual (LTOM) and by checking of books of accounts and/or ledger of business establishment with corresponding Sworn Statement Gross Receipts/Sales and Income Tax Return in accordance to Items (d) & (e) of Section 2C.04 of the Municipal Revised Revenue Code of 2016. Moreover, correct the erroneous interpretation in the computation of taxes based on the tax table.
observations.managementAction: Still in the process of revising the revenue code.
observations.reason: Still in the process of revising the revenue code.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Engineer take immediate action to settle the aforementioned deficiency and avoid incurring the same deficiency in future projects; (b) Municipal Accountant monitor the actions taken by the former on the rectification of the deficiency and if warranted, effect settlement; and (c) Bids and Awards Committee Technical Working Group review the program of works or the basis of the Approved Budget of the Contract before proceeding to the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The Management has directed the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Accountant, and BAC TWG to implement the recommendations. The Municipal Engineer has taken initial steps and the Municipal Accountant is to monitor.
observations.reason: The deficiencies have not yet been fully resolved; initial steps have been taken by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to direct the Municipal Treasurer to: (a) Locate/obtain the documents of the motor vehicles and facilitate the processing of registrations and insurance coverage of the motor vehicles; and (b) Regularly monitor the expiration and renewal of all motor vehicles registration to avoid penalties or fines for late/non-registration.
observations.managementAction: The Management has directed the Municipal Treasurer to obtain the necessary documents for the registration of the vehicles. The Municipal Treasurer is still obtaining the necessary documents for the registration of unregistered vehicles.
observations.reason: The Municipal is still obtaining the documents for the registration of unregistered vehicles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management prepare a 5-Year Business Plan for every Local Economic Enterprises (LEE) to provide a clear direction of the operation aimed towards financial viability and sustainability.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive has issued a memorandum designating technical working groups to focus on drafting the business plan for the Local Economic Enterprise (LEE) of LGU San Agustin. All necessary data have been compiled, except for the financial data. The team is currently in the initial phase of gathering the necessary data and documents.
observations.reason: Due to the change in the scheme of the LGU's water system, the data parameters of the 5-year business plan will be revised.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management follow up its application for land titling to ensure that all lands owned by the LGU are registered under the Torrens Title System in pursuant to the provisions of Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: The Management has followed up on its land titling application. There were 5 approved titles out of eight (8) properties; 1 is timberland. The LGU is fast tracking the titling of the acquired lots.
observations.reason: Complexity of process in titling of the land lots. However, there were 5 approved titles out of eight (8) properties, 1 is timberland.

6.7. We recommended, and the City Accountant agreed to review and
analyze the discrepancies between the City's and the concerned
Barangays’ records and prepare necessary adjustments to both
records to arrive at a reconciled amount.

7.7. We recommended, and the:
7.7.1. City General Services Office and the City Assessor's Office
agreed to conduct regular inventory of the land items owned
by the City government and fast-track the titling process to
secure its ownership and protect these land items from
unlawful settlers or occupants; and
7.7.2. City Accountant agreed to ensure that the Transfer Certificate
of Title (TCT) of the newly acquired land is transferred in the
name of the City before full payment is made to the seller.

8.7. We recommended, and the City General Services Officer agreed to:
8.7.1. Retrieve, reconstruct, and reconcile missing supporting
documents, including delivery receipts and inspection and
acceptance reports, to facilitate payment processing;
8.7.2. send confirmation letters to the creditors of the recorded
outstanding payables to ensure the accuracy of recorded
liabilities; and
8.7.3. strengthen internal document tracking and storage protocols
to prevent misplacement of key records, particularly during
management transitions, and establish accountability for
document handling.

9.6. We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to reconcile the
outstanding balance with the records of the source agencies. Prepare
adjusting entries to correct the balance in the books after the
reconciliation, if necessary.

11.8.1. ensure that all contracts explicitly define the duties,
responsibilities, and required working hours of personnel to
facilitate proper monitoring, performance evaluation, and
alignment with government service needs while maximizing
their services to ensure that wages paid are commensurate
with actual work rendered; and
11.8.2. conduct periodic reviews of job orders, contractual, and
consultancy assignments to verify that their actual tasks align
with their designated roles, ensuring efficiency and preventing
task mismatches.

12.18. We recommended, and the:
12.18.1. City Accountant agreed to immediately stop withholding GS
(Government Share) Pag-IBIG contributions in excess of the
prescribed rate, in accordance with Pag-IBIG Circular No. 460
and R.A. 9679. Additionally, for the employees' share,
establish a written policy and agreement to formalize the
process for upgrading contributions, ensuring that any
additional deductions are voluntary, properly documented,
and compliant with regulatory requirements; and
12.18.2. Human Resource Management Officer agreed to secure a
"Request to Update Membership Savings" form for those
employees who wish to contribute more than the mandatory
contribution.

13.16. We recommended and the City Accountant, the City HRM Officer, and
the City Treasurer agreed to expedite the verification and
reconciliation of these accounts and immediately process the
remittance of all outstanding balances to GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and
PhilHealth within the prescribed period to avoid penalties and interest
charges. Additionally, we recommend regular monitoring and
reconciliation procedures to improve compliance with government
regulations and ensure accuracy of reported balances at each reporting
date.

14.11. We recommended, and the GAD Focal Point Person agreed to ensure
the timely submission of the GAD Plan and Budget and GAD
Accomplishment Report to the Office of the Auditor within the
prescribed timeline set forth in the relevant circular. Moreover,
develop the GAD Agenda in accordance with the prescribed format,
ensuring the inclusion of all required annexes, to effectively establish
a strategic framework for achieving the City's GAD objectives.

proposed Journal Entry outlined below upon receipt of the certificates

immediately the copies of Certificates of Completion to the City
Accounting Office and every month thereafter.

8.16. We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to: (i) communicate
with the PhilHealth Surigao City Branch that the premiums in June
and July 2016, and August 2018 were already remitted in prior years
and submit complete documentation thereof; and (ii) adjust the
December 31, 2024 balance of Due to PhilHealth to reflect only the
actual valid unpaid premiums per SPA (December 2024) of
₱750,516.25.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
adjusting entries to record the transfer of the unexpended/unobligated
balance of the LDRRMF for CYs 2023 and 2024 amounting to
₱1,390,040.81 and ₱2,062,189.64, respectively, and record unexpended
balance of the LDRRMF in future years in the pertinent calendar year of
incurrence.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
adjusting entries to correct the misclassified asset accounts with its
corresponding accumulated depreciation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary
adjusting entries to derecognize the issued semi-expendable properties
in the amount of ₱5,891,468.59 and to recognize the pertinent expense
or government equity accounts.

We recommended that the:
4.9.1 Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile the
differences between their respective records on a periodic basis
to arrive at the correct real property tax and special education tax
collectible balances; and
4.9.2 Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entry to fairly present the
actual tax collectible on the Statement of Financial Position based
on the certified list of tax delinquencies prepared by the Municipal
Treasurer.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting
entries to correct the under-provision of depreciation expenses and
recognize depreciation from the date of completion which shall be for
one month if the PPE is available for use on or before the 15th of the
month, and on the succeeding month if the PPE is available for use after
the 15th of the month.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
adjusting entries to reflect the correct amount of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and accountable
officers concerned exhaust all possible actions to prioritize the
liquidation of fund transfers and request the Source Agencies to apply
for a write-off of dormant accounts.

We recommended and the management agreed that the:
8.10.1. Municipal Treasurer’s Office prepare and forward a complete
statement of water meter reading from each consumer, and
submit the same to the Municipal Accounting Office for
recording in the books of accounts; and
8.10.2. Municipal Accountant record Waterworks System Fees and
Accounts Receivable by ₱913,289.03, Receipt from Market
Operation and Operating Lease Receivable by ₱243,000.00, use
the accrual basis thereafter, prepare a subsidiary ledger per
tenant, and reconcile monthly with the MTO records.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Reclassify the balances recorded under Accounts Payable to their
appropriate accounts; and
b. Revert to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, the
accounts payable in the amount of ₱159,367.88, which has been
outstanding for more than 10 years and have no claim,
administrative or judicial, has been filed or which is not covered by
perfected contracts.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the
adjusting journal entry to reflect the correct amount of the affected
accounts.

We recommended and the management agreed that the:
11.9.1. Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to correct the
recognition of prior period income in the current year, and
request information/data/report from the MT for year-end
undeposited collections which will only be forwarded the
following year; and
11.9.2. Municipal Treasurer report all collections for the year even
including those due for deposit in the ensuing year.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to monitor
closely the liquidations of the fund transfers received by the LGU to
ensure the accuracy and validity of reported balances in the Financial
Statements of Due to NGA account, and make the necessary adjusting
journal entry to reflect the correct amount of the affected accounts.

6.7 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO)
regarding the existence of stale checks and sent written notices
to the concerned payees before checks become stale;
b. For checks which became stale and which require replacement,
issue a new check upon submission of the spoiled or stale
check to the MTO; and
c. Take up the adjustments in the books of accounts for those
which have become stale for a longer period, and no
manifestation for replacement from the concerned payees was
received.

7.6 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to exert all
efforts to investigate, validate, and substantiate the reconciling
items; and thereafter promptly record these in the books.

8.11 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Coordinate with the former Municipal Accountant to explore all
possible means to retrieve and establish detailed information on
the dormant accounts and, if feasible, demand settlement from
the accountable officers or concerned personnel; and
b. Prepare a written request for the write-off of receivable accounts
pursuant to the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2023-
008 if, despite exhausting all efforts, retrieving and establishing
the details prove impossible.

9.8 We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant and the GSO-designate reconcile their
respective PPE records to enable the implementation of the
planned one-time property cleansing and facilitate the proper
disposal of verified unserviceable items; and
b. GSO-designate, in the event that the one-time cleansing cannot
yet be carried out, proceed with the disposal of those
unserviceable items that have already been identified in
accordance with existing rules and regulations.
Management comment/s
9.9 The Municipal Accountant and the GSO-designate agreed to implement
the recommendation. The GSO-designate stated that a disposal
committee will be established to address unserviceable properties and
that efforts will be made to reconcile these items with the accounting
records as part of the one-time property cleansing.

10.8 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to review
and conduct reconciliation of the noted negative or abnormal
balance in the Due to LGUs and make the necessary adjusting
entries to reclassify the improperly booked accounts from Due to
LGUs to its proper account.

3.11. We recommended that the Municipal Engineering Office expedite the water
system and farm-to-market road project implementation. Develop a concrete
course of action outlining specific steps and timelines for project completion.
Henceforth, future project proposals should incorporate comprehensive pre-
engineering activities to ensure its immediate implementation.
Management Comment(s)
3.12. The Municipal Engineer replied and recognized the importance of timely project
implementation, hence he assured the audit team to expedite all remaining
processes to ensure efficient progress.

4.9. In view of the foregoing, we recommended that:
4.9.1. The MPDC review all PPA proposals for a given year to ensure alignment
with the duly approved LDIP and that any modifications are supported
by a supplemental LDIP.
4.9.2. Sectoral workshops be supplemented with other appropriate activities,
such as benchmarking and public consultations, to develop a more
comprehensive LDIP.
4.9.3. Strict compliance with DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No.
1, dated November 4, 2020, be observed moving forward.
Management Comment(s)
4.10. The MPDC explained that due to the urgency of the need, particularly for the repair
of heavy equipment, the LGU inadvertently overlooked the process of supplementing
the LDIP for the said PPA, as well as other PPAs. However, he acknowledged the
recommendation with appreciation, recognizing that it would help enhance their
planning process to be more responsive to the needs of their constituents.

5.7. We recommended that:
5.7.1. The Head of the Agency enforce strict compliance with Section 77 of RA
7160 by ensuring that all recruitment and hiring of JO and COS
personnel undergo proper screening and approval by the HRMO;
5.7.2. The Municipal Accountant not to process any wages claim for JO and
COS personnel if these individuals have not submitted personnel
records to HRMO. These records be included, but not be limited to, a
duly accomplished Personal Data Sheet, a copy of a valid government-
issued ID, and a notarized Job Order Contract; and
5.7.3. Henceforth, the HRMO be solely responsible for preparing JO and
COS payroll wages, not any other offices, ensuring that wage
disbursements are based on officially recorded duty hours and actual
accomplishments.
Management Comment(s)
5.8. In their response, the Management, through the Municipal Accountant, agreed to
implement the stated recommendations. She further stated that, moving forward, the
responsibility for payroll preparation would be turned over to the HRMO to ensure
the accuracy of wage disbursements. Additionally, she committed to fulfilling all
documentary requirements necessary for the processing of Job Order and Contract
of Service wage claims.

6.9. We recommended that the MDRRM Officer, in coordination with all concerned
personnel, refrain from charging administrative expenditures to the MDRRMF.
Moreover, allocate a separate budget for the administrative expenses of the
MDRRM Office under the General Fund to ensure that the Calamity Fund
remains dedicated to its primary purpose of addressing disaster-related needs
within the community.
Management Comment(s)
6.10. In their response, the MDRRM Officer assured the Audit Team that administrative
expenditures would be allocated with a separate budget under the General Fund to
ensure that the LDRRM Fund was utilized strictly for its intended purposes.
Meanwhile, the Municipal Budget Officer acknowledged the noted deficiency and
emphasized that all transactions were undertaken in good faith and in line with the
objective of ensuring timely disaster response and recovery. She further assured the
Audit Team of her Office’s commitment to full compliance with applicable laws, rules,
and regulations.

7.9. We recommended that:
7.9.1. The Sangguniang Bayan incorporate the enactment of an appropriation
ordinance as an essential step in the budgetary procedure for the
utilization of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in the Special Trust
Fund.
7.9.2. Henceforth, strict compliance with the requirements of Section 305(a) of
Republic Act No. 7160 should be enforced to ensure that all local funds
are utilized in accordance with the law.
Management Comment(s)
7.10. In their response, the MDRRMO assured compliance with the requirements of the
law. Also, the Muninipal Budget Officer acknowledged the noted deficiency. She will
take necessary measures to ensure that all future re-allocation of unexpended
LDRMM Special Trust Fund shall be authorized through an enacted ordinance.

8.5. We recommended that the Disbursing Officer – Designate apply for an
increase in the fidelity bond amount to align with her actual cash
accountability. Moreover, conduct review of all other AO’s fidelity bonds to
ensure compliance in the above – cited regulations.
Management Comment(s)
8.6. The Municipal Treasurer replied and agreed to comply with the Audit Team’s
recommendations. An additional amount of bond will be applied immediately in
compliance with the provision of PD No. 1445 Section 101 (2) and Section 5.1 of
Treasury Circular No. 02-2019.

9.6. We strongly recommended that the Municipal Treasurer take immediate steps
to implement a secure payroll mechanism that eliminates or substantially
reduces cash advances by adopting direct bank deposits for all employees.
This can be achieved through the mandatory enrollment of personnel in
payroll accounts, ensuring that salaries and benefits are disbursed in a more
secure, efficient, and transparent manner.
Management Comment(s)
9.7. In their reply, the Municipal Treasurer explained that some personnel were resistant
or uncooperative in enrolling with the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP), which is
why salary payments were still processed through a cash advance by the SDO. She
admitted that the upcoming election and the resulting change in administration might
cause further delay in the implementation. However, she assured the audit team that
she would bring this matter to the attention of the new administration.

10.6. We recommended the following:
10.6.1. The Municipal Treasurer submit the RCD together with the duplicate
copies of official receipts and validated deposit slips to the Municipal
Accountant within five (5) days after the end of the month;
10.6.2. The Disbursing Officer submit the Report of Disbursement together
with the paid payrolls within five (5) days after the end of the pay
period to MACCO; and
10.6.3. The Municipal Accountant submit all outstanding financial documents
immediately and strictly comply with the prescribed reglementary
period for submission as required in the above – cited regulations.
Furthermore, considering the recent reorganization, capacity-building
initiatives should be conducted for accounting personnel to enhance
their efficiency in processing and submitting financial documents
promptly.
Management Comment(s)
10.7. The Municipal Accountant replied that their Office including the Treasury, is working
on the submission of the lacking financial documents and they will request additional
personnel. She also assured the Audit Team that this deficiency be addressed this
year.

11.6. We recommended that the concerned department heads submit the required
data for the updates of database and the GAD focal point system continue the
processing and analysis of relevant information in the GAD database to have
an effective basis and for proper identification of existing gender issues to
ensure that the approved PPAs incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget are
responsive to actual issues for a gender-responsive planning and policy
formulation.
Management Comment(s)
11.7. The GAD Focal Person replied and agreed to implement the recommendation. They
assured the audit team that they will update their GAD database, and collect sex-
disaggregated data. Moreover, the Municipal Mayor had issued a Memorandum to
all offices directing the provision of information required in the GAD database
template.

1.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make concerted
effort to identify the components of the lumped balances for the
account Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs, prepare reconciliation and if
warranted adjust the accounts accordingly and present these details
in the Notes to Financial Statement. Further, maintain a subsidiary
ledger to facilitate tracing of the balances for future references.

2.12 We recommended the following and agreed to:
a. Municipal Engineer provide copies of Certificate of Completion of
the above-mentioned completed infrastructure projects to the
Accounting Office and ensure that similar certificates are prepared
and submitted for all future completed projects, regardless of the
mode of implementation.
b. Municipal Accountant reclassify the completed projects totaling
₱6,316,134.46 to an appropriate PPE item once the certificate of
completion is received, and record the related accumulated
depreciation accurately.

3.12 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prioritize
the retrieval of the necessary data for the accurate application of
depreciation on specific PPE assets that have not yet been
depreciated. Additionally, ensure strict adherence to the disclosure
requirements outlined in IPSAS 17 to guarantee that the financial
statements are reliable, accurate, and compliant with public sector
reporting standards.

4.9 We recommended and the BAC Chairperson agreed to discontinue the
use of inappropriate procurement methods and ensure strict adherence
to the conditions outlined for each alternative mode of procurement
when used, as specified in Annex H of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
Also, reinforce proper procurement practices to prevent recurrence of
the practice in the future.

5.10 We recommended and the MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant
agreed to ensure that all future transactions charged to LDRRMF are
incorporated in the LDRRMFIP and accompanied by the approval
from the Sangguniang Bayan prior to disbursement to maintain
compliance with legal and regulatory requirements.

6.5 We recommended and the MDRRMO agreed to take proactive
measures to ensure the full utilization of cash donations for their
intended purposes and to implement the PPAs to be sourced out
therefrom, within the year.

7.10 We recommended and the BAC Chairman agreed to ensure
appropriate coordination with the BAC Secretary and School District
in Charge of the procurement process, from APP preparation to
implementation. Also, strict adherence to Annex H of RA 9184 to
avoid splitting procurements and secure the most favorable prices
through competitive bidding.

8.7 We recommended and the following agreed to:
a. Municipal Treasurer to discontinue the practice of issuing cash
advances and any reimbursements for the purchase of medicines.
b. Municipal Health Officer and the Pharmacy Assistant to
collaborate with the Municipal Accountant to maintain a unified
inventory management system to streamline the procurement
process.
c. BAC Chairman to ensure the timely processing of purchase
requests for upcoming procurements, adhering to the appropriate
modality of procurement that would facilitate procurement
procedures without delays.

9.6 We recommended and the Municipal Health Officer and Municipal
Accountant agreed to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular
No. 2012-001, specifically the necessary documentary requirements
for procurement of drugs and medicines.

10.7 We recommended and the management agreed to establish GAD
Focal Point System (GFPS) within the LGU, with designated
personnel focused on GAD-related tasks. Additionally, ensure that
the GAD AR is fully supported by the required documents outlined in
the JMC which includes policy issuances, and any other relevant
reports to substantiate the conduct of activities and programs.

11.8 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prioritize
the reconciliation of records and promptly remit the correct amounts
per the reconciliation to GSIS, HDMF, and PhilHealth to prevent any
fines or additional costs that the Municipality and its employees
might incur due to late remittances.

We recommended, and the Management agreed, to fast-track the
request to the Source Agency for the use of the unutilized funds; if not,
return the excess funds to the Source Agency as prescribed in Item 3.8
of DBM LBC No. 140.

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed, that accounts
payable be recorded only when the goods and services covered by the
ORS have been delivered and rendered and supported with valid
documentation. Strictly adhere to Section 46 of PD No. 1177 and
Section 141, Chapter 3, NGAS for LGUs, Volume III for future
transactions in the recording of Accounts Payable.

We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure that all
Delivery Receipts and Inspection and Acceptance Reports are properly
dated upon receipt and acceptance of goods and services. Also, it
requires that all deliveries must be accompanied by properly
documented and verified Delivery Receipts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the
guidelines and procedures for the proper disposition and derecognition
of dormant accounts, as outlined in COA Circular No. 2023-008, dated
August 17, 2023.

We recommended, and the LDRRMO agreed, to strictly adhere to Items
5.1.2 and 5.1.15 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and incorporate the PPAs
to be funded by the unspent LDRRMF from previous years in the
LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the
requirements of Section 305(a) of Republic Act 7160. Moving forward,
the Local Sanggunian enact an appropriation ordinance into its
budgetary procedure for the utilization of the unexpended balance of
LDRRMF in the Special Trust Fund for future transactions.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the
necessary lacking documents for further review and verification.
Further agreed and ensure that future transactions are supported with
complete required documents in accordance with COA Circular 2012-
001 dated June 2014, 2012.

We recommended and Management agreed take necessary procedures
to conduct a complete physical count of PPE in accordance with the
guidelines and procedures prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006,
dated January 31, 2020.

6.12.1. engage the services of qualified geotechnical and structural

structure;

of implementing the recommended improvements against the
potential benefits of preserving and utilizing the property. If
the analysis determines that preservation is feasible, proceed
with the necessary improvements to make the project
operational. Additionally, submit the analysis results to the COA
Office for review; and
