observations.managementAction: and the City Engineer agreed to continue to fast-track the implementation of the PPAs and ensure their utilization before December 31, 2024 and before endorsing the project proposals of the barangay to the Regional NTF-ELCAC, ensure that the projects are inspected and assessed for readiness for implementation to avoid hampering the implementation once the funds are received.

observations.recommendation: We recommended, and the Treasurer agreed on to facilitate the preparation, signing, and transmittal of the reports to the City Information Office (CIO) and for the latter to post the same to the website and full disclosure portal to adhere to the posting and reporting requirements as required by DILG's full disclosure policy to provide information to the public and oversight bodies on the utilization of funds and status of their implementation of PPAs.
observations.managementAction: and the Treasurer agreed on to facilitate the preparation, signing, and transmittal of the reports to the City Information Office (CIO) and for the latter to post the same to the website and full disclosure portal to adhere to the posting and reporting requirements as required by DILG's full disclosure policy to provide information to the public and oversight bodies on the utilization of funds and status of their implementation of PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the City Budget Officer agreed to refrain from charging these expenses against the SEF and strictly adhere to the allowable expenditures prescribed under the existing guidelines, specifically Item 4.0 of the DEPED - DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 and Item 1.0 of the DEPED- DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2020.
observations.managementAction: and the City Budget Officer agreed to refrain from charging these expenses against the SEF and strictly adhere to the allowable expenditures prescribed under the existing guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended, and Management agreed to include in its planning procedures the preparation of the Project Brief for each PPAs, as provided under the local guide by the DILG. This ensures that development projects funded out of the 20% DF are implementation ready under Item 3.2.3 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 04, 2020.
observations.managementAction: and Management agreed to include in its planning procedures the preparation of the Project Brief for each PPAs, as provided under the local guide by the DILG.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management to: a. Direct the Inventory Committee to follow the RPCPPE preparation as the ICF, which shall be the basis for reconciling the property and accounting records. Fast-track the implementation of One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of Government to establish PPE items’ existence, condition, and accountability and for the accuracy of PPE account balances reflected in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: and Management to: a. Direct the Inventory Committee to follow the RPCPPE preparation as the ICF, which shall be the basis for reconciling the property and accounting records. Fast-track the implementation of One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of Government to establish PPE items’ existence, condition, and accountability and for the accuracy of PPE account balances reflected in the financial statements.
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee to prepare the inventory report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management seeks the required authority from the sanggunian regarding any charges against lump-sum appropriations, adherence to Section 22 of Republic Act No. 7160 and COA Memorandum No. 2010-014 adopting the Supreme Court ruling per GR No. 175527, to support disbursements.
observations.managementAction: that Management seeks the required authority from the sanggunian regarding any charges against lump-sum appropriations and adhere to Section 22 of Republic Act No. 7160 and COA Memorandum No. 2010-014 adopting the Supreme Court ruling per GR No. 175527.
observations.reason: There are implemented disbursements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management to instruct the City Accountant to continue the withholding of refund from the payroll and other claims of the liable personnel until full collection. In addition, immediately report the settlement made for the issuance of Notice of Settlement of Suspension, Disallowances and Charges (NSSDC).
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to continue the withholding of refund from the payroll and other claims of the liable personnel until full collection and to immediately report the settlement made for the issuance of NSSDC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management to oblige the Punong Barangay and barangay officials to retrieve the necessary documents for the implemented programs and projects under the fund transfer and liquidate EDF transferred. In addition, instruct the CEO to fast track the preparation of POW, and the issuance of the Certificate of Completion for completed projects. Finally, consider the manpower requirement in the CEO.
observations.managementAction: and Management agreed to oblige the Punong Barangay and barangay officials to retrieve the necessary documents for the implemented programs and projects under the fund transfer and liquidate EDF transferred and instruct the CEO to fast track the preparation of POW, and the issuance of the Certificate of Completion for completed projects. Finally, consider the manpower requirement in the CEO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to: (i) Disseminate a Memorandum to all offices of the City Government of Surigao, urging them to submit monthly consumption reports for their inventories to the CAO, as this will facilitate the adjustment of the inventories on hand; and (ii) Allocate resources and personnel for the development of an Inventory Management System, as this will streamline processes, and make inventory recording more efficient and in hand; and (iii) The recording of already consumed Fuel, Oil and Lubricants and the recording of the consumed inventory.
observations.managementAction: The LCE disseminated Memorandum requiring the offices of the CGSO to submit monthly reports for their inventories to the CAO, as this will facilitate the adjustment of the inventories on hand; and (ii) Allocate the Physical resources and personnel for the development of the Inventory System which would include also the developme nt of the Monitoring property which would record the inventories to be monitored. -already booked-up and the Acting City Accountant agreed to make adjustments to the inventory account.
observations.reason: The LCE disseminated memorandum requiring the offices of the CGSO to submit monthly reports for their inventories to the CAO, as this will facilitate the adjustment of the inventories on hand; and (ii) Allocate the Physical resources and personnel for the development of the Inventory System which would include also the developme nt of the Monitoring property which would record the inventories to be monitored. -already booked-up and the Acting City Accountant agreed to make adjustments to the inventory account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Acting City Accountant agreed to: (i) Address the system issues of the ePPSAS; and (ii) Re-examine the data provided in the Schedule of the PPE and effect the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: The Acting City Accountant and IT Specialist complied with the improvement s that would be needed for the ePPSAS system, which is undergoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to effect the necessary reclassification entries for the completed projects from CIP accounts to the appropriate PPE accounts and compute the depreciation from the year of project completion.

1. We recommended and the Acting City Accountant agreed to: 2. We recommended that the City ensure that all items recorded in the books have actually existed at the time of recording, and in the meantime, record the reversal entry. 3. We recommended that the City capitalize the notarial fees on land acquisition and record the adjusting entries and compute the depreciation from the year of project completion. 4. Ensure that all completed infrastructure be recorded in the proper fund.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant and the Acting City Accountant agreed to effect the adjusting entries and record the reversal entry. Also ensure that all delivered equipment and purchased under trust fund be recorded accordingly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to: (i) Send written notices to the concerned payees before the checks become stale; (ii) Issue a new check upon submission by the payees of the spoiled or stale checks. Also, the Acting City Accountant agreed to: (iii) Periodically coordinate with the CTO regarding the existence of stale checks; and (iv) Take up the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts for checks which have become stale for a longer period, and no manifestation for replacement from the concerned payees was received.
observations.managementAction: Also, the Acting City Accountant agreed to: (iii) Periodically coordinate with the CTO regarding the existence of stale checks; and (iv) Take up the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts for checks which have become stale for a longer period, and no manifestation for replacement from the concerned payees was received.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City adopt the guidelines and procedures of the One-Time Cleansing of PPE accounts as prescribed in COA Circular 2020-006, and immediately implement the same to come up with PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.
observations.managementAction: The City Government is in the process of their one-time cleansing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Acting City Accountant agreed to: (i) Analyze and reconcile the individual SL accounts under the Due to Pag-IBIG accounts, especially those with debit balances, and effect the necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the correct amount of payables to the Pag- IBIG; and (ii) Work back and/or reconcile the remaining unreconciled amount and make the necessary adjusting entries, or remit the same to the Pag-IBIG Fund, whichever is deemed fit as a result of reconciliation. For PhilHealth: (i) Communicate with the PhilHealth Surigao City Branch that the premiums in June and July 2016, and August 2018 were already remitted in prior years and submit complete documentation thereof; and (ii) Effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts for the amount of the unremitted premiums without valid claims.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant and Acting City Accountant agreed to analyze and reconcile the individual SL accounts and to coordinate with PhilHealth to validate payments; and will effect necessary adjustments or remit amounts as appropriate.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to: (i) find effective methods and avenues to exhaust all the pending issues that have or could likely impede the project implementation; (ii) On the inclusion of completed projects, to effect the proper liquidation of all completed projects to the concerned agencies and thereafter remit the balances of the funds after the completion of the project or seek authority from the issuing agency for the utilization of the funds for other purposes, and if the funds have ceased to exist, decide or consider the following courses of action: designate a focal person to keep a complete record of the funds’ project and closely monitor their status to ensure that these are implemented accordingly; request for an authority from the Source Agency to use the balances; and (iii) On the unutilized funds, ensure proper monitoring and coordination with source agencies.
observations.managementAction: 1. On under/non-utilization/underutilized amount to exhaust all the possible avenues to address pending issues or settle all the balances that have or could likely impede the project implementation. 2. On completed projects, to effect the proper liquidation of all completed projects, and remit the balances from the concerned agencies. 3. On funds not returned after project completion, ensure return to the Source agency. 4. For negative balances, effect necessary adjustments. Additionally, the City will coordinate with source agencies and will ensure documentation and reconciliation.
observations.reason: Partial actions taken; some balances unremitted and pending reconciliation; assurance that balances after completion will be returned to source agencies; some funds already returned to DILG; reconciliation ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: (i) Fast-track the implementation of priority projects funded from the current appropriation and continuing appropriations by immediately commencing the unimplemented projects and employing calibrated measures for the delayed projects; and (ii) Carefully plan and evaluate development projects to be included in the AIP to ensure these are well-planned and implementation-ready; (iii) Re-evaluate the viability of the projects that were not implemented during the year for appropriate realignment; (iv) Provide adequate technical support to the CEO by engaging the services of the technical experts as needed.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive agreed to carefully plan and ensure projects are implementation-ready; provide technical support to CEO; and to re-evaluate unimplemented projects for realignment. The City provided engaging of technical services; some projects completed in 2025.
observations.reason: Catch-up plans being prepared for projects with more than or nearly negative 5% slippage; some projects later completed in early 2025.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the proponent officer for the program submit to COA a revised PAP of the above programs with complete details and information for further review of the Audit Team. Also ensure As-Built plans and warranty securities are included as integral documents in final payments and to provide basis that projects are protected by warranty.
observations.managementAction: The proponent agreed to submit the required As-Built Plans and Warranty Securities and include them as integral documents in final payment for completed infrastructure contracts; also to submit revised PAPs with complete details.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to: (i) Stop the practice of having the contracts notarized by lawyer employees for a fee; and (ii) Request the SP for an ordinance mandating the notarial services to be performed by the City Legal Officer or acquire notarial services from private lawyers through the regular procurement process in the absence of a categorical inclusion of notarization as one of the duties and functions of the City Legal Officer, and that services should have been availed for free from the City Legal Officer or at a lower price from a private lawyer acquired through the regular procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive agreed to: (i) stop the practice and require notarization by City Legal Officer and (ii) request SP to issue ordinance or follow procurement process for notarial services. The City Legal Officer to perform notarization of contracts and other documents of the City Government, or if the same is not practical at the moment, acquire notarial services from private lawyers through the regular procurement process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to: (i) Actively involve the implementing offices in the formulation of the comprehensive LDRRM Plan rather than assigning predetermined programs to enhance the quality of the LDRRM Plan, promote coordination, and strengthen the city's overall disaster management strategy; and (ii) Comply with the provisions on regulations for the utilization of LDRRMF and utilize the fund for implementation of DRRM programs only.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive and implementing offices agreed to: (i) involve implementing units in formulating LDRRM Plan; (ii) ensure compliance with regulations for utilization of LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: (i) Refrain from processing payments and issuing checks for undelivered items; and (ii) the LDRRMF Officer properly plan their purchase requisitions ensuring alignment with program implementation timelines.
observations.managementAction: The Acting City Accountant and City Treasurer agreed to refrain from issuing checks for undelivered items and ensure procurement processes align with program timelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: (i) Conduct a comprehensive review of its SEF budget allocation to align it with established regulations and prioritized programs as stipulated in Section 272 of RA 7160 and related guidelines; and (ii) Foster transparent communication and collaboration among entities involved in budgeting to ensure that financial resources are utilized in a manner consistent with educational priorities and regulatory requirements.
observations.managementAction: The Chairman of the Local School Board agreed to conduct a comprehensive review and to align SEF expenditures with regulations and priorities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: (i) Call for a deliberation with all the implementing units before consolidating the PPMPs into APPs to ensure procurement bidding requirements and timelines are aligned; (ii) Require the program coordinators to properly plan implementation timelines and budgetary requirements of their program activities, and completely include in their APPs the needed supplies and materials.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Chairman agreed to call for deliberation with all implementing units and ensure consolidation of PPMPs into APPs are properly done and require program coordinators to plan timelines and budgetary requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: (i) Require the end-users to formulate separate technical requirements and delivery terms for highly specialized or customized goods to be procured; and (ii) Ensure these are well-communicated to the prospective suppliers during the procurement process. Also recommend: (i) Delegate to the OIC-CGSO the responsibility of monitoring the deliveries of procured items to ensure that adequate and timely reminders are made to the suppliers; and (ii) Strictly impose liquidated damages and/or applicable sanctions to suppliers who incurred delays without valid cause.
observations.managementAction: The BAC agreed to require end-users to set technical and delivery terms and the OIC-CGSO to monitor deliveries and impose liquidated damages where applicable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (i) the proponent officer for the program submit to COA a revised PAP of the above programs with complete details and information for further review of the Audit Team; (ii) ensure that programs identified are not duplicates of the other programs and that the related PAPs are complete and well-designed; and (iii) ensure sufficient documentation for disbursements.
observations.managementAction: The proponent agreed to submit revised PAPs and to ensure documentation and improve program design.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: (i) Identify the causes of the deficiency and coordinate with the GSIS to reconcile and update agency/employee records; and (ii) ensure strict observation of the full and proper deduction and remittance of the GSIS premium contributions in compliance with Section 6 of RA No. 8291.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO and Accounting Officer agreed to coordinate with GSIS and reconcile records and to ensure future remittances are performed timely.
observations.reason: Reconciliation ongoing; adjustments to be made after identifying individuals and reconciling remittances; many items relate to Job Order personnel and prior system transitions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the GAD Focal Person agreed to: (a) address the gap in the Executive Order by issuing a revised order that clearly specifies the members of the Technical Working Group (TWG); (b) allocate GAD Funds for training on gender analysis, HGDG and updating/maintenance of GAD database; (c) conduct thorough gender analysis based on relevant data and update the existing database; and (d) submit GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) to COA within the prescribed deadline.
observations.managementAction: The CPDO, GAD Focal Person, and CSWDO recognized gaps and committed to revise EO, update database, conduct gender analysis, allocate funds for training, and ensure timely submission of GPB.
observations.reason: GAD database updates inconsistent; many departments yet to submit HGDG tools; plan to strengthen GAD implementation; commitments made but work ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the necessary accounting entry to correct the erroneous recording of the downloaded funds, as follows:
observations.managementAction: Already booked up in the accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to immediately effect the necessary adjusting entry in the books of the Municipality as follows: Account Title Debit Credit Advances to Special Disbursing Officer 5,870,385.10 Advances to Officers and Employees 5,870,385.10
observations.managementAction: Already booked up in the accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to: (i) Ensure that appropriate and timely recording for the unrecorded liquidation of the Advances to Confidential Funds was duly accounted; and (ii) Prepare the journal entry for the unrecorded liquidation, to wit: Account Title Debit Credit Prior Years’ Adjustments 825,000.00 Advances to Officers and Employees – Confidential Funds 825,000.00
observations.managementAction: Already booked up in the accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to immediately apply the accounting guidelines and effect the necessary adjusting entries in the books, as follows: Account Title Debit Credit Advances to Contractors 1,070,656.42 CIP-Infrastructure Assets 597,056.75 Guaranty Deposits Payable 473,599.67
observations.managementAction: Already booked up in the accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed to: (i) Immediately provide the Accounting Office with a Certified List of Taxpayers with amounts due and collectible for the current year, and a Certified List of RPT Delinquencies as of December 31, 2023 as basis to set up RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year, and to intensify the collection of delinquent property taxes; and (ii) The Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with Sections 19(b), 20, 36 and 93 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, and reconcile the balance of RPT/SET Receivables with the List of RPT Delinquencies (excluding the penalties) every end of the year. We also recommended the following entries be effected in the books of accounts by ₱5,063,712.96 (excluding penalties): Account Title Debit Credit RPT Receivable 2,531,856.48 SET Receivable 2,531,856.48 Deferred RPT Income 2,531,856.48 Deferred SET Income 2,531,856.48
observations.managementAction: Already booked up in the accounting records. The Municipal Accountant has assured that he will effect the necessary accounting entries to the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to re-examine the data provided in the Schedule of the PPE and effect the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct carrying amount of PPE as follows: [list of accumulated depreciation affected accounts and amounts provided in the AAR].
observations.managementAction: Re-examine the data and effect the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct carrying amount of PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to immediately apply the guidelines and procedures outlined in COA Circular 2022-004, and prepare the necessary adjusting entries in the books, and for the Municipal Accountant to provide a copy of the adjusting Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to the Municipal Treasurer, facilitating the updating of the latter’s records pertaining to PPE.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant agreed to apply the guidelines and records. He also agreed to provide a copy of the adjusting JEVs to the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Engineering Office agreed to submit the Certificate of Completion of the completed infrastructure projects to the Municipal Accounting Office so the necessary reclassification entries and computation of the corresponding depreciation will be effected in the books of accounts by the latter. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the turnover documents to be provided to the Barangays for projects that are currently under their possession and being maintained by the Barangays so that these will be dropped from the books of accounts of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer will submit Certificate of Completion for the completed infrastructure projects. The Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the turn-over documents to the Barangays for the completed projects. JEVs already forwarded to the audit team for their reference.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to analyze the cause of the negative balances in the subsidiary ledgers of Due to NGAs account and make the necessary adjusting journal entries, such as below: Account Title Debit Credit Due to NGAs 5,000,000.00 Prior Year’s Adjustments 5,000,000.00 Also, to trace the details of the returned funds amounting to ₱217,000 and determine the program/projects it is attributable. Additionally, reclassify the erroneously recorded fund receipts with the following adjusting entries: Account Title Debit Credit Due to NGAs 311,263.61 Trust Liabilities – DRRMF 311,263.61 We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to immediately prepare and submit the liquidation reports of the above fund transfers to the Source Agency copy furnished the Auditor and effect the reclassification of completed projects into their proper asset accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already traced this and prepared some entries. He agreed to prepare and submit liquidation reports and effect reclassification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to communicate with the Philhealth Surigao City Branch that the premiums in July 2016, February 2017, October 2020 and January to June 2022 were already remitted in prior years and submit complete documentation thereof, and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts for the amount of ₱81,104.21. Henceforth, ensure that a monthly SPA is secured from Philhealth to regularly monitor the posting of remittance in PHIC’s system.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant assured to reconcile the balance in the Due to Philhealth account and establish an amount that has valid claims only and to regularly monitor the posting of remittance in PHIC’s system.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to require the accountable officers concerned to immediately liquidate the outstanding cash advances, and after due notice/demand, if these cash advances remain unliquidated, deduct the outstanding cash advance from whatever amounts due from the concerned officers and grant, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 particularly in the prompt liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Written notice/demand was not sent to concerned accountables officers; hence, they are unable to submit the necessary documents for the liquidation of their corresponding cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) prioritize the urgent repair of heavy equipment crucial for daily operations, specifically for waste covering, and address the shortage of manpower through a thorough review and possible revision of the 11-day work schedule, to ensure that adequate personnel are available to perform essential duties as it is crucial to prevent any health hazards within the community; and the MENRO to expedite the planning and development process for the proposed sanitary landfill. Likewise, the MENRO agreed to not only repair but also ensure the functionality and utilization of the residual containment areas.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented due to a combination of urgent/competing priorities, busy schedule and limited manpower; MENRO agreed to expedite planning and repair actions but implementation pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: (i) Provide the necessary support and resources to the GAD Focal Person, and for the GAD Focal Person to: (ii) Prioritize the completion of the GAD Database; and (iii) Ensure timely submission of the GAD Accomplishment Reports by adhering to the specified deadline.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive agreed together with the Local School Board to provide support. The GAD Focal Person was asked to prioritize completion of the GAD Database.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer agreed to refrain from processing all payments of the said transactions without complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer agreed to refrain from processing payments without complete documentation but some attachments still not submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: (i) Implement developmental projects in phases to effectively utilize the limited available funds; (ii) Consider re-aligning the unimplemented projects to more feasible endeavors, if possible; and (iii) Conduct a thorough evaluation of all proposed development projects to proactively identify potential engineering challenges.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented. The Local Chief Executive agreed to review and re-align as necessary; some project realignment undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Officer agreed to: (i) Conduct a comprehensive review of expenditure policies and enforce rigorous controls to ensure future appropriations to the LDRRMF are utilized judiciously and in strict adherence to established guidelines; (ii) Institute a monitoring system for unimplemented PPAs and update the LDRRMFIP to include activities to be funded from unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE from previous years transferred to the trust fund; and (iii) The Municipal Accountant to initiate transfer and maintain subsidiary ledgers for Trust Liabilities - LDRRMF by year of transfer.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Officer agreed to conduct review and enforce controls. The Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare transfer JEV for unutilized CY LDRRMF and to include Trust Fund balances in reports; subsidiary ledger maintenance to be instituted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: (i) Develop a budget for SEF that equitably allocates appropriations to priority programs and projects consistent with educational priorities and regulatory requirements; and (ii) Ensure that SEF is utilized in a manner consistent with educational priorities.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented. The Local Chief Executive and Local School Board will develop the budget and ensure compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to develop a specific guideline for granting assistance, taking into account the realities of the local community, ensuring fairness, transparency and accountability. The Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed to ask for Official Receipts for every financial assistance extended to barangays.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive agreed to develop guidelines. The Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed to require Official Receipts for assistance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to refrain from extending financial assistance to various individuals on account of the official activities of the different barangays. Instead, solicitations will be coursed through the concerned barangays and supported with barangay resolutions; Acting Municipal Treasurer to ask for OR from the payee barangays for every financial assistance extended to them by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented. The Local Chief Executive and Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed to require barangay resolutions and Official Receipts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the BAC Vice Chairman agreed to ensure that all the eligibility requirements are incorporated in the contract documents for certain types of goods, particularly concerning the procurement of drugs and medicines, and establish extensive verification of documentation.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented. The BAC agreed to incorporate verification requirements in procurement documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: (i) Consider implementing a petty cash fund system for procurement of office supplies with minimal amounts; (ii) Conduct a comprehensive assessment of procurement needs and incorporate in the Annual Procurement Plan; and (iii) Assign offices to prepare PPMPs for recurring municipal activities.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented. The Local Chief Executive agreed to consider establishing petty cash and to conduct comprehensive procurement review; offices to submit PPMPs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to implement developmental projects in phases and provide adequate technical personnel to ensure sufficient supervision and monitoring; require warranty securities as integral attachments to the final billings.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented fully; Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant agreed to implement recommended measures; some actions pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to exert diligent effort to locate the Payrolls/Disbursement Vouchers/Payrolls for the outstanding payable account. If the accounts are found to be no longer payable or without valid claimants it should be reverted to the unappropriated surplus and funds can be used to finance various projects and programs.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented; efforts to locate supporting documents ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to: (i) Exert all efforts to substantiate the reconciling items and record the same in the books to reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements; and (ii) Conduct regular reconciliation of their records at least monthly and adjust the verified reconciling items to avoid its accumulation.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented. The Municipal Accountant agreed to forward Cash in Bank Account Subsidiary Ledger to the Acting Municipal Treasurer to facilitate monthly reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to: (i) Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office regarding the existence of stale checks and send written notices to the concerned payees before checks become stale; (ii) For checks which became stale and which require replacement, issue a new check upon submission of the spoiled or stale check to the MTO; and (iii) Take up the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts for those which have become stale for a longer period, and no manifestation for replacement from the concerned payees was received.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented; MTO will issue new check upon submission of spoiled/stale checks; Municipal Accountant to effect adjustments; actions pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw adjusting journal entries affecting the ongoing infrastructure projects to recognize the Construction in Progress-Infrastructure Asset account.
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Engineering Office employ necessary measures to expedite the implementation of stalled PPAs; b. the Municipal Development Council to properly screen the PPAs and prioritize those that are readily implementable and procurement-ready and in the same way, the suggested PPAs in relation to the capacity of LGU should be thoroughly planned to implement them to prevent the wastage of resources due to delay or non-implementation; and c. strictly comply with the provisions set forth under DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDRRM Officer in coordination with all concerned personnel refrain charging local festivities related expenditures to the MDRRMF; instead, explore alternative funding sources for the mobilization of the Emergency Operation Center during festivities to ensure that the Calamity Fund remains dedicated to its primary purpose of addressing disaster-related needs within the community.
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned officials revise the MDRRMF AIP through the enactment of an ordinance or resolution every time a new expenditure item is charged unto the Special Trust Fund of MDRRM to adhere to the appropriate procedure when utilizing the same fund.
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the respective School Districts promptly submit their PPMPs to the BAC Secretariat who shall consolidate them into an APP before any procurement will be made.
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to: adhere to Section 1 of Rule VI of Republic Act No. 9679 and ensure that LGU’s share of HDMF contributions is within the limits set by the aforementioned provision.

observations.managementAction: Priority PPAs for CY 2023 are consistent with the AIP and LDIP; justification provided for the non-priority project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. the Local Health Board and the Chief of Hospital submit the list of public health workers in relation to the grant of COVID-19 SRA; and b. the Human Resource Office submit an alternative work arrangement plan duly approved by the head of the agency or equivalent document to show proof that the personnel are authorized to report to the workstation during the state of national emergency, which places them in direct contact with COVID-19 patients.
observations.managementAction: Supporting documents were submitted to the COA Office. The Human Resource Office also made necessary adjustments to the books.

observations.managementAction: BAC posted required information; orientation/workshop on Emergency Procurement Reporting and Posting conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: a. designation of appropriate personnel to prepare the Business Plan for the existing LEEs to be presented to LCE for approval; b. that the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan enact an ordinance creating LEEs based on the Business Plan approved by the LCE; and c. henceforth, strict compliance to the provisions of Local Budget Circular No. 111.
observations.managementAction: Created a Development and Economic Enterprise Committee for LEE. Proposal for increase of fees and charges deliberated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. Municipal Treasurer coordinate with the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan to ensure that local tax rates are updated every five years pursuant to Section 191 of the Local Government Code; b. the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan enact the revision of the Local Revenue Code for immediate imposition; and c. henceforth, strict compliance with the Local Government Code.
observations.managementAction: Ordinance No. 18 series of 2023 dated December 11, 2023 was enacted for the Revised Revenue Code.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. the OIC-General Services Office and MEEDMO coordinate with the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan for the enactment of an ordinance authorizing the imposition of fees and charges for the added services of the Carrascal Adventure Park and Public Market Extension and revision of the local ordinance for the operation of Carrascal Waterworks System and Carrascal Public Market to include the changes to its fees and charges; and b. henceforth, strict compliance to the Local Government Code and other issuances relating to the imposition of fees and charges is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: Created MEEDMO and Municipal Treasurer lobbied/coordinated with the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan. Ordinance enacted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. the Market Supervisor and MEEDMO strictly comply with the provisions of Section 24 of the Local Ordinance No. 3, s.2004 and employ the "one stall per person" policy; b. the Market Supervisor and MEEDMO employ other revenue-boosting measures like the prompt posting of vacancies to conspicuous places without prejudice to other local ordinance provisions; and c. henceforth, strict compliance with any local ordinances.
observations.managementAction: Vacancies are already posted.
observations.reason: Space constraints for products.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. the Market Supervisor, MEEDMO, and the other Market Committees work hand in hand and enforce the delinquent payments' collection and impose the cancellation/revocation of contracts should the lessee become delinquent for two (2) months; b. also consider lessees with businesses other than resto-bars in the baywalk stalls since the latter's income is only seasonal; and c. henceforth, strict compliance with Ordinance no. 3, s. 2004 and other related local ordinances.
observations.managementAction: Vacancies are posted. Collection of delinquencies improved.
observations.reason: Delinquency collection can still be improved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. the MEEDMO, Market Supervisor and Market Committees reassess the currently implemented rates and formally submit a proposal to amend the ordinance; and b. henceforth, strict compliance to ordinances is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: All fees are adjusted in the revision of the market tax ordinance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. Municipal Accountant prepare and send demand letters to the recipient NGO/PO through the implementing office for the immediate submission of liquidation reports; b. the Implementing Offices have proper monitoring of the implementation of the transfers to NGO/PO; and c. henceforth, strict compliance of Section 5.4 of the COA Circular 2007-001 is hereby enjoined.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to recipient NGO/POs requiring immediate submission of liquidation reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Management to immediately create an Internal Audit Service to assist the management in ensuring that resources are used consistent with laws, regulations and policies; resources are safeguarded against loss or wastage and misuse; financial and non-financial information are accurate and timely; and operations are economical, efficient and effective.
observations.managementAction: Creation of Internal Audit Service Office and hiring of plantilla personnel to man the IAS Office were implemented but no personnel were hired.
observations.reason: Plantilla positions created but no personnel hired.

The Municipal General Services Officer undertake the disposal of these unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration.
