observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare adjusting entries to correct the misstatement of the Depreciation Expense-Road Networks, Accumulated Depreciation-Road Networks, and Government Equity and the error of recognizing the residual value for the Road Network account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasury Office adhere to the daily remittance of collections as prescribed in Section 29, Volume I of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs to safeguard the funds against risks of loss, theft, and improper use.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer agreed to adhere to the daily remittance of collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the confidential expenses incurred in the 4th Quarter of CY 2022 in the amount of ₱475,000.00 as expenses in the period to which these relate to observe the accrual basis of accounting as prescribed by IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant agreed to observe the accrual basis of accounting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: a. obtain the necessary bank statements and submit the BRS. b. ensure the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) on all depository accounts are prepared and submitted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare and submit the BRS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to require the contractors to provide adequate warranty securities for structural defects as required in Section 62.2.3.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184, effective one year from the end of the defects liability period, to ensure sufficient financial coverage to address potential structural defects during the warranty period.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant assured to require the structural warranty securities for final payments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the BAC Secretariat to designate the GSO to submit copies of the Contracts and POs with the required supporting documents to the Audit Team within five days after perfection.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat assured to submit the Contracts and documents for all procurement to the Office not later than five (5) working days from perfection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for transfers of unutilized Disaster Risk Funds to the special trust fund by year of transfer, in accordance with Item 5.1.12 of COA Circular No.2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared and maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for transfers of unutilized DRRM Funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDRRMO stop charging ineligible expenditures not related to disaster risk reduction and management to LDRRM Fund and strictly observe the guidelines set in COA Circular No. 2012 – 002 and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG in the utilization of LDRRMF to ensure optimal utilization.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO assured that the ineligible expenditures were used to support disaster risk management activities and assured to strictly observe the guidelines set in COA Circular No.2012-002 and Joint Memorandum Circular No.2013-01.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: a. follow-up from the agencies to secure returned excess funds; b. return the excess funds to ensure that the Municipality can still secure funding from the NGAs for future projects; and c. transfer to the general fund as other income those funds whose nature does not require liquidation or have no attached condition.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant communicated with the National Government Agencies to follow-up on the reprogramming of DENR excess funds so intended beneficiaries can benefit from the programs; return the excess funds to ensure that the municipality could secure funding from the NGAs for future projects; and transfer to the general fund as other income those funds whose nature does not require liquidation or have no attached condition.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer in amounts exceeding the maximum cash accountability to grant cash advances in accordance with Section 101 of PD 1445 and Treasury Circular No.02-2019; ensure the maximum cash accountability amounts are not exceeding the fidelity bond of the AO.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer agreed to grant cash advances in amounts not exceeding the maximum cash accountability and increase the fidelity bond of the AO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to verify and determine the acquisition dates of the Land Improvements items and reflect them in the Schedule of Property, Plant and Equipment. Compute the accumulated depreciation expenses of the Land Improvements-Aquaculture and Other Land Improvements items and prepare journal entries to reflect the adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to evaluate and properly classify PPE items into their proper classification in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS and COA Circular No.2015-009.
observations.managementAction: The Management directed the Municipal Accountant to properly classify the PPE items into their proper classification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to: a.) Prepare a Journal Entry Voucher adjusting the Accumulated Depreciation of various PPE items and present the PPE account to their correct carrying amounts; b.) Provide for the acquisition dates of the remaining 23 PPE items, compute for the Accumulated Depreciation and prepare adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to grant cash advances in amounts not exceeding the maximum cash accountability and to increase the fidelity bond of the AO to cater to the maximum cash accountability amounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer agreed to increase the fidelity bond of the AO to cater to the maximum cash accountability amounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to verify and determine the acquisition dates of the Land Improvements items and reflect them in the Schedule of Property, Plant and Equipment; compute the accumulated depreciation expenses of the Land Improvements-Aquaculture and Other Land Improvements items and prepare journal entries.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to: a.) Update themselves on the rules and regulations pertaining to the warranty security required in the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects; and b.) Require suppliers or contractors to provide warranty securities for future procurements to ensure that the supplier will be held liable for any manufacturing defects or non-compliance issues.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant agreed to direct the Municipal contractors to provide warranty securities for payments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to ensure the submission of the As-built plan and the Contractor's Affidavit on Payment of Laborers and Materials, and ensure the complete documentation of future final payments for infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, ensured the submission of the As-built plan of the completed projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. update themselves on the rules and regulations pertaining to the warranty security required in the procurement of goods; and b. ensure that for future procurements, retain an amount equivalent to at least 1% of the contract price or require a special bank guarantee to guarantee supplier liability for manufacturing defects.
observations.managementAction: The Management instructed the Municipal Accountant to update themselves on the rules and regulations pertaining to warranty security and ensure suppliers provide warranty securities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to maintain a special account in the General Fund for all loans availed by the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant agreed to maintain a special account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the Perpetual Inventory Method of accounting for construction materials, taking up first the construction materials procured under the Construction Materials Inventory upon purchase, in compliance with Section 114 of the NGAS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant adhered to the Perpetual Inventory Method of accounting by taking up first the construction materials procured under the Construction Materials Inventory upon purchase.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: a. ensure that for future transactions, only half or fifty percent of the negotiated price will be paid upon execution of the deed of sale and the remaining half will be paid upon transfer of title; b. ensure that all disbursement vouchers relating to land purchase are properly supported with necessary documents before processing the payments; and c. Municipal Assessor to immediately comply with the documentary requirements needed by the BIR to expedite the processing of transfer of title in favor of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Management instructed the Municipal Accountant to ensure compliance with the 50% rule for future transactions and ensure proper documentary requirements are in place for land purchase.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the completed project amounting to ₱12,458,129.35 into appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment accounts and recognize the corresponding depreciation expense as prescribed under the GAM for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Management required the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the CIP - Other Infrastructure to Water Supply System and recognize the depreciation expense as prescribed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Secretariat submit the Contracts/Purchase Orders together with the supporting documents forming part thereof to the Office not later than five (5) working days from perfection.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat instructed the Secretariat to submit the Contracts and documents for all procurement to the Office not later than five (5) working days from perfection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Heads of the Implementing Office to fast track the implementation of the programs and projects and the Bids and Awards Committee to properly plan procurement activities to avoid delayed implementation. Also, the Local Chief Executive to create the Project Monitoring Committee/Project Inspection Team to assist, evaluate and monitor implementation.
observations.managementAction: The Heads of the Implementing Office to fast track the implementation of the programs and projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the RPT Receivable and SET receivable List based on the duly Certified List of Taxpayers (CLT) and ensure proper setting-up of receivables.
observations.managementAction: The Management instructed the Municipal Accountant to adjust the RPT Receivable and SET receivable List.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting journal entry to correct the accounts and use the appropriate accounts in recording financial transactions to ensure the fair presentation of accounts in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly follow the pertinent provisions of the Local Treasury Operations Manual by doing the following:

a. require Liquidating Officers to turn over collections daily and intact and ensure that the Report of Collections and Deposits/Remittances to Local Treasury is duly certified and all the required information such as the date received, be provided therein; and

b. refrain from allowing the collections to be used for any urgent operating expenses of the Municipality, and might as well set up a petty cash fund for that purpose.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned personnel seek the required authority from the Sangguniang concerned before any charges could be made against lump-sum appropriations in adherence in Section 22.c of Republic Act No. 7160 and COA Memorandum No. 2010-014 adopting the Supreme Court ruling per GR No. 175527.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned officials revise the AIP through the enactment of an ordinance or resolution every time a expenditure item is charged unto the Special Trust Fund of MDRRM to adhere to the appropriate procedure when utilizing the same fund.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Secretariat ensure all the PPMPs of end-user units are included in the consolidated APP of the Municipality before any procurement is made.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all key officials refrain from granting benefits unless supported by a proper legal basis or conforms with existing applicable laws, rules and regulations and strictly observe the appropriate procedural guidelines to establish clear legal authorization for granting incentives.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the following:

Municipal Accountant:

a. Ensure the setting up of the beginning balance of RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year, supported with a certified list from the Municipal Treasurer showing the taxpayers’ names and amounts due; and

b. Make appropriate adjustments by recognizing the RPT/SET Receivables based on the list of delinquent taxpayers;

Municipal Treasurer:

a. Provide the Municipal Accountant with a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the name and the amount due and collectible for the current year as the basis for setting up the beginning balance of RPT and SET Receivables, respectively;

Municipal Assessor:

a. Coordinate with the Provincial Assessor with the identification and subsequent clean-up of the Municipal Government’s Tax Declarations and Real Property Assessments; and

b. The Municipal Assessor, Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal Accountant to collaborate efforts in generating accurate/reliable data on RPT and SET receivable balances by indicating the names of taxpayers and amounts due from them, including penalties therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct all concerned personnel to refrain from granting cash advances for personnel pay and instead pay all employed personnel (regular, COS, job order), e.g., through ATM and/or individual bank/payroll accounts.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of charging prior year expenses to the current appropriation and instruct the Municipal Accountant to accrue the December electricity expenses at year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of charging prior year expenses to the current appropriation and instruct the Municipal Accountant to accrue the December electricity expenses at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to adhere to the daily remittance for the collectors to follow the daily remittance in order to safeguard the funds against loss, theft, and improper use.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has issued a Memorandum prescribed in Section 29, in order to follow the daily collection remittance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Assessor to ensure that all Lands owned by the Municipality are duly registered under the name LGU Lanuza and secure copies of the certificates of title (OCT/TCT); and the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer, obtain a list of real properties owned by the Municipality, and take appropriate action to determine the appropriate cost to be recognized in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: As of now, the land in Habag and the has been titled.
observations.reason: Due to limited resources.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to create an Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of PPE and follow the guidelines and procedures prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006 to implement the one-time cleansing of PPE.
observations.managementAction: Recommended to create an Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of PPE and follow the guidelines and procedures prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006 to implement the one-time cleansing of PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to immediately return the unexpended/excess fund transfers, otherwise coordinate in writing with the SA to determine the correct disposition of the unexpended/excess fund transfers, otherwise return these to the SA in accordance with existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant to coordinate in writing with the SA to determine the correct disposition of the unexpended/excess fund transfers, otherwise return these to the SA in accordance with existing rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Engineering Office to increase the number of completed projects charged to the 20% Development Fund and for the Municipal Development Coordinator to convey in writing to the Municipal Development Council during budget preparation that priority development projects of the Municipality must be procurement-and-implementation-ready.
observations.managementAction: Recommended to increase the number of completed projects charged to the 20% Development Fund and for the Municipal Development Coordinator to convey in writing to the Municipal Development Council during budget preparation that priority development projects of the Municipality must be procurement-and-implementation-ready.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that only those participants and/or personnel with duties and responsibilities relatable to the LDRRM activity be allowed to participate in trainings, seminars, and workshops to ensure that funds are not spent on unnecessary charges.
observations.managementAction: The management has already followed your recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local School Board (LSB) to ensure approval of the SEF Budget by the appropriate approving authority and ensure adherence with the provisions of DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s.2017.
observations.managementAction: Local School Board has agreed that the SEF Budget must be signed by the appropriate signatories to ensure adherence with the provisions of DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require DTRs and accomplishment reports as supporting documents in the payment of security services and ensure completeness of supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has followed your recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant fast-track the reconciliation of the records with the GSIS, HDMF and PhilHealth and, if possible, allow your Office to render overtime to finish the reconciliation within a given time frame, then remit the correct amount per reconciliation to GSIS, HDMF, and PhilHealth to prevent fines or additional costs due to late remittances.
observations.managementAction: On-going reconciliation of the records with the GSIS. Municipal Accountant to fast-track the reconciliation of the records and, if possible, allow overtime to finish the reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System submit the GAD AR supported with a brief summary of the reported program or project, copies of reported policy issuances, and results of HGDG tests for evaluation, if any, and the documents that show proof of the conduct of activities to properly establish the success in addressing gender issues.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Point System has already submitted the GAD AR supported with a brief summary of reported program or project and required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System submit justification documents that substantiate and support the identified gender issues for these subject activities to ensure that the expenditures are eligible to be charged to GAD.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Point System has already submitted the letter to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee require the winning bidders to secure the certification from the Insurance Commission for the Performance Bond in the form of a Surety Bond posted for the awarded projects.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee committed to require the winning bidders to secure the certification from the Insurance Commission for the Performance Bond in the form of a Surety Bond posted for the awarded projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Health Board, in coordination with the Municipal Health Office, carefully and adequately plan and prepare a PFPR Fund Utilization and Program Plan to maximize the fund's utilization so that the intended beneficiaries avail optimum benefits.
observations.managementAction: The Local Health Board, in coordination with the Municipal Health Office, prepared the PFPR Fund Utilization and Program Plan to maximize the fund's utilization.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to create a team and conduct the One-Time Cleansing and follow the general guidelines and procedures provided in the Circular to establish the reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: Create a team and the Management agreed to conduct the One-Time Cleansing and follow the general guidelines and procedures provided in the Circular to establish the reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management optimize the utilization and implementation of the 20% Development Fund through proper planning, ensuring that programs and projects are procurement-and-implementation-ready before the start of the budget year.
observations.managementAction: Management to optimize the utilization and implementation of the 20% Development Fund through proper planning.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to ensure adherence to the IPSAS provisions about the recording of the construction materials procured to their appropriate Construction Materials inventory accounts.
observations.managementAction: As of this writing, the LGU has no warehouse or storage facility to accommodate the Construction Materials. Ongoing preparation of the Construction Materials warehouse/storage facility.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipality undertake the procedures in adopting COA Circular No. 2020-006 to come up with the reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality recommended to undertake the One-Time Cleansing under COA Circular No. 2020-006 to come up with reconciled and corrected PPE balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounting records with the Report of Physical Count of PPE and render an accounting on the result of the recently conducted physical inventory and reconcile the differences between the two records.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Treasurer submitted to the Municipal Office the Report of Physical Count of PPE, which is still subject to reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management send demand letters to the debtors and impose strict collection of the amount due. For Loans Receivable-General Fund, take the necessary procedures in the write-off of dormant accounts as provided in COA Circular 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: Management sent demand letters to the debtors and imposed collection activities. Some borrowers were already deceased.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management impose the collection of the liquidated damages from the supplier and follow-up through correspondence and legal steps as necessary.
observations.managementAction: The management already sent a follow-up letter to the supplier through post. As of this writing, the supplier has responded and stood firm in their opinion that they have not incurred liquidated damages.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to direct the Contractor, through the Municipal Engineer, to fast-track the implementation of the project and impose liquidated damages for the delay, require the Municipal Engineer to conduct regular inspections and monitoring, and inform the DILG-CARAGA of the project status as stipulated in the MOA.
observations.managementAction: The project was completed in December 2018, but as to the imposition of Liquidated Damages, the management has yet to compute.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the intra-agency receivables and intra-payables accounts and effect the adjustments to reflect the correct account balances ensuring the presentation of reliable data on the financial reports of the LGU as of a given period of time.
observations.managementAction: Management directed the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the intra-agency receivables and intra-payables; reconciliation was performed to ensure correct account balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the Accountable Forms, Plates and Stickers Inventory and Other Deferred Credits account in the same amount of ₱1,290,000.00.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant commented that the Accountable Forms, Plates and Stickers Inventory and Other Deferred Credits account in the same amount of ₱1,290,000.00 had already been adjusted per JEV no. 100-2024-02-394, dated February 29, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to adjust the Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund account to its accurate balance.
observations.managementAction: During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant commented that she would prepare a JEV to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately transfer the CY 2023 unutilized DRRM-QRF funds to the Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund Books and draw the necessary accounting entries to adjust the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant acknowledged the observation and adhered to the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to require the contractors to provide warranty securities as required in Section 62.2.3.3, effective one year from the end of the defects liability period.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant commented that it was their usual practice to ask contractors for warranty securities after one year from the end of the defects liability period. However, they also ensured that they would implement the Audit Team's recommendation to demand warranty security before the release of the retention or within the warranty period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant stop charging ineligible expenditures against the 20% DF to ensure optimal utilization of the funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant commented that they would adhere to the recommendations to stop charging ineligible expenses to the 20% development fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop charging ineligible expenditures to the LDRRM Fund and strictly observe the guidelines set in COA Circular No. 2012 – 002 and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG, and to instruct the Municipal Budget Office to allocate money from the general fund for the utilization of the LDRRM Fund to ensure optimal utilization.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO agreed to strictly observe the guidelines and to instruct the Municipal Budget Office to allocate money from the general fund for the utilization of the MDRRM fund to ensure optimal MOOE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer: a) Intensify collection of real property taxes by notifying all delinquent taxpayers through sending Notices of Delinquency as required by law; b) Assign MTO Personnel to conduct information and education campaigns on real property tax payment; and c) Enforce collection by implementing the remedies specified in the Local Government Code in coordination with the Provincial Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer commented that they have already requested additional revenue collection clerks from the Management to enforce RPT collections and IECs. They are hopeful that as the LGU’s NTA returns to normal by next year, new positions can be created to address the manpower shortage, allowing for more IECs per barangay or on a quarterly basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to: a) Locate/obtain the documents of the motor vehicles and facilitate the processing of registrations and insurance coverage of the motor vehicles and; b) Regularly monitor the expiration and renewal of all motor vehicles registration to avoid penalties or fines for late/non-registration.
observations.managementAction: Vehicles of the Municipality has been registered with LTO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management allocate budget to hire an external consultant to prepare the 5-year business plan as prescribed under Section 4.6 of the Local Budget Circular 111 dated June 10, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Business Plan for the Economic Enterprise has been prepared and approved already.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a) Direct the Municipal Health Officer to submit the approved DTRs, accomplishment reports, and office order/ work arrangement; and b) Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all claims against government funds, especially the SRA and AHDP, are supported with complete documentary requirements as provided under Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Complete documentation before claims be submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a) Direct the MPDO to incorporate specific PPAs in the AIP and prepare a supplemental AIP; b) Seek authorization from SB thru a Resolution for the COVID expenses charged against the lump-sum appropriation presented in generic terms; c) Direct the MBO to ensure proper authorization of PPAs with generic terms, before certifying the Obligation Request; and d) Require the MA to require SB Resolution before certifying the completeness and propriety of supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The LGU agreed to: direct the MPDO to incorporate specific PPAs in the AIP and prepare a supplemental AIP; seek authorization from SB thru a Resolution for the COVID expenses charged against the lump-sum appropriation presented in generic terms; direct the MBO to ensure proper authorization of PPAs; and require the MA to require SB Resolution before certifying supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide adequate disclosures on Loans Payable account so that the liquidity and solvency of the Municipality could be properly assessed.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide adequate disclosures on Loans Payable Account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer provide the documents required for the setting up of the RPT and SET Receivable pursuant to Section 20 of NGAS and instruct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate reconciliation of the balances reflecting the balances in the duly certified list of taxpayers.
observations.managementAction: The Management directed the Municipal Treasurer to provide the documents required for the setting up of the RPT and SET receivables and the Municipal Accountant to facilitate reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: a) Update themselves on the rules and regulations pertaining to the warranty security required in the procurement of infrastructure projects; and b) Require contractors to provide warranty security for future procurements to ensure that the contractor will be held liable for non-compliance issues that may arise from the infrastructure projects procured.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant agreed to direct the contractors to provide warranty security for future procurements to ensure that the contractor will be held liable for non-compliance issues that may arise from the infrastructure projects procured.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to: a. Recognize assets and corresponding liabilities to ensure that the Financial Statements reflect the true value thereof; and b. Institute measures to improve communication channels between said offices involving asset procurement to ensure that all relevant parties are promptly informed of it.

We recommended that the SFP Focal Person develop a course of action to complete the program implementation, mitigating unreasonable delays and considering all factors that could hinder immediate action in future agreements with government agencies.

We recommended that: a. Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) employ necessary measures to expedite the implementation of stalled PPAs; b. Municipal Development Council (MDC) properly screen the PPAs and prioritize those that are readily implementable and procurement-ready and thoroughly plan the suggested PPAs in relation to the capacity of LGU to implement them to prevent the wastage of resources due to delay or non-implementation; and

We recommended and the concerned implementing office agreed to seek the required authority from the Sangguniang concerned before any charges could be made against lump-sum appropriations, in adherence to Section 22.c of Republic Act No. 7160 and COA Memorandum adopting the Supreme Court ruling per GR No. 175527.

We recommended and the MDRRMO In-Charge agreed to: a. Submit the documents that are lacking, as mentioned above, to help substantiate the transactions; b. Sign the DTRs to personally show up in the Auditor’s Office to prove that they indeed worked on those days indicated in the DTR; c. Henceforth, ensure for overtime pay have been duly supported with complete documentation by using the list of documents in COA Circular No. 2023-004.

We recommended and the MDRRMO In-charge agreed to revise the AIP every time a new expenditure item is charged unto the Special Trust Fund of MDRRM to adhere to the appropriate procedure when utilizing the same fund.
We recommended and the BAC Secretariat agreed to require the respective School Districts to promptly submit their PPMP to them for integration in the APP before any procurement will be made.

We recommended that: a. Municipal Engineer take immediate action to settle the aforementioned projects deficiency and avoid incurring the same deficiency in future projects; b. Municipal Accountant monitor the actions taken by the former on the rectification of the deficiency and if warranted, effect settlement; and c. Bids and Awards Technical Working Group review the program of works or the basis of the Approved Budget of the Contract before proceeding to the procurement process.

We recommended that: a. Municipal Engineer take immediate action to settle the aforementioned deficiency and avoid incurring the same deficiency in future projects; b. Municipal Accountant monitor the actions taken by the former on the rectification of the deficiency and if warranted, effect settlement; and c. Bids and Awards Technical Working Group to review the program of works or the basis of the Approved Budget of the Contract before proceeding to the procurement process.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly adhere to Section 1 of Rule VI of the Home Development Fund Law of 2009 and ensure that LGU’s share of HDMF contributions are within the limits set by the aforementioned provision.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to strictly follow the pertinent provisions of the Local Treasury Operations Manual and do the following: a. Require Liquidating Officers to turn over collections daily and intact and compel that the Report of Collections and Deposits/Remittances be prepared on a daily basis or as a receipt of collection/remittances occurs; b. Regularly check the cashbooks of the Liquidating Officers and require her to record her collections and remittances daily; and c. Stop allowing the collections to be used for any urgent operating expenses of the Municipality, and might as well set up a petty cash fund for that purpose.

observations.managementAction: Efforts have been made by the Municipal Treasurer to ensure daily remittances

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adhere to COA Circular No. 2012-002, dated September 12, 2012, which was issued to prescribe the Accounting and Reporting Guidelines for the LDRRMF of the LGUs.
observations.managementAction: reclassified in the books

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Health Board, in coordination with the Municipal Health Office, carefully and adequately plan and prepare a PFPR Fund Utilization Program Plan to maximize the fund's utilization so that the intended beneficiaries avail themselves of optimum benefits.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person submit the GAD AR supported with a brief summary of the reported program or project, copies of reported policy issuances, results of HGDD tests, and actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and recommendations for evaluation, if any, and the documents that show proof of the conduct of activities to properly establish the success in addressing gender issues.

observations.managementAction: Mandatory positions set in the JMC have been posted and waiting for PSB.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere to the guidelines in granting funds to NGOs/POs provided under COA Circular 2007-001 to ensure that the recipient NGOs/POs can implement the projects. Refrain from releasing the fund without evaluating the documentary completeness of requirements.

observations.reason: Require the concerned NGO to submit their liquidation report

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to review the provisions of every Memorandum of Agreement they entered into with any government agency and strictly adhere to the prescribed period provided.
observations.managementAction: To check the contract or MOA. Liquidation still in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to strictly observe and enforce the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and its IRR, specifically its requirements for posting warranty securities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management refrain from entrusting the procurement process to the requesting office for goods not subject to public bidding. Instead, delegate to the BAC, all procurement activities, may it be public bidding or alternative mode of procurement, to ensure the proper way of procuring based on RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Management undertake to delegate procurement activities to the BAC to ensure proper procurement processes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the heads of recipient offices and the BAC to give extra effort to implement projects funded by National Government Agencies.
observations.managementAction: Management instructed the heads of recipient offices and the BAC to give extra effort to implement projects funded by National Government Agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit immediately to COA the original contracts and all pertinent supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Management submit immediately to COA the original contracts and all pertinent supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create the mandatory positions for LDRRMO and appoint personnel who met the qualification standards set in the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 in order to institutionalize the LDRRMO’s organizational structure and manpower compliment aimed to carry out the DRRM plans and programs in the long run.
observations.managementAction: Mandatory positions created and appointments made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant with the Deed of Donation and all other relevant documents pertaining to the donated Ambulance and the former to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV).
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer provided the Municipal Accountant with all the necessary documents for preparing the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV). Subsequently, the Municipal Accountant prepared the JEV to properly record the asset in the Municipality's books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant appropriately disclose the receipt of funds amounting to P100,000.00 in the Municipality’s Notes to Financial Statements. Also, the Municipal Treasurer follow up with the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) Baguio branch to obtain the details of the fund transfer.
observations.managementAction: Subsequently, the Municipal Accountant prepared a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the previous entry. The Municipal Treasurer follow up on transfer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant classify PPE items to their proper account classification in accordance with the revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has prepared the necessary journal entry for the classification of PPE items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasury Office adhere to the daily remittance of collections as prescribed in Section 29, Volume I of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs to safeguard the funds against risks of loss, theft, and improper use.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer implemented the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer use the prescribed format of the RPCPPE and implement the tagging of property stickers to conform with the guidelines set.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer to adopt the prescribed format of the RPCPPE and implement the tagging of property stickers to conform with the guidelines set. Retagging of property stickers is currently ongoing to ensure compliance with the prescribed guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the contractors to provide warranty securities for structural defects as required in Section 62.2.3.3 effective one year from the end of the defects liability period, to ensure sufficient financial coverage for potential structural defects during the warranty period.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer has adopted the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for transfers of unutilized DRRM Funds to the special trust fund by year of transfer.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared and maintained SLs for transfers of unutilized DRRM Funds to the special trust fund by year of transfer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management to direct the HR Designate to ensure that the vacant plantilla positions are filled with eligible and deserving personnel.
observations.managementAction: The Management has directed the Human Resources Designate to ensure that the vacant plantilla positions are filled with eligible and deserving personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide adequate disclosures on the LGU's Loans Payable account so that the liquidity and solvency of the Municipality could be properly assessed.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has fully disclosed all relevant details regarding Loans Payable in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure transparency in government procurement and maintain a special account in the General Fund for all loans availed by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The loan was fully settled. Additionally, the Municipal Accountant assured the Audit Team that a special account within the General Fund will be maintained for all future loan transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat update themselves on the rules and regulations on the posting requirement of the Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed, to ensure the on-time posting of the same to the PhilGEPS website, the Municipality’s official website, and the three conspicuous places; attach a Certification of the posting of Notice of Award in the conspicuous places, upon submission of the contract documents; and direct the Municipal Administrator to fast track the updating of the Municipality’s official website, and immediately post the Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed, thereto.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) has adhered to the recommendations. The BAC Secretariat was required to update and attach certification of posting; the Municipal Administrator was directed to fast track updating of the website.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly impose the collection of the balances from water rental fees, enforce disconnection of water supply if the delinquent resident failed to pay the amount due, and ensure that the official receipts covering payments are presented prior to the release of the equipment in compliance to the municipal ordinance.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has recognized an allowance for impairment on long-overdue accounts, which is reflected in the financial statements as of December 31, 2023. Additionally, the enforcement of disconnection for delinquent water bills aging 90 calendar days (90 CD) is ongoing, in accordance with Municipal Ordinance No. 38, Series of 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to:
1. Record the following adjusting entries:
+Prior Period Adjustment ₱3,445,677.38
Other Supplies and Materials Inventory ₱3,445,677.38
To adjust the PPE’s recorded as inventory.
Special Education Fund
Prior Period Adjustment ₱544,355.00
Other Supplies and Materials Inventory ₱544,355.00
To adjust the PPE’s recorded as inventory.
2. Strictly adhere to the guidelines provided under COA Circular No. 2022-004 to ensure proper recording and control of semi-expendable assets.

We recommended that the: (a) Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of the immovable properties of the LGU and prepare the necessary steps for the recognition of the missing items in accordance with the guidelines provided in COA Circular No 2020-006; (b) Municipal Accountant to derecognize the PPEs that are missing during the physical count or non-existing, after grant of specific authority from COA; and (c) Property Custodian to issue new Property Acknowledgement Receipts and Inventory Custodian Slips to all properties found during the physical count, secure copies of these documents and update the record every three years or when there is a change of ownership.

We recommended that the Municipal Development Council to include in its priority programs, the titling of the lands owned by the LGU and allocate funds for the processing of documents/requirement for the Certificate of Title to protect the property rights of the government.

We recommended that the Supplementary Feeding Program to prepare an action plan identifying the issues encountered in the implementation of the SFP and their corresponding solution so that program implementation will be completed before the end of the current school year of the target beneficiaries and unreasonable delays can be prevented after due consideration of all factors that could hinder immediate action, especially when entering agreements with other government agencies, which includes addressing issues such as delays in preparing Purchase Requests and initiating procurement activities.

We recommended that the: 1. Municipal Accountant to demand from the concerned claimant the corresponding supporting documentary requirements; and 2. MDRRMO to avoid restoring to reimbursement and coordinate with the respective department heads for the formulation of PCF or cash advance for special purpose.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain her own Report of Collections and Deposits, to make deposits in the bank using her own RCD, and strictly follow the guidelines provided in the Manual on New Government Accounting System Volume I and Volume II in order to fully attain the transparency of collections and deposits.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to require all collectors to prepare the RAAF and submit to her by the end of the month to report the number of accountable forms received, issued, and canceled and consolidate the RAAF into a CRAAF to be submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from approving the Obligation Request for the aforementioned PPA that is stated in generic terms unless there is an authority through an approved resolution by the local sanggunian.

We recommended that the: 1. Municipal Engineer to require the contractors of the nine infrastructures projects out of 13 to post warranty security to guarantee their performance in the event of eventual structural defects/failure; and 2. Municipal Accountant to require guarantee instruments for advance payment in any of the enumerated in Paragraph 4.2 of Annex E.

We recommended that the GPFS to guarantee the completion of all scheduled activities in accordance with the guidelines outlined in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 by identifying gaps and addressing them immediately with the active support of Management.

We recommended and Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the provisions of the law regarding the mandated monthly net take-home pay for government employees to boost the morale and productivity of its employees in government service.

We recommended and Management to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan and the Sangguniang Bayan to ensure that local fees and charges are updated every five years pursuant to Section 191 of the Local Government Code, with the view of increasing the revenue collections of the Municipality.

We recommended that the: a. The Inventory Committee to conduct an annual physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality and prepare a Report on Physical Count of PPE to be submitted to COA not later than January 31 of each year. b. The Municipal Treasurer’s Office and Inventory Committee to cause the immediate disposal of unserviceable items and the Municipal Accounting Office to record the dropping from the books of the items disposed of; and c. The Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prepare and maintain Property Cards and ensures reconciliation against the Accounting ledgers cards.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to ensure that all parcels of land owned by the Municipality are duly registered under the name LGU San Miguel and secure copies of the corresponding Torrens Certificates of Title (TCTs).

We recommended that Management direct the inventory committee to complete the physical inventory in three (3) months or less and to prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the COA Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities, and ensure full implementation of “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided for in COA Circular 2020-006. Further, direct the pertinent official to comply with the guidelines set in COA Circular No. 2018-002 to insure the properties with the GIF.

We recommended that Management direct the Inventory Committee to undertake the necessary action/s for the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties of the LGU to avoid further deterioration and consequent loss and/or further deterioration in its value. Systematic and timely disposal will yield benefits of, among others, a higher appraised value and by enabling storage areas available for other purposes.
We recommended and Recognize the amount pertaining to the road lot recorded in the books.

We recommended and agree that Management: a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of using expense method in recording supplies and materials purchases, maintain an inventory account using the perpetual inventory system, and prepare supplies ledger card per stock number; b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain supplies and materials stock cards per stock number, regularly prepare the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued, and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for recording purposes to present its inventory accounts in the financial statements fairly; and c. Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count pursuant to the provisions of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

Four (4) mandatory positions for the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (LDRRMO) were not provided pursuant to DILG-DBM-CS JMC No. 2014-1, thus, may not assure the continuity of the implementation of DRRM plans and its realization in the long run.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to provide/submit to COA Office the lacking documentary requirements for further evaluation. Further, strictly adhere the guidelines in the granting, utilization and accounting of the funds released to NGOs/POs as provided under COA Circular 2007-001 to ensure that government funds are spent in accordance with the purpose.

We recommended and Management, thru the Sangguniang Bayan, expedite the revision and updating of the Municipal Economic Development Code in order that fees and rates will be adjusted and/or updated to meet the financial needs of the LGU.

We recommended that the Management direct the Inventory Committee prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit to the auditor. Likewise, reconciliation of accounting and property records should be done. Moreover, property custodian designate shall update and renew the PAR; and maintain property cards which shall be regularly reconciled with accounting ledger card.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers with the stated amount due and collectible for the year which would be the basis of the latter to set-up the RPT/SET receivable at the beginning of the year. Further, we also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the detailed/updated list of delinquent taxpayers every year and reconcile their records with the Accountant.

We recommended and Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the detailed list of delinquent taxpayers and impose the legal remedies on the collection of delinquent taxes to increase/improve revenue collections.

We reiterated our recommendations that Management prioritize the creation of inventory committee and conduct the physical inventory count. Likewise, prepare the Report on Physical Count of PPE and to reconcile with the accounting records.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to:

Avail the conduct of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts and file the request for authority to derecognize non-existing/ missing PPEs to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/ or the Supervising Auditor (SA)                     as provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and 

 To direct the City Accountant, the City General Services Officer, and the Inventory Committee to promptly complete the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the Accounting records and provide the necessary details for 'For Recon' accounts.
observations.managementAction: The City GSO already has ongoing one-time cleansing of Property, Plant and Equipment Accounts (PPE) by physical count every department/ office and review the Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment of accountable personnel.

observations.managementAction: The Management agreed to adhere with the recommendation.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to:






Immediately prepare the list of all real property tax delinquencies and establish a proper monitoring mechanism for the subsequent collection of delinquencies;

Intensify collection efforts on delinquent Real Property Taxes and institute appropriate administrative and judicial remedies, if necessary, to enforce tax compliance from delinquent taxpayers; and

Henceforth, strict adherence to the provisions of R.A. 7160, specifically the enforcement of administrative and/or judicial actions for the collection of Real Property and Special Education Tax.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to the following:






Direct the Municipal Accountant to 

Ensure the setting up of RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year, supported with a certified list from the Municipal Treasurer showing the taxpayers’ names and amounts due based on the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/Taxpayer’s index card, and the proper recording of subsequent facilitation of RPT/SET collection; and

Make necessary adjustments by recognizing the RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Income based on the list of delinquent taxpayers.

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to 

Provide the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the name and the amount due and collectible for the current year based on the RPTAR as the basis for setting up the beginning balance of RPT and SET Receivables, respectively; and

Furnish the Provincial Accountant with the summary of the JEVs showing the breakdown of the amounts Due to LGUs pertaining to the share of the Provincial Governments and Barangays and the Abstract of Real Property Tax Collection.

Direct the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal Accountant to collaborate efforts in generating accurate/reliable data on RPT and SET receivable balances by indicating the names of taxpayers and amounts due from them, including penalties therefrom.

On the subsequent renewal, kindly indicate on the contracts or memorandum of assignment and ensure that the JO/COS know their duties and functions, including the office/location of assignment, before the rendition of their services.  

Strengthens payroll processing controls by ensuring that payrolls are only prepared and processed if duly supported with corroborating documents, such as the DTRs and accomplishment reports.

Require the submission of PDS, a copy of identifications, and other government-issued clearances to validate the identity of hired JO/COS personnel.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: (i) Disseminate a Memorandum to all offices of the City Government of Surigao, urging them to submit monthly consumption reports for their inventories to the CAO, as this will facilitate the timely adjustment of accurate inventories on hand; and (ii) Allocate resources and personnel for the development of an Inventory Information system, as this measure aims to streamline inventory recording processes, and make inventory recording more efficient and easily monitored.
Also, we recommended and the Acting City Accountant agreed to make appropriate adjustments to the inventory account as follows:
Table 5. Suggested adjusting entry for inventory account

We recommended and the Acting City Accountant together with their IT Specialist agreed to: (i) Address the system issues of the ePPSAS; and (ii) Re-examine the data provided in the Schedule of the PPE and effect the necessary adjusting entries such as below:
Table 7. Suggested Adjusting Entries for the Accumulated Depreciation

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to effect the necessary reclassification entries for the cost of completed projects from CIP accounts to the appropriate PPE accounts and compute the corresponding depreciation from the year of project completion.
Table 9. Suggested adjusting entry for the completed projects

We recommended and the Acting City Accountant agreed to ensure that all PPE items recognized in the books have actually existed at the time of recording, and in the meantime, record the following reversal entry:
Table 12. Suggested adjusting entry for the undelivered sea transport

We recommended and the City Treasurer agreed to: (i) Send written notices to the concerned payees before the checks become stale; and (ii) Issue a new check upon submission by the payees of the spoiled or stale checks.  Also, the Acting City Accountant agreed to: (iii) Periodically coordinate with the CTO regarding the existence of stale checks; and (iv) Take up the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts for checks which have become stale for a longer period, and no manifestation for replacement from the concerned payees was received.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to issue an EO adopting the guidelines and procedures of the One-Time Cleansing of PPE prescribed in COA Circular 2020-006, and immediately implement the same to come up with PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

For Due to Pag-IBIG account:
We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to: (i) Come up with appropriate measures to ensure that the withheld contributions are remitted promptly and accurately; and (ii) Work back and/or reconcile the remaining unreconciled amount and make the necessary adjusting entries, or remit the same to the Pag-IBIG Fund whichever is deemed fit as a result of reconciliation.
For Due to Philhealth account:
We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to: (i) Communicate with the PhilHealth Surigao City Branch that the premiums in June and July 2016, and August 2018 were already remitted in prior years and submit complete documentation thereof; and (ii) Effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts for the amount of ₱680,696.37. Henceforth, ensure that a monthly SPA is secured from PhilHealth to regularly monitor the posting of remittances in PHIC's system.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed: i) On under/non-utilization/ implementation, to find effective methods and exhaust all possible avenues to address pending issues or settle all differences that have or could likely impede the project implementation. Otherwise, consider returning the funds to the source agency/s so that these can be effectively used for other highly developmental projects; ii) On the inclusion of completed projects, to effect the proper liquidation of all implemented projects to the concerned agencies and remit the remaining balances of the funds after the completion of the project or seek authority from the issuing agency for the utilization of the excess funds; and iii) On the non-return of balance or unimplementable funds, to conduct further evaluation/verification of each of the projects listed in Annex H (since our evaluation/assessment was based only on very limited information), and thereafter, decide or consider the following courses of actions: (iv) Designate a focal person to keep a complete record of information on the funds’ project and closely monitor their status to ensure immediate project implementation and compliance with existing regulations; and (v) Request for an authority from the Source Agency to use the balances from completed projects and those which could no longer be implemented based on the stated purpose to apply the funds to other purposes, otherwise, immediately return the same.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to:
generate quarterly the PPE schedule from the system with computed depreciation, recompute manually the actual depreciation of the asset to check any discrepancies and prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to recognize adjustments.
draw adjusting journal entries for the unrecorded depreciation as of December 31, 2023:

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to make the necessary disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements as prescribed in IPSAS 1 to provide users with essential information relative to the cash items that cannot be presented on the face of the FS.

We recommended that the:
City Accountant and Treasurer process and disburse the payment for wages and overtime pay of COS and JOWs directly out of the 20% DF bank account;
City Accountant initiate the remittance of mandatory deductions withheld in the 20% DF out of the GF-Proper only if, funds from the 20% DF to GF-Proper were already transferred and are available for disbursement; and
City Accountant require supporting documents for the transfer of funds, such as approved payrolls and other relevant documents for the transfers for the payment of wages and overtime pay and remittance list for transfers for remittances of mandatory deductions.

We recommended and the CPDO agreed to ensure that future PPAs to be funded under the LGSF-SBDP of the NTF-ELCAC as required by Item 4.2.1 of LBC No. 144 dated February 28, 2022, and LBM No. 151 dated March 30, 2023 are incorporated in the Annual or Supplemental Investment Plan.

We recommended and the City Engineer agreed to:
continue to fast-track the implementation of the PPAs and ensure their utilization before December 31, 2024; and
before endorsing the request letter for project proposals of the barangay to the Regional NTF-ELCAC, ensure that the projects are inspected and assessed for readiness for implementation to avoid hampering the implementation once the funds are received.

We recommended and the City Treasurer agreed to facilitate the preparation, signing, and transmittal of the reports to the City Information Office (CIO) and for the latter to post the same to the website and full disclosure policy portal to adhere to the posting and reporting requirements as required by DILG's full disclosure policy to provide information to the public and oversight bodies on the utilization of funds and status of their implementation of PPAs.

We recommended and the CDRRM Officer agreed to coordinate with the concerned implementing offices to submit their implementation status and problems encountered that caused the non-implementation or delay, so that the former can bring up these issues and concerns to the CDRRMC for immediate resolution.

We recommended and the CDRRM Officer agreed to include in the LDRRMFIP all PPAs identified by the CDRRMC, including the list of PPAs to be charged against the unexpended balance and submit the same to the CPDO for incorporation into the AIP.

We recommended and the City Budget Officer agreed to refrain from charging these expenses against the SEF and strictly adhere to the allowable expenditures prescribed under the existing guidelines, specifically Item 4.0 of the DEPED -DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 and Item 1.0 of the DEPED -DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the transfer of the various PPE accounts under the Trust Fund to the General Fund. Corresponding depreciation expenses shall then be provided in the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant know when to use the accounts Land and Other Land Improvements and properly monitor the records of accounts to ensure the accuracy and reliability of reported balances in the Financial Statements. Further, make the necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct amount of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Accounting Office evaluate the tangible items acquired and issued before CY 2022 with amounts from ₱15,000.00 to below ₱50,000.00 which can be derecognized from the books and make adjusting entries as directed under the above COA Circular. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer prepare and maintain the prescribed forms, registries, and reports mentioned in COA Circular No. 2022-004 to strengthen controls over the semi-expendable properties; and to segregate semi-expendable properties as low-valued and high-valued items.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to record the fund transfers received by the LGU from 2009 to 2013 since these were received by them as per the Source Agencies' records.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant compute the depreciation allowance of the above PPEs from the time the asset is already available for use. Prior years’ depreciation should be taken up as Prior Period Adjustment in the books to be closed to Government Equity at the end of the reporting period.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record in the books of accounts all issuances as expenses, with RIS and SSMI as supporting documents. Further, a supplies ledger card (SLC) for each item of supplies and/or other inventory items shall be maintained to record the acquisition, receipt, and issuance pursuant to the Manual on the NGAS; and the GSO to consolidate the issuances, supported with RIS, on a monthly basis in the SSMI to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant, who shall compute the cost of supplies issued and ending inventory using the moving average method as prescribed under Section 121 of the same Manual.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exhaust all possible means to enforce settlement/liquidation for the long overdue receivables; if there is a possibility of non-collection, provide an allowance for impairment in accordance with the IPSAS 21; and if no chance of being collected, request for authority to write-off from the Commission on Audit and submit the required supporting documents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical count of local road networks and to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network not later than January 31 of each year, require the Municipal Accountant to present in the Notes to Financial Statements the required disclosure for local road networks, and to prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards for each local road; and require the General Services Officer to prepare the annual Report of Local Road Network, and to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property Cards for each local road.

We recommended that the personnel-in-charge of the preparation and submission of the required supporting documents for the cash advance for Confidential Funds be submitted within seven (7) days after the release of the check and support it with complete documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the accountable officers concerned exhaust all possible actions to prioritize the liquidation of fund transfers for every project; for long outstanding FTs, if supporting documents cannot be traced, request the Source Agencies to apply for a write-off of dormant accounts, and if granted, immediately submit the required documents for write-off.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) and the end-user of the 20 percent fund ensure that realignment/reprogramming is always supported by an appropriation ordinance and strictly adhere to Section 336 of RA 7160 to ensure the validity and legality of the newly programmed PPAs and their implementation.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer not release stockpiling/prepositioning items to ineligible beneficiaries and remind them that such items will only be released during emergency cases or already stored for a long time but before their expiration dates, supported with proper documentation.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee Team take full opportunity the application of the prescribed guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006, particularly the conduct of full physical count of PPE, the recognition of PPE items found at station, and disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items, if any, for the purpose of one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the LGU and require the Inventory Committee to prepare the Physical Inventory Plan, to be approved by the Local Chief Executive and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period, and to comply with the prescribed unique property number indicated in the property stickers used for inventory tagging.

We recommended that the General Services Officer (GSO) retrieve the records of the properties of such Property Tags/Stickers on all properties for easier cross-referencing with the AREs and proper identification of the properties; and attach Property Tags to the old and newly acquired PPEs before these are distributed to the intended end-users to establish control on the use of the government property and equipment.

We recommended the MDRRM Officer demand from the supplier the payment of the liquidated damage amounting to ₱11,550.00 if possible since the supplier has no existing claims to the LGU and require the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the provision of Sections 62 and 68 of the RIRR of the RA 9184 to protect the rights of the government from defaulting suppliers.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) prepare annually the necessary LGU Status Report on Institutional Mechanisms for Gender Mainstreaming, and the Monitoring and Evaluation Team prepare the LGU GAD Evaluation Report every end of the LCE’s three-year term for submission to the DILG.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that the funding source used for the payment of the CNA Incentive follows Sections 4.3.3 and 4.3.6 of Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Budget Circular No. 2023-1 dated November 10, 2023.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the necessary entry to correct the erroneous recording of the downloaded funds, as follows:
Cash Local Treasury – TF 	₱2,300,000
Due to NGAs	₱2,300,000
The Municipal Accountant acknowledged the above error and committed to correct the same accordingly in the books of the ensuing year.

Advances to Officers and Employees	₱5,870,385.10
The Municipal Accountant has committed to appropriately classify the cash advances for a special purpose and confidential fund to Advances to Special Disbursing Officer account.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to: (i) Ensure that appropriate and timely recording for the  Liquidations for Confidential Funds was duly accounted; and (ii) Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry for the unrecorded liquidation, to wit:

To adjust for the Liquidation of the CAs for CF

The Municipal Accountant agreed with the recommendations and assured to submit the relevant adjusting JEVs to the audit team.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to immediately effect the necessary adjusting entries in the books of the 20% DF to correct the errors cited above, and strictly follow the correct accounting treatment for financial transactions as prescribed by the COA. Also, the Municipal Accountant to apply the necessary adjustments for the on-going projects under the other funds, such as the Share from National Wealth fund, and others with the same deficiency.

Advances to Contractors			₱1,070,656.42
Construction in Progress –

We recommended and the Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed to: (i) Immediately provide the Accounting Office with a Certified List of Taxpayers, with amounts due and collectible for the current year, and a Certified List of RPT Delinquencies as of December 31, 2023 as basis to set up RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year, and to intensify the collection of delinquent property taxes; and (ii) The Municipal Accountant also agreed to comply with Sections 19(b), 20, 36 and 93 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, and  reconcile the balance of RPT/SET Receivables with the List of RPT Delinquencies (excluding the penalties) every end of the year.
We also recommended that the following entries be effected in the books of accounts for the ensuing year:
