The Municipal Accountant agreed to adopt a consistent residual value of 5 percent for the computation of depreciation on PPE.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to immediately apply the guidelines and procedures outlined in COA Circular 2022-004, and prepare the necessary adjusting entries in the books, as presented below; and for the Municipal Accountant to provide a copy of the adjusting Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to the Municipal Treasurer, facilitating the updating of the latter's records pertaining to Property, Plant, and Equipment (PPE).
Suggested Adjusting Entries for the reclassification of capitalization threshold of PPE

We recommended and the Municipal Engineering Office agreed to submit the Certificate of Completion of the completed infrastructure projects to the Municipal Accounting Office so that the necessary reclassification entries and computation of the corresponding depreciation will be effected in the books of accounts by the latter.
The Municipal Accountant has committed to reclassifying the existing completed projects that are actively utilized by the Municipality and have already yielded future economic benefits, also those for which the Municipal Engineer can furnish Certificates of Completion.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to analyze the cause of the negative balances in the subsidiary ledgers of Due to NGAs account and make the necessary adjusting journal entries, such as below:
Also, to trace the details of the returned funds amounting to ₱217,000.00 and determine the appropriate programs or projects it is attributable.
Additionally, reclassify the erroneously recorded fund receipts with the following adjusting entries:
We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to immediately prepare and submit the liquidation reports of the above fund transfers to the Source Agency copy furnished the Auditor and effect the reclassification of completed projects into their appropriate asset accounts.
The Municipal Accountant agreed to the audit observations and committed to the immediate implementation of the audit recommendations.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to communicate with the Philhealth Surigao City Branch that the premiums in July 2016, February 2017, October 2020 and January to June 2022 were already remitted in prior years and submit complete documentation thereof, and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts for the amount of ₱81,104.21. Henceforth, ensure that a monthly SPA is secured from Philhealth to regularly monitor the posting of remittances in PHIC’s system.
The Municipal Accountant assured that they will communicate with the PhilHealth Surigao City Branch and reconcile their records with theirs.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to: (i) Exert all efforts to substantiate the reconciling items and record the same in the books to reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements; and (ii) Together with the Municipal Treasurer conduct regular reconciliation of their records at least monthly and adjust the verified reconciling items to avoid its accumulation to reflect correct and reliable account balances.
Likewise, the Municipal Accountant agreed to forward a copy of the Cash in Bank Account Subsidiary Ledger to the Acting Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the monthly reconciliation.
The Municipal Accountant agreed to conduct monthly reconciliation with the records of the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed: (i) To periodically coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO) regarding the existence of stale checks and send written notices to the concerned payees before checks become stale; (ii) For checks which became stale and which require replacement, a new check may be issued upon submission of the spoiled or stale check to the MTO; and (iii) Take up the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts for those which have become stale for a longer period, and no manifestation for replacement from the concerned payees was received.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to: (i) Require the accountable officers concerned to immediately liquidate the outstanding cash advances, and after due notice/demand, if these cash advances remain unliquidated, deduct the outstanding cash advance from whatever amounts due from the concerned officers and employees; and (ii) Direct all concerned officials responsible in the grant, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 particularly in the prompt liquidation of cash advances.
Likewise, the Municipal Accountant agreed to initiate the request for write-off of those unliquidated cash advances attributed to individuals who have already passed away.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to exert diligent effort to locate the Disbursement Vouchers/Payrolls for the outstanding payable account. If these payable accounts, found to be no longer payable or without valid claimants and/or undocumented, it should be reverted to the unappropriated surplus and funds can be used to finance various projects and programs.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the misstatement of
the PPE accounts accordingly by rectifying the error of capitalizing amounts
that should be expensed/charged to the prior period adjustments so that the
PPE accounts are presented at their correct carrying amounts and to ensure
strict compliance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS I and Section 4 of COA Circular
No. 2022-004, dated May 31, 2022.

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to draw adjusting
journal entries affecting the ongoing infrastructure projects to recognize the
Construction in Progress-Infrastructure Asset account.

record the following adjusting journal entries:

review the journal entries prepared to ensure that the accounts are properly classified in accordance with the revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare and submit the Monthly BRS with complete supporting documents pursuant to the above-mentioned guidelines, draw adjusting entries upon determination of the reconciling items per book, recognize in the books the cash deposited to the newly opened special trust fund bank accounts and the remaining balance of the Social Pension account.

We recommended and the:
Local Chief Executive agreed to create an Inventory Committee that will conduct an annual physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality and prepare a Report on Physical Count of PPE to be submitted to COA not later than January 31 of each year;
Property Custodian agreed to issue new PAR and ICS to all properties found during the physical count, secure copies of these documents and update the record every three years or when there is a change of ownership; and
Municipal Treasurer agreed to prepare and maintain Property Cards and ensure reconciliation against the Accounting ledgers cards.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to require the Driver’s Trip Tickets as supporting document for the utilization of fuel; and direct the concerned vehicle drivers and the personnel in-charge to prepare and submit to the Auditor, on a monthly basis, the Monthly Report of Official Travel and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, respectively, for proper analysis, verification, and the necessary audit action on fuel consumption.

We recommended and the DO agreed to refrain from transferring CA to one AO to another and to strictly conform to the pertinent regulations in order to provide for a more efficient and effective control over the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
review, analyze and determine the existence and validity of the dormant accounts; and
facilitate the completion of the documents needed for the request of write-off and if it is not possible due to the absence of records and documents or when original documents are lost or destroyed, or cannot be produced, follow the procedures of proper disposition of dormant account as per latest issued COA Circular No. 2023-08.

We recommended and the MDRRMO agreed to prepare annually the LDRRMFIP after the recommendation of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (MDRRMC), submit the same to the Local Chief Executive for approval and to the Municipal Development Council (MDC) for inclusion in the AIP to comply to the provision of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the district school representatives agreed to submit PPMPs, and the BAC Chairman agreed to consolidate them into an APP to ensure that all the procurements are included in their APP.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to reconstitute a complete GFPS structure and composition which comprises ExeCom, TWG and Secretariat that will supervise, advocate and install a GAD Focal Point System who are responsible promoter of gender-responsive governance, protect and fulfill women’s human rights, and promote women’s economic development.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the misstatement of the Depreciation Expense-Road Networks, Accumulated Depreciation-Road Networks and Government Equity to rectify the error of recognizing the residual value for the Road Network account and be compliant with the latest provisions regarding the computation of depreciation for this account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify completed projects included in the CIP to the appropriate asset accounts and take up the corresponding depreciation expenses from the time of completion and the Municipal Engineer furnish a copy of the Certificate of Completion for every completed project to the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant prepare a journal entry voucher to reclassify the cash donation received from other LGU from Trust Liability (Trust Fund Proper Account) to Trust Liabilities-Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (DRRM-Trust Fund Account).

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer submit her cashbook to the Municipal Accountant so that the latter can initiate the reconciliation of the cash balances in the bank. Also, any necessary adjustments for differences and/or discrepancies noted during reconciliation must be immediately reflected by the Municipal Treasurer in her cashbooks.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer adhere to the daily remittance of collections as prescribed in Section 29, Volume I of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs in order to safeguard the funds against risks of loss, theft, and improper use.

We recommended that Management create an Inventory Committee and conduct a physical count of PPE in accordance with the guidelines and procedures prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006, dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor secure copies of the certificates of title (OCT/TCT) of all land owned by the LGU; and the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer to obtain a list of real properties owned by the Municipality and take appropriate action to determine the appropriate cost to be recognized in the financial statements.

We recommended that the PESO Manager-Designate coordinate with the Bids and Awards Committee to fast-track the procurement of rice and basic commodities to facilitate the project's completion and properly plan and evaluate future similar project proposals.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the necessary supporting documents, particularly the MOA as one of the basic documents supporting fund transfer as provided in Item 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, and take note of the appropriate account titles in recording LGU counterparts transferred to other agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist and Municipal Engineer work together to fast-track the immediate and proper implementation of the project and facilitate its liquidation to the source agency so that the Municipal Government can join again the search for National MMK.
We recommended that the MDRRMO convert the Toyota Hilux pick-up into a rescue vehicle equipped with the necessary equipment and apparatus in accordance with the DBM Budget Circular.

We recommended that the Heads of Offices submit their Purchase Requests within a reasonable time to give the BAC ample time to follow the proper procurement process so that dividing or breaking up government contracts into smaller quantities will be avoided and for the latter to send out three RFQs when using NP-SVP.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System submit the:
a. GAD AR supported with a brief summary of the reported program or project, copies of reported policy issuances, results of HGDG tests, and actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and recommendations for evaluation, if any, and
b. documents that show proof of the conduct of activities to properly establish the success in addressing gender issues.

We recommended that the GFPS submit documents that would substantiate or support the gender issues identified for these subject activities to ensure that the expenditures are eligible to be charged to GAD.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal HRM Officer fast-track the reconciliation of records and, if possible, request authority for the rendition of overtime to finish the reconciliation within a given time frame, then remit the correct amount per reconciliation to GSIS, HDMF, and PhilHealth to adhere to the provisions relative to GSIS, HDMF, and PhilHealth and prevent any fines or additional costs that the Municipality and its employees might incur as a result of late remittances.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct:

(a) The City Treasurer’s Office to upgrade the electronic system for                         real property to provide a summary of certified list of taxpayers, with amounts due and collectible for the year, as basis to set up RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year and                                          to furnish the City Accounting Office (CAO) of the List of                                       Real Property Tax Delinquencies as of December 31, 2022                                          to record the adjustments to RPT/SET Receivables and                                      Deferred RPT/SET Income and to intensify the collection of delinquent RPT; and 

(b) The City Assessor’s Office to update the status of real properties                      for proper valuation of the real property taxes to be collected.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:

Direct the City Accounting Office to properly record and account the collection of real property taxes, both GF and SEF, where payment is through issuance of single check and the consummation of mobilization fee to its respective fund to avoid the recurrence of the same errors; and

To exert more effort in identifying the forwarded negative balances of the Due to BIR account in the General Fund to reflect the correct amount of unremitted taxes.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:

Direct the City Accounting Office (CAO) to furnish the Bank Manager, thru the Audit Team Leader, of the copies of BRS with erroneous posting committed by the bank to ensure timely correction of bank errors to avoid a loss at the City Government’s expense and to prepare adjusting journal entries upon receipt of ₱268,609.66 from the DOH/Bureau of Treasury; and 

To furnish the City Treasury Office of the summary list of stale checks for the latter                           to inform in writing those payees with stale checks totaling ₱91,716.46, to return the stale checks to the City Treasurer or to submit a sworn statement if the checks were lost.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed to adhere with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:

Direct the City Accounting Office (CAO), City DRRM Office, and the City Budget Office to comply with Sections IV.B and V.A.1 on the receipt and utilization of cash donations for DRRM program.

Likewise, the CAO reclassify the Other Payables account on cash donations received for               Typhoon Odette to Trust Liabilities – DRRM Fund and transfer the unutilized cash donations to DRRM Special Trust Fund bank account.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed to adhere with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to:

Require the officials and employees concerned to timely liquidate their cash advances in compliance with Sections 5.1.3 and 5.8 of COA Circular No.  97-002. 

The City Accounting Office to continue sending the demand letters to the latter to timely report the expenses in the financial statements; and

Likewise, the LCE agreed to file the request for authority to write-off dormant, 10 years or more, unliquidated cash advances to the COA Audit Team Leader and Supervising Auditor by adhering COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:

Direct the City Accounting Office to reconcile the electronic system installed for Trust Funds with Subsidiary Ledgers and the City Treasurer’s Office to install the system for all Trust Fund receipts for every account to avoid usage of funds from other Trust Funds; and

Likewise, the LCE follow-up the DOH and/or the Bureau of the Treasury on the immediate release of ₱268,609.66 that was erroneously returned to them.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed to adhere with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:

Direct the City Engineering Office to carefully carry out the                                            detailed engineering for the procurement of infrastructure projects prior to project implementation to ensure that all issues are properly addressed, thoroughly review the program of works; and 

Impose liquidated damages to contractors who cause significant delays.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the City Engineering Office to carefully prepare the program of works according to the existing guidelines provided for in DPWH Department Order No. 197, Series of 2016 and/or any equivalent thereto to arrive at reasonable project cost and sufficient quantities of project items of works and to avoid recurrence of the same deficiency in the future.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Local Finance Committee and the City DRRM Office to carefully review the allocated percentages for QRF and 70 % for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery to comply with Section 4.0 of the said JMC.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed that:

a)Management promptly and properly identify the PPAs to be included in the 70% DRRMF. 
b)Further, the LCE agreed to direct the concerned implementing offices and the Bids and Awards Committee to fast-track the procurement process on all planned DRRM projects, programs and activities during the year for the constituents’ immediate benefit of the outcomes of its timely implementation.

We recommended and the Management agreed to comply with the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2012-002 by returning the unutilized amount of the DRRM Special Trust Fund for CYs 2012 to 2016 to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund and make the funds available for other social services through an enactment of an ordinance by the local sanggunian.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Ensure the usability and serviceability components of CWESS Phase 1                                                to safeguard it against loss or wastage; and

Conduct regular CWESS maintenance and repair schedule and provide necessary trainings of CWESS personnel to ensure system’s proper and efficient operation in the future.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Further assess and evaluate the necessity of hiring consultants and make use of the available personnel in the department to avoid duplication of duties and functions; and

Discontinue the contract of service whose duties are readily available and did not require special skills and their services can be dispensed with                                    to save government funds and resources.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed that:

Management formulate hiring plan of casuals/contractual/job orders to strengthen internal controls pertaining thereto and clearly define its                                        functions and duties; and
 To direct the HRMD Office to facilitate the installation of electronic daily time records and to conduct regular spot checks for employees assigned in the barangay. 

We recommended also those employees who have been working with the city for more than 10 years be given priority, if qualified, for casual positions to be created.
observations.managementAction:
The Management agreed to:

1.Formulate local policy on hiring job orders and contract of service employees

2. Facilitate installation of biometric machines in government buildings outside the City Hall compound. 

3. Conduct of regular spot checks for employees assigned in the barangay.

4. Prioritize hiring of qualified Job Orders who have been in the government for 10 years or more for vacant regular positions.
observations.reason: The City is still contemplating on hiring casuals, since additional appropriations will be needed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:

Direct the City Treasurer’s Office to use the ATM payroll system for the payment of wages to all its Job Order workers to deter possible risks of loss of government funds; and

 To instruct the City Human Resource Management Officer to request the DBP Manager for the enrolment of those JO workers not yet enrolled to the ATM payroll account.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed to adhere with the recommendation.
observations.reason: There are still JOs who could not avail the ATM Payroll as they are just project-based.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
Require the concerned officials on the immediate submission of the                  Accomplishment Report as of September 30, 2022 and the Schedule of generated savings from the allowable MOOE to the Auditor concerned in adherence to DBM Budget Circular No. 2022-3 dated                    October 19, 2022.

Likewise, direct the Accounting Office to check the completeness of the documentary requirements before processing the claim to avoid the same deficiency in the future.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:

Direct the Local School Board to ensure that all proceeds from the additional one percent (1%) tax on real property accruing to the SEF are all appropriated, allocated and utilized within the budget year to support the City Division’s public schools.

Likewise, the DepEd City Division regularly monitor the implementation of the PPAs charged against the SEF and request the City Government for its timely implementation, if a delay occurs.
observations.reason: The experienced implementation delays are primarily attributed to unforeseen circumstances beyond their control.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Immediately send demand letters to the concerned barangays requiring them to liquidated/ settle their unliquidated fund transfers and to comply with Section 6.4 of COA Circular No. 94-013. 

Direct the City Accountant to issue Certification as a documentary requirement for fund transfer as provided for in the COA Circular No. 2016-002.

We recommended that Management construct an adequate storage/ warehouse facilities in accessible, safe and secured areas with shelves or cabinet to protect the goods procured by the City Social Welfare and Development Office for stockpiling and the General Services Office for other inventories of the City Government to accommodate more goods necessary to efficiently manage disaster and other office needs.
observations.managementAction: As confirmed by the City Administrator, the City planned to establish a warehouse near the CDRRMO building at the City Bus and Jeepney Terminal area.
observations.reason: The planned storage facility at the Old Ice Plant faced ownership issues, making repair or renovation impossible. The CSWD proposed and received approval for renting a warehouse to store food and non-food items, addressing the need for additional space to stockpile family food packs from the DSWD Regional Office. The CDRRMO is currently preparing required documents for the warehouse rental.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the concerned personnel in the City General Services Office (CGSO) to strengthen its internal control system by not preparing the Inspection and Acceptance Report (IAR) for goods or services not yet delivered or rendered and not certifying in the delivery receipt that the City has already received the undelivered goods to avoid legal implications in the future.

Instruct the CGSO to inform this Office upon receiving the questioned goods/services to enable us to witness the receipt and inspection.
Instruct the City Accountant to review subsidiary ledgers of individual creditors for possible duplication of entries, to recognize accounts payable in accordance with Paragraph 10 and Item B of Paragraph AG 16 of International Public Sector Accounting Standard (IPSAS) 41, and to draw the following proposed journal entry to correct the accounts affected.
observations.managementAction:
a. Instruct the CGSO to implement the recommendation.


b. To Direct the CGSO to follow the recommendation.


c. To Direct the City Accountant to review the SL of creditors for possible duplication, strictly adhere to Paragraph 10 and Item B of Paragraph AG 16 of IPSAS, to draw JEV to correct affected accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the City Accountant to prepare AJE for the book reconciling items after preparing the BRS, including those stale checks to reflect the correct Cash in Bank-LCCA balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: To direct the City Accountant to follow the recommendation

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct CGS Officer to submit, as soon as possible, the complete supporting documents to the City Accounting Office (CAO) to better facilitate the recording of the procured heavy equipment and firefighting equipment and accessories to its proper PPE Accounts.

Instruct the City Accountant to draw the following journal entries.
observations.managementAction:
a. Follow the recommendation.

b. To direct the City Accountant to draw the journal entries

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Refrain from engaging in similar kinds of transactions in the future;

Effect payment to the appropriate supplier/ contracting party.

Follow the provisions of RA 9184 on whatever mode of procurement is adopted to avoid irregular disbursement of funds.
observations.managementAction:
a. Follow the recommendation.

b. Instruct the BAC and CTO to follow the provisions of RA 9184

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the City Health Officer to fast-track the utilization of the fund as stated in the MOA to deliver the benefits afforded to the public health and non-health workers, taking into consideration the completeness of the supporting documents to show the validity of their claims.
observations.managementAction: Instruct CHO to follow the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the City Assessor and Sangguniang Panlungsod to conduct the general revision of SFMV once every three (3) years to generate additional revenue from increased real property tax assessment to finance its various programs, projects, and activities (PPAs).
observations.managementAction: Instruct the City Assessor and the Sangguniang Panlungsod to follow the recommendation

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the City Treasurer to perform the following actions necessary to proceed with administrative actions:
 
Ensure the service of notice of RPT delinquency through mail or personal service to property owners/administrators at the last known address as shown in the City’s tax rolls or property tax records or at his residence, if known to the City Treasurer or Barangay Captain. 

Prepare updated and detailed monitoring reports indicating the served and unserved RPT delinquency notices, the number of notices made, and the underlying reasons for non-service for the management’s proper disposition.
observations.managementAction: Instruct the City Treasurer to follow the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

Revisit and identify the City’s unimplemented programs/ projects/ activities (PPAs) funded by the amount invested in time deposits/HYSA.  Determine whether these PPAs still need to be implemented for the management’s proper disposition.

Determine any idle funds and make them available for the implementation of identified PPAs instead of applying for loans with higher interest rates than interest rates from investment.

Henceforth, ensure that only idle funds in the General Fund, if any, defined under existing rules and regulations, are put in money market placements with authority from the Sangguniang Panlungsod.
observations.managementAction:
a. Instruct all department heads to revisit and asses the PPAs.

b. Revert PPAs that not implementable and re-appropriate for projects that readily implementable.

c. Terminate all Time deposits/HYSA

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Direct the CAO to effect the following proposed adjusting journal entry.

Instruct the CGSO to prepare and submit to CAO the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI), together with the supporting Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) every month, if necessary, to support the adjustment of the inventory account in the books; and

Direct the CAO to draw the necessary JEV on the basis of the submitted report to recognize the issuance and correspondingly reflect the correct balance of the inventory account.
observations.managementAction: The required RSMI had been submitted by the City General Services Office to the City Accounting Office.
Necessary adjusting journal entries to effect the reclassification for some completed projects were already drawn.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Acting City Accountant to thoroughly evaluate the transactions based on applicable guidelines and strictly adhere to the provisions of Item 4.5 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-1 on the grant of COVID-19 hazard pay to prevent double payment of such benefits.  Moreover, requires the concerned employees to refund the excess payment of Hazard Pay.
observations.managementAction: Required concerned PHW/SW to refund the hazard incentives received thru payroll deduction.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

Direct the City Budget Officer to ensure that grants subject to fund availability, be only made when such condition is satisfied and to ensure that charges made against 20% DF do not include items expressly stated as ineligible under Section 2.2 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2020-01;

Instruct the Acting City Accountant to carefully review, prior to approval, disbursements processed against applicable laws, rules, and regulations to avoid possible disallowance in the audit; and

Likewise, require the concerned officers and employees to refund the amount of hazard pay which was inappropriately charged against the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: Required concerned PHW/SW to refund the hazard incentives received thru payroll deduction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the City Accountant record the bank credits and debits CYs 2013 to 2018 in the books based on the validated bank statement to the affected cash in bank accounts. Additionally, request the debit and credit memos for bank debits and credits reflected on the BS for CY 2019 from the concerned depository banks. Further, record the unrecorded check disbursements. On the other hand, instruct the City Treasurer to communicate with the payees of stale checks for replacement otherwise, forward the list to the City Accountant for necessary adjustment to the cash in bank accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the job orders or contract of services personnel the registration documents from HDMF to fully remit the unremitted balances on the calendar year 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommend Management to require the City Accounting Office conduct a detailed review on the paid payrolls in previous years, and if the amount was determined and established require the City Treasurer’s Office to pay the amounts due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth. In addition, require the Human Resource Office to review the master list of all employees in previous years. observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to strictly adhere to the full implementation of RA No. 9003 and hasten the establishment of sanitary landfill to ensure the protection of the City’s constituents from possible ecological impact.

We recommended that Management shall strictly comply with the minimum standards and operating requirements of a controlled dump in accordance to Sections 39 and 41 of RA 9003.

To address the challenges in the existing sanitary landfill the City shall:

a.	Maintain the drainage system to ensure proper flow of water;

b.	Provide cover procedure befitting the existing condition using landfill equipment to promote lateral runoff of precipitation and to prevent ponding. Grades shall be established of sufficient slopes to account for future settlement of the fill surface; and

c.	Maintain access road to facilitate better transport of collected solid waste.
observations.managementAction:
Additional heavy equipment in the Eco-Park

2. Purchase of brand-new backhoe and dozer

3. Convert Eco-Park to Sanitary Landfill

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall revisit or carefully study the legality and propriety of the operation to ensure that the interest of the government is amply protected. Further, we recommended that Management shall execute a Memorandum of Agreement/contract with the authority of the Sangguniang Panlungsod that clearly defines the responsibilities and accountabilities of both parties to guarantee that the operation is clothed with legal authority.
observations.managementAction:
1. The Office of the City Mayor issued a memorandum dated January 19, 2017 designating evaluation team for operations of the Public Private Mix at the City Health Office in connection to COA Findings dated December 2, 2016.

2. Series of meetings were held related to review and evaluation of the PPM facilities

3. Proposed agreements/ resolution of the issue is for finalization

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant faithfully represents the effects of transactions, other events, and conditions in accordance with the definitions and recognition criteria for assets, liabilities, revenue, and expenses set out in IPSASs.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee, that is composed of two or more employees including the GSO who will conduct physical count of all PPEs, shall prepare the RPCPPE and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor, the GSO maintains PPE Cards, and the Accounting Office prepares and maintains PPE Ledgers Cards and ensures that reconciliation of their records is done before preparation of the annual financial statements and that the Municipal Accountant, Property Custodian, and Inventory Committee to complete the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the Accounting Office records to validate the accuracy of the PPEs.

We recommended that the Management requires the Municipal Engineer and the end-users of the PPAs to ensure that the 20% Development Fund and its PPAs be fully utilized and monitored in order to immediately address issues that will be encountered in the project implementation and achieve the optimum desirable socio-economic and environmental development.

We recommended that the Management requires the LDRRM Officer to formulate or propose programs that are achievable to ensure implementation within the year and ensure that the QRF is utilized for its intended purpose in order to maximize the use of the fund which is to respond and recover from the impact of disaster/catastrophe.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to employ additional procedures that would fast-track the reconciliation of their records and computations so as not to cause delay, uses only one bank account in the issuance of checks in relation to the remittance of the barangays’ share of RPT to prevent inconsistencies and to have proper monitoring of the accounts and observe the rules and regulations provided in the Local Government Code, and release all the shares to the barangay governments pursuant to the provisions of the LGC.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive create a Disposal Committee be created, pertinent documents be prepared by the Disposal Committee to facilitate appraisal review of the unserviceable properties by the COA, including: a properly accomplished Inventory and Inspection Report for unserviceable properties, appraisal Report of unserviceable properties for disposal; and other related documents such as photographs of unserviceable properties for disposal; and files the application for disposal with the COA, pursuant to COA Circular 92-386.

We recommended that the Liquidating Officer immediately turn over all collections received to the Municipal Treasurer for review and validation of the official receipts to ascertain the accuracy and completeness of the amounts received for deposit in compliance with Section 65(c) of the Government Accounting Manual, Volume I.
We recommended that the Management adheres to and follows what has been imposed in the guidelines of any assistance at all times to eliminate or at least avoid questionable transactions.

We recommended that the Management requires the concerned officials to immediately submit the required Accomplishment Report, in adherence to the prescribed laws, rules, and regulations and to support the validity of the payment on CNA incentives. Likewise, directs the Accounting Office to check the completeness of the documentary requirements before processing such claims. Moreover, obliged the concerned officials and employees to refund the overpayment of ₱46,800.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant pays the unpaid cash gift, year-end bonus, and CNA Incentive of the five elected officials and one employee totaling ₱239,808.10, allot budget for the monetary benefits of the elected officials during their terms and strictly adheres to the provisions of DBM Budget Circular No. 2016-4 dated April 28, 2016, and SMMEA Resolution No. 001 series of 2022 dated November 23, 2022, in granting the cash gift, year-end bonus, and CNA Incentive.

We recommended that the Management should be updated on any regulations and strictly adhere to the DBM Circular No. 2022-1 dated February 11, 2022, in the procurement of motor vehicles in the ensuing transactions.

We recommended the following: directs the Municipal GSO-Designate to facilitate the maintenance of stock cards for supplies to account for the receipt and disposition of the same and prepare Requisition and Issue Slip for issuance and the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued monthly and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for recording purposes to fairly present its inventory accounts in the financial statement, instructs the Municipal Accountant to observe proper adjustment of inventory accounts, prepare supplies ledger card per stock number and make an internal control policy on the maintenance of stock cards of the MDRRMO, MTO and MHO to establish authority on the control of inventory and prepare and submit the distribution lists to the Municipal Accounting Office monthly for recognition of expense; and reviews and revitalizes the composition of the Inventory Committee and fuel them up to religiously conduct the required annual physical count of unused/undistributed supplies and materials.

We recommended the following that the Municipal Accountant coordinates with the Municipal Engineer and reconciles the prior years’ completed projects not included/booked in the PPE accounts, the Municipal Engineer conducts inspection/validate status of identified completed projects booked as CIP with no details/schedules/unknown status so that the proper adjustments may be made to the CIP accounts, the Municipal Engineer issues Certificate of Completion on the validated completed projects to be used as basis by the Municipal Accountant in the reclassification of the CIP accounts to the proper PPE account; and the Municipal Accountant takes up the corresponding depreciation expenses of their classified completed projects from the time of completion until December 31, 2021, and henceforth ensure reclassification of all completed projects in the CIP account to the appropriate asset accounts.

We recommended that the Accounting Office closely monitors the liquidations of the fund transfers received and see to it that the said liquidation will be received by the Sourced Agency before dropping the said amount in the books of the LGU to ensure the accuracy and reliability of reported balances in the Financial Statements and make the necessary adjusting journal entry to reflect the correct amount of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant applies all means to identify the details of the payable accounts so as to settle these obligations, or to revert this amount to the unappropriated surplus of the Barangay since this has been long outstanding and no actual claim was made against it, and strictly monitors the balances of guaranty/security deposits payable and facilitate the prompt payment release of retention money through proper communication by phone or email with the proper officials of the concerned suppliers or contractors to prevent further accumulation of balances of the said account.

We recommended that the Management obliges the Source Agencies to provide MOAs to all fund transfers received by the LGU, and direct the responsible official/s of the Municipality to submit the Memorandum of Agreements (MOAs) of the above fund transfers. Henceforth, in the ensuing years that all MOAs covering fund transfers should be submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period for a timely and thorough audit.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: 

(a) Commit that the appropriated 20% Development Fund projects are procurement-and implementation-ready through measures by ensuring that planning and pre-implementation documents are prepared during the budget call and ready upon approval,  and 

(b) Direct the procurement practitioners (HOPE, BAC, and end-users) of their obligation to comply with the timelines for procurement and come up with courses of action to reduce the number of days of the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The Management assured, during the exit conference, compliance with the audit recommendations in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct:

(a) The LDRRM Council to regularly monitor and evaluate the utilization of the LDRRMF based on the LDRRMFIP; and 

(b) The LDRRM Office to properly plan the period of implementation of PPAs in the LDRRMFIP for optimum utilization of the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The members of the LDRRM Council informed that they will endeavor to strictly monitor the utilization of the LDRRMF and ensure that its programs, projects, and activities are given proper timelines for its target implementation.

observations.recommendation:

                                                  (b)  The municipal Treasurer coordinates with the Municipal Accountant on the immediate remittance of the remaining balance of PDAF                                 totaling ₱4,000,000.00 to the National Treasury.

observations.managementAction: The Management asked for consideration and informed that the audit observations serve as a challenge to improve their system. However, they assured compliance with the audit recommendations in the ensuing year.

 Furnish, at the beginning of the year, the Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) of the Certified List showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year in compliance of Section 20 of NGAS Manual for LGUS, Volume I. withhold from the salaries of concerned employees, as listed in Annex “I”, the uncollected P546,662.15 income tax and remit thereafter to the BIR.

Municipal Accountant to-

prepare appropriate adjusting entries to record the uncollected balances from stallholders as of December 31, 2019; and

ensure that the succeeding revenues from contract lease will be recorded in accordance with accrual basis and Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards.

We recommended that Management:

a.	Direct the Provincial General Services Officer to disseminate the information of the provisions of Section 243 of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, Volume I and observe strict compliance to it;

b.	Remind the Provincial Accountant to ensure that all disbursement vouchers relating to land purchase are adequately supported with necessary documents as prescribed in Section 13.1 COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, before processing the payments; and

c.	Instruct the Provincial Assessor to assist in securing all the necessary documents in the transfer of the legal property rights of real property to the Provincial Government

We recommended that Management:

Direct the Provincial Engineer to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents; and

Henceforth, ensure that submitted contracts for technical review and requests for technical inspections are compliant with all the required supporting documents to avoid unnecessary delay in the conduct of the desired action.
observations.reason: No documents were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately create a functional Internal Audit Service to assist the management in ensuring that resources are used consistent with laws, regulations, and policies; resources are safeguarded against loss or wastage and misuse; financial and non-financial information are accurate and timely; and operations are economical, efficient and effective.
observations.reason: IAS to be established in FY2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management temporarily relieved the members of the Inventory Committee from their regular duties to complete the physical count of all PPEs in three months or less and require them to submit an updated RPCPPE after completion of the physical count. Direct the General Services Office and Accounting Office to update and reconcile the Property Cards and PPELC and the latter to identify the details of the “For recon” accounts in the PPE.

We recommended that Management direct the City Accounting Office                    to submit to the Office of the Auditor the RCDs, with copies of official receipts and validated deposit slips, within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month. Likewise, ensure that the City Treasurer and all accountable officers who received and issued with accountable forms for collections made for the City prepare monthly RAAF and the City Treasure submits the Consolidated RAAF with the individual monthly RAAFs not later than the fifth (5th) day of the ensuing month.

We recommended that Management continue to send demand letters to all concerned individuals for the immediate liquidation of their cash advances. Otherwise, instruct the City Accounting Office (CAO) to deduct from the salaries of the concerned personnel if they still fail to liquidate or perform all means for the collection of the outstanding amount. Require the CAO to prepare and submit journal entry vouchers on the adjustments for the erroneous recording of liquidations/settlement of cash advances on the respective books of accounts.

We recommended that Management immediately send demand letters to the concerned barangays requiring them to liquidated/settle their unliquidated fund transfers and to comply with Section 6.4 of COA Circular No. 94-013. Direct the City Accountant to issue Certification as documentary requirement for fund transfer as provided for in the COA Circular No. 2016-002.  In addition, refrain from granting additional fund transfers to the concerned beneficiary-barangays who still have unliquidated balances.

We recommended that Management expedite the reconciliation of subsidiary ledger balances for Due to NGAs account as of December 31, 2021 and immediately submit the journal vouchers on the adjustments. Otherwise, disclose the negative balances in the Notes to Financial Statements.

For the above-cited observation, we recommended that Management:
Require the Inventory Committee in coordination with the Provincial General Services Office to conduct validation of the existence of these lots and evaluate whether future economic benefits are expected to flow to the Provincial Government.
Instruct the Provincial Accountant to take up in books of accounts the unrecorded lots which are duly validated and evaluated by the Inventory Committee at their fair market value should the acquisition cost is not available.
Henceforth, strict adherence to proper recognition of an item of Property, Plant and Equipment as well as the fair presentation of financial statements provided in Philippine Application Guidance No. 3 of Paragraphs 19-20 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 1 and Paragraph 13 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standard (IPSAS) 17 is enjoined.

We recommended the following and the Management agreed to:
Require the Provincial Accountant to take up in the books of accounts the identified understatement/overstatement by drawing the following proposed adjusting journal entry:
Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain PPELCs for all recorded PPEs and create strategies to ensure accurate and timely recognition of depreciation for said items.
Henceforth, strict observance of the provisions of the proper maintenance of PPE accounts in adherence to IPSAS 17 is enjoined.

We recommended that Management direct the Provincial Accountant to:
Adjust the Cash in bank accounts by effecting the following proposed journal entries:
Communicate with the AGDB those items which require confirmation and adjust the books accordingly.
No book adjustment shall be made if the bank agrees with the reconciling item since the bank will adjust the figures reflected in the bank account.
If the bank proved that it has validly caused the addition or deduction to the account, draw the following proposed journal entry:
Require the Provincial Treasurer to deposit the amount of service charges to the affected bank account and henceforth exercise proper monitoring of accounts to prevent unnecessary wastage of government funds.
Conduct close supervision of the person in-charge of the preparation of BRS.

We recommended the following and the Management agreed to:
Direct the in-charge personnel to submit the BRS of the two (2) accounts related to Philhealth Hospital Charges and Professional Fees with account nos. 0792-0223-32 and 0792-0223-40, respectively, for CY 2021.
Direct the Provincial Accountant to submit all Bank Reconciliation Statements together with the supporting documents within the prescribed period to the Auditor to effect the timely review and evaluation of transactions as to their propriety, completeness and accuracy and consider the use of bank account snapshots to expedite the preparation thereof.
Henceforth, strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular 96-011.
We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to the guidelines of the utilization of 20% DF and 5% LDRRMF and effectively plan and monitor the utilization, henceforth.

We recommended that the Management:
Require Local Health Board to submit the list of frontline HRHs and public HWs, including BHWs, in relation to the grant of COVID-19 AHDP and SRA;
Require the Human Resource Office to submit a summary of the number of days per month from September 15, 2020 to June 30, 2021, for which the personnel of the district hospital physically reported to work together with the individual DTR; and
Require the Human Resource Office to submit an alternative work arrangement plan duly approved by the head of the agency or equivalent document to show proof that the personnel is authorized to report to the workstation during the state of national emergency, which places them in direct contact with COVID-19 patients.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Local Health Board and Chief of Hospital of every district/community hospital of the Provincial Government to submit a certified list of personnel (medical/allied-medical/other personnel) who are catering directly to COVID-19 patients from September 2020 to June 2021 and the record of patients attended.
Require the respective Chief of Hospital of every district/community hospital of the Provincial Government to submit an explanation detailing the events/circumstances wherein these 290 administrative and other personnel are in direct contact with COVID-19 patients in those number of days they claimed in the payroll from September 2020 to June 2021.

We recommended the following and the Management agreed to:
Cause the immediate full liquidation of outstanding fund transfers 	received from National Government Agencies; and
Henceforth, strict adherence with the provisions of Section 4 of COA 	Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994, and Section 3.1.2 of COA 	Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, is enjoined.

We recommended the following and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Provincial Accountants Office (PAO) and Provincial Disaster 	Risk Reduction Management Office (PDRRMO) to submit the required 	quarterly reports on receipt and utilization of LDRRMF, cash 	donations, and in-kind donations for CY 2021 in prescribed format to 	the NDRRMC through the OCD, copy furnished the COA Auditors.
Direct the PAO and PDRRMO to coordinate and relay relevant 	information as necessary and be informed of the required quarterly 	reportorial requirements as provided for in COA Circular 2014-002 and 	COA Circular 2020-009.
Henceforth, strict compliance to any circulars/memorandum/advisories 	related to the receipt and utilization of LDRRMF and donations.

We recommended the following and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Provincial Engineer to regularly supervise/monitor the implementation of projects to determine the actual status at a certain period before the targeted completion date to recommend measures to hasten completion;
Impose Liquidated Damages for the delay and deduct the same from any money due or which may become due to the contractor or collect from the securities or warranties posted by the contractor, whichever is convenient; and
Therefore, strictly adhere to the amendments of 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Provincial Engineer to make representation with the Governor’s Office for possible hiring of additional personnel to ease the present workload of the Engineering Office;
Direct the Provincial Engineer to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents; and
Henceforth, ensure that submitted contracts for technical review are compliant with all the required supporting documents to avoid unnecessary delay in the conduct of the desired action.

observations.managementAction:

The City General Service Officer also assured that all recommendations cited on the observation, especially to update for the latest guidelines regarding the recognition of PPE accounts, will be faithfully adopted immediately in coordination with the City Accounting Office.

The latter also informed that they are still undertaking an      on-going reconciliation, and the entire process requires ample time for these errors/deficiencies to be properly addressed.

observations.managementAction:
The City Treasurer informed that they already acted on the recommendation of opening separate depository account for LDRRMF share from regular sources, as enacted in Resolution No. 01, series 2021 of the Local Finance Committee, duly indorsed to the Honorable Mayor, for consideration and endorsement to the Sangguniang Panglungsod.

The City Accountant commented that the 30% share of LDRRMF QRF and 70% share of LDRRMF – Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses from regular sources, and the disbursements or expenses for the current year are part or reflected in the General Fund DBP account.  Whatever unutilized amount on the current year will be transferred to the Trust-Liabilities DRRMF account. Based on the recommendation with regard to this matter, they will create two accounts for 30% LDRRMF-QRF and 70% LDRRMF-MOOE from regular sources and transfer unutilized balance to trust books in CY 2021.
observations.reason: The city already adhered with the recommendation.

observations.managementAction: The City Planning and Development Coordinator relayed that they already indorsed the issue to the concerned offices and noted that the same was posted in the City’s website and in three (3) conspicuous places as part of their full disclosure portals located at the City Hall main building lobby, public market and land transport terminal.  They took note of pending deficiencies cited in the observation; he assured that their office will raise the same on the level of the Local Finance Committee since their primary function is on the level only of handling the posting of the subject financial reports.
observations.reason: The City already adhered with the recommendation.

observations.managementAction:
The Acting City Accountant informed the Audit Team that the Quarterly and annual reports for CY 2020 regarding the LDRRMF utilization are now submitted. She assured that the recommendations with regards to this matter will be implemented.

The CDRRMO communicated that their office was overtaken by too much response and operations to COVID-19 and other hydro-meteorological hazards that took place in 2020 that is why the submission of LDRRM expenses report was overlooked. However, as of the moment, their office has constantly coordinated with City Accounting Office to comply with the monthly submission of reports and their office has enhanced the system of monitoring the flow of expenses chargeable to LDRRMF Funds.
observations.reason: The CDRRMO already submitted the LDRRMF Investment Plan for CY 2020 and the City Accounting Office submits regularly the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund

observations.managementAction:
The Sanggunian Secretary commented that a resolution authorizing the City Treasurer to temporarily deposit to a Special Saving Deposit for 60 days with the Development Bank of the Philippines the idle cash of the city in the amount of ₱60 million, was enacted in compliance with previous observation relative to this matter.

The City Treasurer informed that this matter was effected due to the recommendation of former State Auditors, as concurred by the Sangguniang Panglungsod. Further, he noted that the said high yield deposits can be pre-terminated anytime since it has a maturity date of only 60 days.

The Acting City Accountant mentioned that the cash in bank – local currency, time deposit account has already been reconciled from their accounting record to the City Treasurer Office’s record as of December 31, 2020; they already prepared adjustments upon receipt of bank certificate of deposit balance from DBP and LBP. 

The City Planning and Development Coordinator relayed that their Office’s primary concern in this regard would be the 20%DF since it serves as the primary funding source of the LGU’s development initiatives. Their Office however is not a party to the financial management aspect of the City accounts such as the details of the decision to open up or place certain amounts which are considered idle funds into Time Deposit accounts. Such decision is best left to the wisdom of the primary financial managers of the City and the Sangguniang Panlungsod to have considered the strategic importance of such an initiative.

The City Planning and Development Officer said that the identification of projects for funding under the 20% DF is always taken from the approved AIP, with concerned offices presenting and proposing the same every annual workshop conducted yearly for the purpose of identifying priority projects. Thus, the cause of non-implementation however is not an issue of development planning/ project identification process per se but more on the twin aspect of: (1) readiness of the recipient barangay to comply with the requirements of receiving the funds; (2) absence of program of works and other technical documents; both causes are neither a primary function nor an assigned responsibility to their end. However, they assured to strengthen their monitoring functions specially those funds downloaded to the implementing barangays. 

The City Engineer mentioned that their office is eager to implement all the identified priority projects, in order to obtain the optimum socio-economic development of the City. However, he said that their office has no any discretion to directly implement those priority projects since they cannot defy the policy on the implementation of projects directly fund transferred to the Barangay, since the City Budget Office will automatically download the funds to the Barangay. The City Engineering’s only function is to extend their technical expertise to the implementing Barangay.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 11, part II of this report.

observations.managementAction: The Budget Officer informed that their non-availment of the               LGSF-COVID-19 FA to LGUs was attributed to their delayed receipt of the Local Budget Circular. The LBC was issued in September 2020, but was received by the Budget Office only in November 2020 which is already late for filling of requirements. Nonetheless, the Budget Officer assured to exert efforts to be more informed with the issued circulars and regulations, to avoid the same slip in the future.

In this regard, the City Health Office (CHO) assured that they will comply with the recommendation to have an effective inventory management system for acknowledgement receipt, to properly document/monitor the distribution and recording of goods; to implement the inventory of remaining undistributed items/good; and to facilitate the verification of correctness of balances of goods and monitoring. Also, the CHO will comply with the submission of a Summary/List of Donations received and distributed and balances to the City Accounting Office and City General Office.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.managementAction: The Superintendent of the DepEd Surigao City Division relayed her intention to comply with the appropriate format of the SEF Budget Annex A as well as the ranking of priority projects to be financed under the SEF.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 12, part II of this report.

observations.managementAction:
The City Treasurer informed that he had no intentions to delay the implementation of the projects. Alternatively, his recommendation was to minimize expenditures in contemplation of the pandemic expe-rienced by the City, particularly in the 3rd Quarter CY 2020 where the City Government only got 30% collection efficiency. However, with the diligent efforts exerted, the target was later met in December 2020 and showed sufficient funds to finance the projects. As member of the LSB, he commits to support the call for education and ensure that all available funds and existence appro-priation for the purpose will be reinforced accordingly.

The City Engr. also commented that their office has no hold when it comes to the implementation of projects by the DepEd. Nonetheless, he spoke to the DepEd repre-sentative to reli-giously submit reports to their office, in order to monitor timely the projects to be undertaken, since coordination bet-ween offices is essential with regards to the matter.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 12, part II of this report.

The DepEd also agreed to finance the installation of the idle transformers in the current year, for it to be utilized immediately.
observations.reason: The building is already completed and operational.

observations.managementAction: The Management stated that the main reason on the non-implementation of the Ordinance No. 420 was the pandemic. The City considered delaying the implementation of the Ordinance since most businesses cannot provide for the increase of the rentals and some of the renter went closed due to COVID-19. Nonetheless, they assured to implement the appropriate rental fees in the year 2021.

The City Engineer agreed with the recommendation to inform the auditor within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/ or public notices are already posted.
The City Budget Office assured to submit the report immediately.
The BAC Chairman already instructed the BAC secretariat to retrieve all the lacking documents and will immediately submit it to the COA once completed.

The City ENRO relayed their plan to improve the Management of the Sanitary Landfill by CY 2021. He enumerated the following reasons with regards to non-discharge of leachate: (1) In CY 2019 - initial sampling on waste water was conducted and found to pass the indicator/criteria; (2) In CY 2020 - suppose final application at EMB for a Discharge Permit application in order to execute /Implement the Final Discharge Activity. However, due to the pandemic it was not conducted and now targeted to be done this year. Nonetheless, constant coordination between the City ENRO and EMB-13 is implemented with regards to the application of the permit.

The City ENRO stated that this is happening due to limited funds for the construction of a Functional/Standard MRF. He mentioned their objective to reduce and minimize waste with the following commitments, to wit: (1) Construction of Standard MRF will be proposed; (2) Waste segregation will be institutionalized; (3) Continued implementation of collection of Recyclable Waste; and (4) Final waste segregation at SLF-MRF will be strictly followed.
The City Tourism Office assured that they will look into other possible assistance, technical and financial from other line agencies to ensure that the objectives of the project will be attained.

The GAD Focal Person assured that the City GAD Focal Point System will give priority activity regarding GAD management for CY 2021 with HGDG assessment consistent to the rules and regulations cited on the observation.
observations.reason: Subject for further verification by the Audit Team.

The CDRRM Office already sent demand letters to the supplier requiring the submission of the documents. Further, the matter was already elevated to the City Legal Office for appropriate legal action.

The Acting City Treasurer assured that all recommendations cited shall be taken up and implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a.	Require the Provincial Engineer to provide a complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

b.	For existing road networks, direct the Provincial Assessor to assist the Provincial Engineer in the assessment of the fair value of the road lot component of local roads under the jurisdiction of the Provincial Government.

c.	Direct the Provincial Accountant to recognize the amount pertaining to the road lot as part of the reported book value of the Road Networks account.

d.	Direct the Provincial General Services Office (PGSO) to prepare and maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map.

e.	Direct the Provincial Accountant and the PGSO to conduct reconciliation between the list of inventories per local road inventory report and road map with those recorded in the Registry of Public Infrastructure.  

f.	Direct the Provincial Accountant to take up the book value of Road Networks completed prior years in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall develop a well-planned specific project strategy that is feasible to every target beneficiary considering the geographical area of the barangay before project implementation and advice the City Planning and Development Office to look into other viable options so that the project can be put into use.
