observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create an Inventory Committee, who are temporarily relieved of all their regular duties, to complete the physical inventory in three (3) months or less, and to prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the COA Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities; and ensure full implementation of “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided for in COA Circular 2020-006. Further, direct the pertinent official to comply the guidelines set in Section 5.1 of COA Circular No. 2018-002 to insure the properties with the GIF.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the City Accounting Office to immediately prepare journal vouchers to adjust the properties below the capitalization threshold and its related accounts as follows:
Ensure that all future acquisitions of asset items with the individual cost of at least ₱15,000.00 and a life of more than one year shall be recognized as Property, Plant and Equipment in compliance with Section 4.ee of GAM for LGU Volume I. Otherwise, record them as Inventories.

We recommended that Management require the City Accounting Office to immediately submit the 3,073 DVs with complete supporting documents and to adhere the prescribed timeline in the submission of paid vouchers and documents evidencing the transactions to the Auditor concerned pursuant to COA rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management require the City Accounting Office to immediately submit the ten (10) DVs with complete supporting documents and to adhere the prescribed timeline in the submission of paid vouchers and documents evidencing the transactions to the Auditor concerned pursuant to COA rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management designate/assign personnel in the Accounting Office to analyze and reconcile the Assets and Liabilities accounts with subsidiary ledgers identified as “for recon”,  to present fairly the Statement of Financial Position. Otherwise, augment the staff of the Accounting Office if not adequate.

We recommended that Management direct the City Accounting Office to recognize Inventories account on purchases of drugs, medicines, medical, dental and laboratory supplies and Expenses account upon submission by the City General Service Office (CGSO) of the Monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) with the supporting Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS). Likewise, require the CGSO to maintain stock cards and the recipient office with the CGSO to conduct physical count on these inventory items to ensure adequate controls and monitoring thereof.

We recommended that Management come-up with an action plan with timelines, from which the Accountant and the Supply Officer to observe, for the establishment of accurate general ledger balances and Supply records on all of the inventory accounts; and undertake complete physical inventory every six months, and submit the   GAM-prescribed RPCI.

We recommended that Management immediately send demand letters to the concerned barangays requiring them to comply Section 6.4 of COA Circular No. 94-013 and that the City Accounting Office adhere to Section 6.5 of the afore-cited issuance, and furnish the concerned BLGU, as the implementing agency, with a copy of the journal entry voucher taking up the expenditures. Direct the City Accountant to issue the required Certification, as documentary requirement for fund transfer, as provided for in COA Circular 2016-002.

We recommended that accountable officers, who took charge of the deposit of collections and to whom the collections were finally turned over, reconcile their cashbook balances with the accounting records monthly and accomplish the RCD properly, and Accounting Office keep the subsidiary ledger with accurate information for            Cash-Local Treasury account.

We recommended that Management direct all concerned officials/employees to immediately settle the unliquidated cash advances by sending them demand letters. Instruct the Acting City Accountant to deduct from the salaries of the concerned officials/personnel with long outstanding cash advance if they still fail to liquidate and coordinate with the family of the deceased or perform all means for the collection of the outstanding amount.

We recommended that Management open a separate depository account for LDRRMF share from regular sources of the City through     Sanggunian Panlungsod resolution and transfer the unutilized balance of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to Trust Fund books.

We recommended that the Inventory to Team expedite the completion of the physical count being conducted to establish the actual physical existence and condition of the assets of the City and to reconcile the results thereof with the records of the Accounting Office. 

We also recommended that the CGSO prepare and maintain property ledger cards for all the PPE of the City to ensure the correctness of its account balances and promote effective monitoring and control thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed to adhere with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of the CY 2019 physical count are still on-going, as well as the establishment of property cards and ARE/PAR. Restated in Finding No. 1, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that: 

i. the City Accountant determine those projects which are still lodged in the CIP account but were actually completed and recognize the appropriate asset account and the related depreciation thereon; and

ii.	the City Engineering Office submit and coordinate timely with CAO on the status of the on-going and completed projects and its supporting documents to properly classify and present a more accurate account balance of the CIP.
observations.managementAction: The Monitoring Division of the City Engineering Office consistently furnished the City Accounting Office a copy of our Accomplishment report for a proper coordination to promote a proper classification and faithful presentation of the CIP.
observations.reason: The City Accounting are still on the process of determining those projects which are still lodged in the CIP account but were actually completed. Restated in Finding No. 1, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Acting City Accountant be authorized to withhold the salaries of employees who failed to heed the final demand letters sent to them to settle their outstanding cash advances in accordance with paragraph 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office already sent several demand letters reminding the concerned personnel for the settlement of unliquidated cash advance and last Feb. 4, 2020 the former sent again a final demand letter to the employees concerned and informing them that failure to comply herewith shall constitute a valid cause for withholding of their salary as provided for under the said circular.
observations.reason: Actions were already taken by the City Accounting Office for the settlement of the unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (i) demand settlement of these outstanding accounts; (ii) request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts where settlement/collectability could no longer be ascertained as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005; and (iii) establish adequate monitoring controls on receivables to avoid accumulation and dormancy of outstanding balances. Henceforth, stop further fund transfers to Barangays with unliquidated balances and ensure that they understood their indispensable obligation on the utilization and liquidation of funds transferred and their corresponding accountability therefor.
observations.managementAction: The management already sent demand letters to all concern barangays for the settlements of their unliquidated Fund transfer/Financial Assistance. Also, the management already controlled the releases of financial assistance to the Barangay. No financial assistance was released to barangays with pending unliquidated financial assistance.
observations.reason: The Management already monitored and accounted the funds in accordance with the existing rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant use the perpetual inventory method in accounting for the procurement of drugs and medicines and require the preparation of the necessary consumption and inventory reports from the CGSO as bases for the recording of relevant expenses. Further, the CGSO down to the level of the end-users shall prepare and maintain stock cards for these inventory items and said inventory items should be subjected to monthly physical count by the ERS, MPHS, CHO and DHU to facilitate verification of the correctness of balances of the same and ensure adequate controls and monitoring thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed to adhere with the recommendations.
observations.reason: Per verification, there are still procurement of drugs and medicines which were still recognized as outright expense. Restated in Finding Nos. 6 and 7, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

i.	Require the GSO to prepare a comprehensive record of all the motor vehicles owned by the City indicating therein the dates they were acquired, the acquisition cost of each, the repairs and maintenance undertaken, including the cost and period of every repair or maintenance undertaken on each vehicle to facilitate adequate monitoring and avoid incurring unnecessary and excessive expenditures on fully depreciated vehicles. 

ii.	Consider replacing needed but fully depreciated motor vehicles used for emergency and rescue operations by brand new units rather than spending as much as beyond 30 percent of the cost of a new vehicle for the repairs and maintenance of each of the old units.  

iii.	Direct the Accounting Office to record in the Agency books all donated assets to the City Government of Surigao and require the concerned personnel to prepare all the required documents before a necessary repair of any vehicles is conducted.
observations.managementAction: The CGSO assured that all purchase requests for repair and maintenance of vehicles and equipment will be in accordance to COA Circular No. 2012-003, monitoring will be strictly maintained, and recommendations will always be faithfully adhered to.
observations.reason: The Audit Team is yet to receive the result of the conduct of inventory and inspection regarding the serviceability if the said vehicle.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit explanation/ justification with supporting documents or computations to address the variances specified above. Otherwise, the total amount representing the variances shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Management already adhered with the recommendations and observed prudent preparation of POWs by maintaining an updated price monitoring of construction materials and labor to obtain the most reasonable price.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop the practice of splitting of requisitions and purchase orders and submit written explanation thereof. Otherwise such procurement shall be disallowable in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed to adhere with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: The audit team is yet to receive information regarding the actions taken by the management regarding the matter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC submit the mentioned lacking necessary documents and ensure strict compliance with the prescribed procurement procedures in RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC submitted the documents to the audit team.
observations.reason: Review of the submitted documents disclosed that there are still lacking documents such that of the construction of safety and health program approved by the DOLE for the Construction of BOSS Museum Building. The Audit team is yet to receive the lacking documents. Reiteration in Finding No. 26, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct, particularly the Local Development Council, to properly plan and review the project proposals submitted and to ensure prioritization of funding on developmental projects that are readily doable and without unresolved issue/s to prevent delay; and likewise, assure to establish internal control measures by conducting timely coordination, regular monitoring and evaluation of every development project to ensure that resources are managed in the most effective and efficient manner to meet the needs of its constituents.
observations.managementAction:
The Management taken the following action:
• Budget was realigned to suffice the total project cost of Brgy. Danawan Health Center;
• Re-survey was conducted to determine again the boundaries for the completion of Fencing at Residential Center for Street Children 
• Pre-construction negotiation was conducted between the owners of the structures affected by government projects.
observations.reason: Some field monitoring schedules did not push through due to the COVID pandemic and community lockdowns implemented. Reiteration in Finding Nos. 16 and 17, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Acting City DRRMO secure and submit to the Office of the Auditor any proof of product authenticity such as enumerated under Section 9.1.3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, bill of lading from the country of product origin and/or certification from the supplier that the delivered products were the same as what was presented in the bid documents. Likewise, the Acting City Accountant check the completeness of required supporting documents before claims are processed; and the Inspection Committee shall ensure that succeeding transactions and deliveries conform strictly to the specifications embodied in the bid documents.
observations.managementAction: The CDRRM Office already sent demand letters to the supplier requiring the submission of the documents. Further, the matter was already elevated to the City Legal Office for appropriate legal action.
observations.reason: The Audit Team is yet to receive the result of actions taken by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the LDRRMO to expedite the implementation of identified PAPs through efficient and effective coordination among other implementing offices with precise time frame. Henceforth, strictly observe the regulations required for prompt project implementation.
observations.managementAction: The CDRRMO assured to expedite the implementation of the identified PAPs through efficient and effective coordination among other implementing offices with precise time frame.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to Management the following courses of action: 

a)	Revisit the City’s waste diversion program and the set targets to include a performance indicator to monitor the status of waste diversion implementation towards achieving its year 2024 target of 90;
 b)	Initiate measures to address the identified root causes which hindered the efficient implementation of the program; and
 c)	Intensify the information and advocacy campaign on the importance of establishing and sustaining the MRF in the barangay level including the establishment of backyard composting activity.
observations.managementAction:
The Management taken the following actions, to wit:
The responsibility for the Residual Waste Collection was assigned to the CGSO.
Expansion of the coverage of collection to all 33 mainland barangays as compared before which only covered the five urban barangays; from 33.15 tons/day in 205 to 44.35 tons/day in 2020.
The Waste Segregation was already implemented by the City ENRO with initiatives to reduce Volume of waste dumped through “Oplan Kuha Cellophane/Papel and recyclable waste strategy”
observations.reason: Per inquiry, the record on Biodegradable and Compostable Waste were still not determined. However, the City ENRO assured that they will institutionalize the Implementation Strategy to all barangays, establishments, offices/department, groups and individuals on CY 2021, in order to attain target plan on the reduction on volume of waste dumped. Reiterated in Finding No. 28, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management ensure to amend Ordinances No. 12 and 190 series of 1988 and 2002, respectively and thereon increase revenue collection to finance other City’s developmental projects.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer stated that the Local Finance Committee already drafted and passed the resolution No. 19, series of 2019, recommending to the Local Chief Executive and Sangguniang Panglungsod the passage of an Updated Comprehensive Local Revenue Code.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that: 

a)	the Acting City Treasurer collect local business taxes with appropriate penalties and surcharges from the above-named contractors and suppliers by sending notices of tax deficiencies with the corresponding tax assessments; 

b)	henceforth, taxpayers be required to submit a Sworn Declaration of its gross sales or receipts for the preceding year with VAT returns/ Income Tax returns when it becomes available to countercheck the taxpayer’s declarations; and 

c)	pursue the collections of Local Business Taxes in compliance with R.A. 7160 and Surigao City Revenue Code to be able to generate additional funds for the City.
observations.managementAction: The Acting City Treasurer assured that all recommendations cited shall be taken up and implemented.
observations.reason: The Audit Team is yet to receive communication from the Management regarding the results of actions taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management develop a well-planned and detailed specific bangus project implementation strategy that would ensure that every target beneficiary could benefit from it by considering the availability of the Bangus fingerlings suppliers that can deliver on specified dates and requiring longer expiration periods for the fish feeds.
observations.managementAction: The City Agriculture Office made it a standard practice that feed delivery may only be done on the day or a day after the fingerlings have been delivered. Furthermore, the office plans to penalize the bangus supplier.
observations.reason: Per inquiry, the City Agriculture relayed that the bagus fingerlings were delivered and were utilized by the recipient associations; all 3 recipient were able to harvest bangus in fish cages and they benefited from the project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management revisit the project contract and identify the cause/s of the delayed project implementation, and address the same soonest. Further, compel the contractor to complete the project and make it occupiable and serviceable as soon as possible, and impose liquidated damages as provided for in the contract agreement.
observations.managementAction: The Management already compelled the contractor to satisfactorily complete the works within the specified contract time; and certificate of completion of the project has been issued when the projects was already 100% completed.
observations.reason: Although the building was already completed; per inspection it still non-operational.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management shall intensify the conduct of analysis and reconciliation of these accounts so as to present fairly the accounts in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.managementAction: The Management is still on the process of verification and reconciliation of these accounts.
observations.reason: The ‘for recon’ accounts pertaining to PPE were already adjusted. However, other ‘for recon’ accounts are still existing. Reiterated in Finding No. 5, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly observe the due dates for remittances to GSIS and Pag-ibig, and regularly monitor and analyze the recording and remittances to avoid payment of interest, penalties and surcharges.
observations.managementAction: The management assured that recommendations will be implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall undertake thorough gender analysis based on relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues to ensure that the approved PPAs incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget are responsive to issues and concerns; and continue the processing of relevant information in the GAD database to have an effective basis for gender responsive planning and policy formulation.
observations.managementAction:
The following actions were taken by the Management:

• Facilitated the passage of CDC Resolution no. 5 series of 2019: A Resolution Enjoining all Instrumentalities by Local and National Government Agencies Operating in the Locality to Maintain and Submit Sex-Disaggregated Data last May 27, 2019

• Sent letters to concerned offices for submission of sex disaggregated data

• Enjoined GAD Focal Points to submit their GAD data during the conduct of GAD meetings
observations.reason: The actions to be taken by the Management were delayed due to the absence or lack of pertinent data from concerned offices. Reiterated in Finding No. 30, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall require the BAC Secretariat to include in the pre-procurement conference the a.) detailed engineering according to the prescribed standards in the case of Infrastructure Projects; and b.) confirmation of the availability of right-of-way site or location, and the possession of the affected properties, subject to Section 17.6 of this IRR to assess readiness of the procurement at hand.
observations.managementAction: The management assures that recommendations will be adhered and implemented immediately.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2020, there are still contracts that lack detailed engineering and confirmation of availability of location or right-of-way necessary for the readiness of procurement at hand. Restated in Finding No. 26, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall: (i) enforce compliance with the minimum operating requirements for sanitary landfills pursuant to Section 42 of RA 9003; (ii) direct the efforts towards the maintenance of landfill facilities such as access road signs, perimeter barriers, adequate and steady water supply; (iii) strictly require the operation and maintenance personnel to wear appropriate protective gear/equipment when inside the landfill; and (iv) expedite the repair of the lone bulldozer and to conduct proper maintenance thereof so that spreading and compacting of the wastes can be done as rapidly as practicable.
observations.managementAction:
In compliance with the minimum requirements in operating a Sanitary Landfill, the management taken the following actions:
-Access Road signs/billboards to SLF Facility were already installed in CY 2019, and some were already damaged in CY 2020
-Protective gear/equipment of SLF Personnel was regularly provided annually, such as Raincoat, boots, uniforms and protective gears.
-Heavy equipment was already purchased such that of the 1 crawler Excavator (Backhoe) in addition to the 1 unit Backhoe already owned.
observations.reason:
Per inquiry with the City ENRO, discharge permits examination supposed to be conducted by the EMB-13 was scheduled in the 1st or 2nd quarter of 2021 due to the pandemic. Nonetheless, close coordination with EMB-13 for the said permits will be made and all other permits with be undertaken within the year CY 2021.

Furthermore, soil embankment to SLF-Cell No. 3 will be done in CY 2021 if weather warrants the performance of such. Restated in Finding No. 27, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the OIC – City Accountant and General Services Officer in coordination with all offices of the City Government to: (i) conduct a physical count of inventories in order to establish the existence; (ii) consolidate all inventory on-hand; and (iii) conduct reconciliation of inventory accounts to arrive at the accurate and actual amounts; and 

Instruct the City Accountant to (i) comply PPSAS 12, that is, record the purchases of supplies and materials as inventory and recognize expense when the inventories are consumed or distributed; and (iii) prepare JEV to adjust the reconciling items.

observations.recommendation: Management instruct the City Engineer to strictly monitor the projects and assign a representative that has technical competence to oversee its implementation compliant to quantity, quality and specifications as stipulated in the POW and Plans.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall develop a well-organized accounting system in order to expedite those accounts needing immediate payment to avoid penalties and surcharges. Management should also inform or notify in writing those departments/offices directly involved with the processing of transactions and also where the transactions commence, especially those which processing is delayed, regarding the accounts that need immediate payment in order for them to give priority and speed up the settlement prior to its due date. Henceforth, any officials who neglected to pay the bills on or before the due date be made personally liable for any surcharges resulting therefrom.

observations.recommendation: We commended the Office of the City Economic Enterprise for the significant accomplishment and encourage them to sustain the effort in the efficient and effective revenue collection to ensure sustainability and to generate more funds to the City.
We recommended that the Management shall intensify the conduct of analysis and reconciliation of these accounts so as to present fairly the accounts in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that Management shall:
conduct the annual physical count of PPE in accordance with section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Vol. 1 to reconcile and establish the balances per actual count and per accounting records; and
prepare and maintain the property card and ARE/PAR and to renew it every three years in accordance with the instruction on the use of PAR to promote effective monitoring and controls over PPE.

We recommended that Management shall conduct regular monitoring on the utilization of fund transfers to the concerned barangays and to send demand letters thereto for the immediate liquidation of their outstanding balances.

We recommended that Management shall direct the Office of the Acting City Accountant to effect the adjustment on loans payable account. Henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation to prevent unreconciled balances.
We recommended that Management shall strictly observe the BIR due dates on the remittances of taxes withheld to avoid payment of interests, penalties and surcharges.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the due dates for remittances to GSIS and Pag-ibig, and regularly monitor and analyze the recording and remittances to avoid payment of interest, penalties and surcharges.

We recommended that Management shall develop a well-organized accounting system in order to expedite those accounts needing immediate payment to avoid penalties and surcharges. Management should also inform or notify in writing those departments/offices directly involved with the processing of transactions and also where the transactions commence, especially those which processing is delayed, regarding the accounts that need immediate payment in order for them to give priority and speed up the settlement prior to its due date. Henceforth, any officials who neglected to pay the bills on or before the due date be made personally liable for any surcharges resulting therefrom.

We recommended that Management shall strengthen its procurement planning activity so as to reflect the entire necessary information mandatory in the preparation of the APP as required by the GPPB; and Require the responsible persons to run-through the accurate preparation of the APP to avoid committing the same deficiency in the future.

We recommended that Management shall undertake thorough gender analysis based on relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues to ensure that the approved PPAs incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget are responsive to issues and concerns; and continue the processing of relevant information in the GAD database to have an effective basis for gender responsive planning and policy formulation.

We recommended that Management shall direct the Acting City Accountant and Acting City DRRMO to prepare and submit to COA the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for the period January to December 2018 using the prescribed format.

We recommended that Management shall instruct the head of the implementing offices to carefully review and validate the submitted request for contract time extension by the contractor and to enforce the original contract time, if feasible to avoid delay on the delivery of services to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that Management shall direct the concerned officials to submit to the City General Services Office the approved purchase request for the equipment and the approved plans and specifications for the infrastructure projects funded under SEF as soon as possible so that schoolchildren can avail of the benefits of the government projects.

We recommended that Management shall require the BAC Secretariat to include in the pre-procurement conference the a.) detailed engineering according to the prescribed standards in the case of Infrastructure Projects; and b.) confirmation of the availability of right-of-way site or location, and the possession of the affected properties, subject to Section 17.6 of this IRR to assess readiness of the procurement at hand.

We recommended that Management shall formulate measures that will protect its personnel against delayed payment of compensation that could cause demoralization, hence personnel’s productivity will not be optimized.

We recommended that Management shall require detailed engineering, timely coordination, and regular monitoring and evaluation for every development projects to ensure that resources are managed in the most effective and efficient manner to meet the needs of its constituents.

We recommended that Management shall: (i) enforce compliance with the minimum operating requirements for sanitary landfills pursuant to Section 42 of RA 9003; (ii) direct the efforts towards the maintenance of landfill facilities such as access road signs, perimeter barriers, adequate and steady water supply; (iii) strictly require the operation and maintenance personnel to wear appropriate protective gear/equipment when inside the landfill; and (iv) expedite the repair of the lone bulldozer and to conduct proper maintenance thereof so that spreading and compacting of the wastes can be done as rapidly as practicable.

We recommended that Management shall develop a well-planned specific project strategy that is feasible to every target beneficiary considering the geographical area of the barangay before project implementation and advice the City Planning and Development Office to look into other viable options so that the project can be put into use.

We recommended that Management shall: (i) require the supplier to complete the supply/delivery and installation of hardware components in accordance with the agreed technical specifications; (ii) require the supplier to submit the documents under items A-4, A-6, A-6, A-12 and E-1 to 4 of Annex “K”; (iii) expedite the installation of the transformer to provide sufficient power supply in the Command Center Building; and (iv) review the plans and timetables and make the necessary action to avoid extended delay in the commencement of operation of the CWESS Project.

We reiterate our prior year’s audit recommendation for Management to take appropriate steps in requiring strict settlement of accounts of concerned city officials and employees.  The ninety (90) days prescribed period must be observed to comply with the requirements to settle transactions suspended in audit.  Settlement of disallowances must immediately be effected by withholding the salaries or other claims of persons liable in satisfaction of the amount disallowed.

Create a task force to conduct the analysis and reconciliation of accounts to include personnel who are accounting graduates and with relevant experience, under the supervision of the Acting City Accountant, and to pursue the reconciliation process until completion.
observations.managementAction: The LCE expressed his plan to hire experts to conduct the reconciliation of ‘for recon’ accounts to hasten the process in order to clear and present the accounts fairly in the financial statements.
observations.reason: The City Government of Surigao already created a task force to analyze and reconcile the mentioned accounts. However these accounts still existed in CY 2018. This observation is reiterated under Finding No. 2, page 41, of this report.

observations.managementAction: The Management agreed to adhere with the recommendations.
observations.reason: The City Government of Surigao exerted their efforts to fast track the reconciliation. Please see Finding No. 2, page 41, of this report.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the OIC – City Accountant and General Services Officer in coordination with all offices of the City Government to: (i) conduct a physical count of inventories in order to establish the existence; (ii) consolidate all inventory on-hand; and (iii) conduct reconciliation of inventory accounts to arrive at the accurate and actual amounts; and 

Instruct the City Accountant to (i) comply PPSAS 12, that is, record the purchases of supplies and materials as inventory and recognize expense when the inventories are consumed or distributed; and (iii) prepare JEV to adjust the reconciling items.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed to adhere with the recommendations.
observations.reason: The Management plan to conduct a Physical Inventory Count in the CY2019.

observations.recommendation: The Acting City Accountant should keep track stale checks and prepare the pertinent adjusting entries.
observations.reason: The Acting City Accountant has immediately prepared journal entry voucher to effect the adjustments on the particular reconciling items based on the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Require the Acting City Accountant and City Treasurer to: (i) immediately conduct reconciliation of Cash in Local Treasury accounts; and (ii) enhance monitoring control on Cash in Local Treasury accounts by conducting regular reconciliation of balances per GL and Cashbook.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to immediately implement the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: The Acting City Accountant explained that part of the discrepancy of P1,566,954.79 was the undeposited collections of the former cashier totaling P713,618.73. Legal case was already filed before the Ombudsman Mindanao. Reconciliation is still in progress for other balances.
Demand letters were already issued to some officers with long outstanding unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Acting City Accountant to reclassify the non-current portion of the Loans Payable – Domestic from Current Liabilities to Non-Current Liabilities.
observations.reason: As per Management’s comment, this is a system error. They are still on the process of improving their system to present fairly the Financial Statement of the Agency.

observations.recommendation: Require the Acting City Accountant to (i) monitor the receipt and utilization of the fund transfers against the physical accomplishment of the projects/activities for a timely return of excess amount to the source agencies; and (ii) return immediately the excess amount of P481,629.89 to the concerned source agencies.
observations.reason: The unutilized balance of fully implemented fund transfers was already return/remitted to respective source agencies.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the City Treasurer and the Acting City Accountant to (i) conduct comprehensive reconciliation of accountable forms; and (ii) issue letter of demand to concerned AOs for the immediate settlement of unaccounted AFs; and
Stop the practice of issuing additional AFs to AOs who failed to submit the report of accountabilities.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurers Office committed to complete the encoding of all accountable forms issued and the corresponding remittance of each AOs before the end of CY 2018.
observations.reason: The CTO explained that partial verification of the listed accountable forms showed that these AFs were already remitted in various dates. The review was based on the Report of Collections and Deposits files of the individual accountable officers. The office is closely monitoring the remittance of individual accountable officers and those AOs committed delayed in their remittance were issued with demand letters.

observations.managementAction: Management assured that all audit recommendations will be considered.
observations.reason: The Acting City Accountant informed that there in on-going manual and in-house system developed for a consolidated procurement process of purchase requests of all offices within the City Government. This would address the influx of procurement documents from different offices wherein the procurement process in Accounting, Budget. General Services as well as the BAC will be minimized. Aside from that, monitoring control of prices of goods and services will be established.

observations.recommendation: Management shall direct the Bids and Awards Committee to undertake the procurement of drugs and medicines through public bidding, pursuant to Section 10 of RA 9184.  It is likewise recommended that Management study the utility and advantage of the use of Ordering Agreement in the procurement of drugs and medicine, and adopt the same if feasible.
observations.reason: As per discussion, the Management defended that there are no other suppliers willing to transact with the City. However, as much as possible, they will ensure to adhere with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:

Require the City Engineer as in-charge in the conduct of the detailed engineering, Members of the Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group to review, evaluate and ensure the completeness of the documents supporting the contracts entered into by the City Government of Surigao to prove the integrity of the bidding process conducted and also to facilitate the evaluation and review in audit; and
b.	Adhere to the laws, rules and regulations for government procurement.
observations.reason: As per review, most of the necessary supporting documents were already included in the contract folder. However, there are still information needed to be attached such as the proof of detailed engineering as discussed in Finding no. 14, page 61 of this report.

observations.recommendation: Management instruct the City Engineer to strictly monitor the projects and assign a representative that has technical competence to oversee its implementation compliant to quantity, quality and specifications as stipulated in the POW and Plans.

observations.recommendation: The BAC Chairman shall develop a mechanism that would hasten the preparation of contract documents to ensure compliance with the prescribed period of its submission.

observations.recommendation:

intensify review of the Barangays’ annual budget to consider compliance to budgetary and other requirements as prescribed in  RAs 7160, 9344, 10121, PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular 2012-01 and  DBM/DSWD  Joint Circular No. 2003-01; and
advise all barangays the prescribed allocation of funds  of the said programs.
observations.managementAction: Management sought consideration with respect to the findings with the commitment to exert greater due diligence in the review of barangay budgets for the ensuing year and assured that the audit recommendations set forth are duly noted and will form part of the agency action plan to further improve the local government’s performance.
observations.reason: The Management is still on the process of reviewing the Barangays’ budget for the CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: Fast track the submission of 1,774 DVs to avoid audit suspension and strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2009-006.

observations.recommendation: Management should (i) adhere with the laws, rules and regulations pertaining to fund transfers as well as the provisions stipulated on the MOA; and (ii) regularly monitor the receipt, utilization and liquidation of fund transfers, to avoid recurrence of deficit and disqualification to avail future grants.
observations.reason: The City Agriculturist had already taken the necessary steps to ensure there will be no delay in the liquidation because of unmonitored submission to the source agency. observations.recommendation: Management direct the concerned officials to (i) revisit the existing monitoring control on investment property and identify loopholes for enhancement in order to avoid the recurrence of subleasing; (ii) conduct a validation of occupants of all the units/stalls and identify those lessees who subleased the same and effect the termination/cancellation of the contracts.

observations.recommendation:

Enhance monitoring control in the implementation of projects to (i)immediately track problems encountered in order to effect prompt solution for its timely completion; and (ii) ensure specifications as defined in the technical documents are complied.
observations.managementAction: Management assured that the audit recommendations set forth are duly noted and will form part of ensuring agency action plan to further improve the city government’s performance in project implementation.

observations.recommendation:
(a) advise the concerned personnel to submit the required Accomplishment Reports on claims of overtime services as reflected in Annex E;  and

(a) Management to (i) conduct effective and efficient LDRRMF planning duly supported with sufficient appropriate documents particularly on highly innovative and technical programs/projects in order to have maximum understanding and prompt review process by the SP; and (ii) establish an effective communication and coordination with the legislative body in order to have a smooth and prompt review process geared towards achieving goals and objectives; and 

(b)   Sangguniang Panlungsod to (i) fast track and consider LDRRMF Investment Plan as one of the priorities in the review and approval based on sufficient and appropriate supporting documents in order to enable the LGU implement the programs/activities/projects promptly and avoid unwanted massive destruction, otherwise higher authority may pinpoint the SP as one of the responsible bodies; and (ii) establish an effective communication and coordination with the executive body in order to have a smooth and prompt review process geared towards achieving goals and objectives.

observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation:
Direct the City Accountant and the OIC-City General Services Officer to revisit the existing internal control system particularly the inspection of deliveries and enhance the system by: 

(i) having a regular reshuffling of Inspectorate Team to avoid closeness/intimacy to the suppliers/contractors;

(ii) monitor the activities of the inspectors by conducting a random re-inspection of deliveries to ensure non-recurrence of the deficiencies discussed above and warn them to refrain from doing again otherwise appropriate legal action may be taken; and 

(iii) require the concerned suppliers to deliver the lacking items or this may be disallowed in audit and warn them to refrain from doing again otherwise appropriate legal action may be taken against them.
observations.reason: Though the City Accountant has exerted efforts to enhance the internal control system, reshuffling of Inspectorate Team was not undertaken.

Inspection of bodega disclosed that CEO has undertaken rehabilitation of dilapidated storage structure while City Health Office has established a storage room where the medical supplies were in place and organized. Other offices still need rehabilitation/ establishment of their safe storage room.

observations.recommendation:
(a) stop the practice of allowing the same personnel to initiate, undertake and review/inspection of the projects/activities; and

observations.reason: Hiring of Manager to undertake the economic enterprise operations was not yet effected.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the City Engineer to:

(a) revisit the existing controls in the implementation of project particularly in the right water-cement ratio or the standard slump of the concrete mix during concrete pouring as this is detrimental on the strength of the concrete pavement;

(b) identify loopholes and install mechanism to strengthen the supervision and monitoring controls in order to avoid recurrence of the noted deficiency in the future; and 

(c) direct persons responsible/liable to effect remedial action by installing pressurized concrete epoxy to the affected parts or removable or replacement of the affected blocks at their own expense otherwise this may result to audit disallowance.
observations.reason: Remedial action by the City Engineer was not effectively done.  Scaling of concrete blocks was worsen. It can be recalled that this project was completed in June 2015 and barely three months the defects were noticeable.

observations.recommendation: Direct the City Accountant and City General Service Officer to – (i) adhere to the regulations of inventory and property management as provided in Section 114 of NGAS Manual Volume II; (ii) designate personnel to (a) handle the recording of purchases and issuances of inventories and PPE; and (b) maintain the appropriate ledger and stock/property cards – not necessarily to hire personnel for this purpose but assign additional load to a reliable personnel; and (iii) design a systematic and effective control in recording of properties and supplies.
observations.reason: As far as inventories is concern, recording of purchases of inventories is outright expense, thus, no inventory account was recognized which is deviation to PPSAS 12. observations.managementAction: Appropriated P15 million to complete the structure from the Share of National Wealth for CYs per Procurement process was undertaken and Contract Agreement was executed between the Mayor and CV Construction and Development for the completion of the building costing P14.981 million.

observations.recommendation:
(a) Management to (i) conduct effective and efficient LDRRMF planning duly supported with sufficient appropriate documents particularly on highly innovative and technical programs/projects in order to have maximum understanding and prompt review process by the SP; and (ii) establish an effective communication and coordination with the legislative body in order to have a smooth and prompt review process geared towards achieving goals and objectives; and

(b)   Sangguniang Panlungsod to (i) fast track and consider LDRRMF Investment Plan as one of the priorities in the review and approval based on sufficient and appropriate supporting documents in order to enable the LGU implement the programs/activities/projects promptly and avoid unwanted massive destruction, otherwise higher authority may pinpoint the SP as one of the responsible bodies; and (ii) establish an effective communication and coordination with the executive body in order to have a smooth and prompt review process geared towards achieving goals and objectives.
observations.managementAction: The CY 2017 Annual Budget of the City was approved on February 16, 2017 as the 2017 LDRRMF went into a series of revisions before it was finally approved on February 9, 2017.
observations.reason: To compare with the previous year’s LDRRMF, approval of the SP this year is earlier. its because the concerned officials have coordinated each other to substantiate the supporting documents for the proposed projects. However, utilization of funds is minimal in 2017 as discussed in paragraph 35, pages 102 to 104 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
(a) direct the CSWD and Barangays officials/employees to (i) ensure the availability of communication especially during emergency cases; (ii) coordinate immediately with the CDRRMC upon receipt of advisory of PAGASA and media for catastrophic event that will possibly strike the City and

(b) ensure timely information dissemination relative to the activation of DRRMC towards the Barangay officials.
observations.managementAction: CSWD has established strategies for immediate response to barangays officials calamity such that communication and supplies are timely available.

observations.reason: Remedial action to address the flaw was made to the project and reported as 100% complete. But this is still subject to COA TAS review and inspection.

observations.recommendation: Management (a) submit written justification on the deficiency as discussed above, otherwise this may cause audit disallowance of the amount incurred; (b) in the future similar case, appraisal and inspection of motor banca and other similar equipment should be conducted by competent and reliable City personnel in order to ensure attainment of the most advantageous price, quality and durability.
observations.managementAction: Management has submitted written justification of the deficiency. During 2017, another motor banca was purchased and thorough inspection was made to ensure attainment of the most advantageous price.

observations.recommendation: Management comply with the regulation on the budget allocation of one percent (1%) from the IRA for the LCPC programs/activities/projects identified in the approved Annual Work and Financial Plan as stipulated in Section 15 of RA 9344.
observations.managementAction: Management appropriated exactly 1 % of IRA for LCPC in 2018 Annual Budget.

observations.recommendation: Require the (a) City Treasurer to conduct reconciliation with the Accounting records in order to produce accurate data for the adjustments of affected accounts; (b) City Accountant and City Assessor to coordinate in the reconciliation of affected accounts, particularly the problematic accounts and effect necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer and the City Accountant has conducted initial reconciliation of these accounts.

observations.recommendation: (a) direct all officials/employees involved in the procurement process to stop the practice of advance withdrawal of goods without procurement documents and strictly comply with the regulations pertaining to the purchases of supplies and materials as mentioned above, otherwise may cause appropriate legal action against erring officials/employees; (b) instruct the City Accountant to comply the regulations on the recording of purchases of supplies and materials as mentioned above; (c) consider as priority, the provision of an adequate and safe storage room for inventories; and (d) instruct the General Services Officer to comply the requirement on the preparation of RSMI every end of the month to record the issuance of goods.
observations.managementAction: The City General Services Officer initiated the proposal on the establishment of a safe storage structure. Hopefully the budget will be realized in CY 2018 Annual Budget with an estimated cost of P10 million; The City Accountant proposes to segregate the custodianship and issuance of inventories per office.  Then, consolidation will be conducted in GSO to support the issuance of JEVs as far as receipt and consumption of inventories.
observations.reason: As of year-end of CY 2017, some of the storage facilities were already rehabilitated like the City Health Office, City Engineering Office, and other, but the treatment of outright expense of inventories still existed.

observations.recommendation: (a) direct the City Accountant to (i) thresh out details of PPE booked in bulk; (ii) provide corresponding depreciation to each property involved as soon as the necessary data are already in place; and (iii) prepare Journal Entry Vouchers for the adjustments of accounts in the provision of depreciation expense; and (b) require the erring officials/employees to render justification for the prolong/delayed action on the above deficiency. Any unsatisfied justification may cause disciplinary action against them.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant has already prepared reconciliation template laying down the PPEs recorded in their database and identifying those PPEs to be reconciled with the GSO records.
observations.reason: Provision of depreciation still not undertaken due to incomplete reconciliation of PPE accounts Accounting and General Services records.

observations.recommendation:
1. Require the concerned accountable officers to (a) prepare a schedule that properly explain/present their accountabilities indicating therein (a) chronological sequence of official receipts (OR) and other accountable forms issued; (b) the corresponding amount collected per accountable form; (c) total amount collected; and (d) other data, either in electronic or hard copies; and (b) attach the schedule to the Report of Collections and Deposits together with the duplicate copies of official receipts and validated deposit slips.

2. Revisit the existing control on the review of Report of Collections and Deposits and identify loopholes that may cause deficiencies of the said report and recommend measures to enhance the control system on collections.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant already prepared reconciliation template laying down the PPEs recorded in their database and identifying those PPEs to be reconciled with the GSO records.
observations.reason: The massive collection in the market has already supported with detailed report wherein users of the report could fully understanding the nature of the collections.

observations.recommendation: (a) direct all the concerned officials/employees to immediately settle the unliquidated cash advances; (b) instruct the City Accountant to (i) effect deduction of their unliquidated liabilities from their salaries if they still fail to submit liquidation report relating to the unaccounted cash advances; and (ii) coordinate with national agencies top management to direct those officials/employees previously assigned to the City Government to immediately submit liquidation report; and (c) adhere to the regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant has already refrained from granting additional cash advances unless otherwise the previous advance is liquidated. Adherence to the regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances was already religiously observed.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2017, unliquidated cash advances totaled at P53,731,054.94, a decreased of P3,617,926.95 or 6.3% compared to last year’s balance. However, the City Accountant still need to enhance its strategies to reduce more this amount by coordinating the national agencies top management to direct those officials/employees previously assigned to the City Government to settle long standing unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation: Direct the concerned officials to conduct a validation of occupants of all the units/stalls and identify those lessees who subleased the same and effect termination/cancellation of the contracts.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer with the coordination of the City Legal Officer have conducted validation of occupants/lessees which is still in progress.
observations.reason: Inquiry with the concerned officials disclosed, validation still in progress.

observations.recommendation: (a) instruct the OIC-General Services Officer to (i) officially request the GSIS to conduct evaluation of properties for the consideration in the insurance plan coverage; (ii) coordinate with the City Budget Officer in the estimation of premiums that may be incurred for the insurance plan; and (iii) fast track the completion of the physical inventory report; (b) advise the City Budget Officer to include in the supplemental budget, if any, expenditures for the insurance plan premium.
observations.managementAction: CGSO has officially requested the GSIS to conduct evaluation of properties for consideration in the insurance plan on April 7, 2016.
observations.reason: During 2017, GSO made a tracer letter to GSIS for the evaluation of properties that can be qualified for insurance plan.  GSIS has visited the City Hall for the said evaluation.  As of year-end, still awaits the result of the same.

observations.recommendation: (a) direct all the concerned officials/employees to refund the excess payment of PEI or instruct the City Accountant to deduct the same from their claims; and (b) refrain from committing the same error and adhere to the law in the granting of PEI.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant effected the deduction of the excess amount from the claims of retired personnel only.  The City Government plan to make an appeal with the COA Regional Office within the prescribed period of six months.
observations.reason: COA resolved that CY 2014 PEI was regular and with legal basis per COA Decision No. 2017-199 dated June 28, 2017.

observations.recommendation: (a) require the concerned officials to refund the amount upon receipt of notices of disallowance and refrain from incurring similar transactions in the future; and (b) adhere to the regulations on the austerity measures adopted under Administrative Order 103.
observations.managementAction: The City Government has stopped the practice adherence to AO 103.
observations.reason: The practice was already discontinued.

observations.recommendation: (a) require the City Engineer to (i) carefully prepare the POW with prudence in their estimation in order to arrive at reasonable project cost; and (ii) maintain an updated price monitoring or database of construction materials and labor by conducting regular canvass of prices not only in Surigao City but also in other places and in website of known suppliers/manufacturers in order to obtain the most reasonable price; and (b) initiate the material test to be conducted by appropriate office to vouch the good quality of product and its reasonable price.
observations.managementAction: Payments of infrastructure projects showed that material test report was already attached.  Careful preparation of POW was effected.
observations.reason: Despite of management justification, the error was sustained by COA TAS and Notices of Disallowance were issued. Refund was initially effected amounting P119,593.03 out of P796,628.70 excess amount.

observations.recommendation: (a) instruct the concerned officers to adhere the procurement law; (b) instruct the City Accountant to refrain from granting cash advance for the procurement of goods and services instead conduct the procurement process as required by RA 9184, otherwise this may result to appropriate sanction towards erring officials; (c) direct the concerned officials to refund the amount; and (d) advise the DepEd School Board to prepare the SEF Budget earlier than the usual in order to have ample time in the procurement of goods and services especially those projects/activities held in the first quarter of the year.
observations.managementAction: Early preparation and approval of School Board Budget was made in CY 2017.  It was promulgated on November 17, 2016.  The City Accountant and Budget Officer have already stopped the grant of cash advance relative to this activity.
observations.reason: Review of Advances to Officers and Employees recorded in the ePPSAS ledger, the grant of cash advance relative to this activity was already stopped. The concerned official claimed that adherence with applicable regulations was already effected.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the concerned officers to adhere strictly the regulations on granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Same above

observations.recommendation: (a) the Sangguniang Panglunsod: (i) fast track the evaluation and approval of the proposed LDRRMF Investment Plan in order to immediately implement the projects/activities; (ii) in the future similar case, give priority to the review and approval of disaster related programs/projects in order to enable the LGU to be prepared for the possible disaster and avoid massive damages to life and property, otherwise the higher authorities will point to the SP as one of the responsible for the unwanted massive damages; and (b) the Management: (i) follow up and coordinate with the SP for the immediate provision of required documents and explanation/justification on issues raised; (ii) in the future similar case, plan the proposed projects effectively duly supported with detailed data in order not to cause delay of its review and approval; and (iii) advise the LDRRM Council to identify the specific projects/activities for a more effective and efficient Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction:
The SP Presiding Officer claimed that fast tracking process was observed considering that LDRMF programs/projects is very critical or urgent. However, approval could not be done immediately due to lack of supporting documents of the identified programs/activities/projects in the Investment Plan.
On the other hand, Management exhausted all means to fast track its approval by giving necessary supporting documents to the SP for their thorough understanding.

observations.recommendation: Direct the City Accountant and the OIC-City General Services Officer to revisit the existing internal control system particularly the inspection of deliveries and enhance the system by: (i) having a regular reshuffling of Inspectorate Team to avoid closeness/intimacy to the suppliers/contractors; (ii) monitor the activities of the inspectors by conducting a random re-inspection of deliveries to ensure non-recurrence of the deficiencies discussed above and warn them to refrain from doing again otherwise appropriate legal action may be taken; and (iii) require the concerned suppliers to deliver the lacking items or this may be disallowed in audit and warn them to refrain from doing again otherwise appropriate legal action may be taken against them.
observations.reason: Though the City Accountant has exerted efforts to enhance the internal control system, reshuffling of Inspectorate Team was not undertaken.

observations.recommendation: (a) direct all the concerned officials/employees to observe the proper management of inventories and properties in order to comply the basic requirements for internal control system; (b) consider establishing an adequate storage space as one of the projects that will be undertaken by the City Government in the near future in order to protect the inventories and properties from possible theft, fire and deterioration; and (c) advise the CEO to utilize the supplies and materials for the intended purpose otherwise this may result to audit suspension or disallowance.
observations.reason: Inspection of bodega disclosed that CEO has undertaken rehabilitation of dilapidated storage structure while City Health Office has established a storage room where the medical supplies were in place and organized. observations.managementAction: The City Agriculturist ensured that there is a MOA between the City Government and the People’s Organization prior to the implementation of the project to protect the interest of the City Government and the funding agency.

observations.recommendation: (a) hire a Manager to undertake the affairs of the economic enterprise; (b) meantime, advise the City Treasurer to enforce immediate collection of the delinquent lease accounts and if still unsettled despite of the grace period given, apply the termination/cancellation of the contracts; (c) direct the concerned officials to (i) revisit the existing monitoring control on economic enterprise and identify loopholes for enhancement in order to avoid the recurrence and/or accumulation of significant uncollected rental fees; and (ii) maintain an updated ledger for each lessee.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer and Legal Officer have coordinated in the enforcement of collection of delinquent accounts thru issuance of demand letters to lessees. Final demand letters were also issued awaiting for the grace period to elapse before filing case for ejectment.

Significant delinquent balances still existed despite demand letters issued to the lessees. No termination/cancellation of contracts yet issued. Hiring of Manager to undertake the economic enterprise operations was not yet effected.
