observations.recommendation: (a) hire a Manager to undertake the affairs of the economic enterprise; (b) meantime, instruct the City Treasurer and City Legal Officer to (i) exert extra effort to retrieve the contracts and supporting documents and store the same in the safe vault; (ii) renew expired contracts, if any; and (c) in the future similar case, ensure that contract documents are sufficient of pertinent data forming part of the agreement in order to become legal and binding.
observations.managementAction: The City Legal Officer had submitted copies of lease contracts to the Office of the Auditor; and The City Treasurer and Legal Officer coordinated to ensure contracts are complete of important data to make the documents valid and enforceable.  Sample contracts were deficient of important data such as (i) date when contract was perfected; (ii) data under acknowledgement of the contract and signature of the notary public; (iii) signature of the City Government representative; and (iv) signatures of both parties and witnesses in every page of the contract, which might affect the validity of documents.
observations.reason: Hiring of Manager to undertake the economic enterprise operations was not yet effected.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the City Engineer to: (a) revisit the existing controls in the implementation of project particularly in the right water-cement ratio or the standard slump of the concrete mix during concrete pouring as this is detrimental on the strength of the concrete pavement; (b) identify loopholes and install mechanism to strengthen the supervision and monitoring controls in order to avoid recurrence of the noted deficiency in the future; and (c) direct persons responsible/liable to effect remedial action by installing pressurized concrete epoxy to the affected parts or removable or replacement of the affected blocks at their own expense otherwise this may result to audit disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The City Engineer to comply with the recommendations –; material testing to be included in the POW, conduct materials testing and submit the result; For the City Engineer to assign a project engineer and establish systems and procedures in monitoring; Submit a report of the remedial action done.
observations.reason: Remedial action by the City Engineer was not effectively done. Scaling of concrete blocks was worsen. It can be recalled that this project was completed in June 2015 and barely three months the defects were noticeable.

observations.recommendation: (a) direct the concerned officers to find ways and means to procure the lacking item as soon as possible, install the equipment in place at the recipient beneficiary premise and conduct hands-on training on the proper use of the equipment.  Also, require the supplier to provide manual of the equipment delivered and its warranty.  Otherwise this may result in audit disallowance amounting to P320,178.00; (b) impose sanction for failure to deliver the complete set of equipment; (c) advise the BAC to recommend blacklisting of the supplier; and (d) in the future similar case, include in the project proposal the orientation or hands-on training for highly technical equipment, warranty and manual of the equipment.
observations.managementAction: The city Agriculture Office has already hired expert to install and orient the cooperative in operating the equipment. The concerned officials assured that in future similar case, they will include in the project proposal the orientation or hands-on training for highly technical equipment, warranty and manual of the equipment.
observations.reason: The City Agriculture Office verbally informed the Audit Team that the equipment was already installed and operated by the Cooperative. However, the Audit Team still awaits for the schedule of actual operation of the equipment at MAFVEMCO.

observations.recommendation: (a) direct all the concerned officials/employees to (i) coordinate with the end-users of the project during the planning phase in order to fully define their needs; and (ii) fast track completion of the projects in order to attain the BUB objectives; (b) advise the Accountant to coordinate with the implementing offices for the timely submission of the liquidation reports of the funds to the source agency in order to avoid delay in the succeeding releases; (c) advise the City Agriculturist to (i) prepare a more comprehensive project proposal with back-up computation and the required process period for agriculture and marine projects to explain the quantity of required materials/supplies, in future similar projects; (ii) maintain an updated roster of suppliers for agriculture and marine products needed for the implementation of projects; (iii) coordinate with the City Accountant for the timely preparation and submission of the liquidation reports; (d) instruct the City Government Officials involved in the monitoring of BUB projects to strengthen the monitoring control by conducting a timely evaluation and inspection of projects in order to address immediately the identified problems or hindrances for its timely completion; and (e) in future similar transaction, advise the City Engineer to conduct effective and efficient planning thru project site inspection by technical personnel in coordination with beneficiaries to avoid delay and adjustments in the implementation of project.
observations.managementAction: The Local Poverty Reduction Action Team (LPRAT) is more vigilant in the implementation of BUB projects thru regular effective monitoring and meetings among the committee members for prompt completion of projects in accordance with plans and specifications. The City Agriculture Office has already maintained a roster of suppliers of agricultural and marine products.
observations.reason: Evaluation of projects under BUB program showed that the LPRAT has improved monitoring system thru regular meetings discussing the status of implementation as well as the prompt submission of liquidation reports to source agencies. However, coordination of LGU officials to the beneficiaries or end-users during the planning of the projects still need to be enhanced and the prompt completion of the projects.

observations.recommendation: (a) direct all the concerned officials/employees to (i) demand from the supplier the immediate delivery of lacking construction materials to project site; and (ii) fast track completion of the projects; (b) advise the Accountant to (i) impose liquidated damages against the claims of the supplier; and (ii) submit timely the liquidation report of the fund transfer to source agency to avoid delay in the succeeding releases; (c) instruct the concerned officer to blacklist the supplier; and (d) in future similar transaction, advise the City Engineer to conduct effective and efficient planning of the projects by (i) preparing appropriate plans/specifications and/or drawings; (ii) project site inspection by technical personnel to avoid delay and adjustments in the implementation of project.
observations.managementAction: The City Engineer ensure that planning of the projects will entails preparing appropriate plans/specificatio ns and/or drawings and project site inspection will be conducted by the technical personnel to avoid delay and adjustments in the implementation of project.
observations.reason: All of the projects involved herewith were already completed and operational. However, report on the imposition of liquidated damages to suppliers was not submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: (a) adhere to the regulations on real property tax as provided in Sections 254 of RA 7160; (b) advise the City Treasurer to (i) conduct more drastic action for the collection of delinquent accounts by issuing notices to defaulting tax payers and warrant of levy before the public auction; (ii) install a sound monitoring control on real property tax; (c) advise the City Accountant to continue motivating the barangay officials to assist in collecting the delinquent accounts of property taxes located at their concerned barangays by releasing promptly their share of property tax collected duly supported with template on the computation of shares; and (d) direct the City Assessor to coordinate with the City Treasurer in the reclassification of problematic accounts in order to exclude from the delinquent accounts.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer has issued warrant of levies to island barangays and this year at Barangay Taft. By 2018, list of accounts will be established for possible auction.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2017, accumulate delinquent accounts totaled at P65,400,119.03 net of problematic accounts. The amount still significant, had this be collected can contribute development of the City.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Heads of BPLO and City Treasury to – (i) intensify collection by conducting inspection on site to ascertain the real condition of the business operations and enforce collections of long outstanding delinquent accounts; (ii) impose appropriate action as provided under Article 272 of IRR of RA 7160 to businesses that are found continuously operating  with unrenewed permit; (iii) conduct monitoring of accounts by regular inspection to business addresses and update of ledger accounts; and (iv) reconcile records of BPLO and City Treasury Office relative to all delinquent accounts and come up with the harmonized data deleting those inactive accounts for the top management’s decision making.
observations.managementAction: Monitoring of accounts were conducted and
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2017, the accumulated delinquent accounts still existed.

observations.recommendation: Direct the CSWD Officer to – (i) find an appropriate and sanitized venue for the distribution of goods in order to protect these from contamination from bad elements; (ii) store the goods in enclosed room and assign personnel accountable for easy pin pointing of responsibility in case of losses; and (iii) continue the inspection of goods by City Veterinary representative in order to maintain quality of deliveries in accordance with the specifications as indicated in contract/order.
observations.reason: Delivery, storage and distribution of goods still conducted at the Office of the CSWD.

observations.recommendation: Require the accountable officers to prepare a schedule that properly explain/present their accountabilities indicating therein (a) chronological sequence of official receipts (OR) and other accountable forms issued; (b) the corresponding amount collected per accountable form; (c) total amount collected; and (d) other data, either in electronic or hard copies; and attach the schedule to the Report of Collections and Deposits together with the duplicate copies of official receipts and validated deposit slips. Further, revisit the existing control on the review of Report of Collections and Deposits; identify loopholes that may cause deficiencies of the said report and recommend measures to enhance the control system on collections.
observations.managementAction: Issue memorandum to the accountable officers to comply with the recommendations of COA; Strengthen system of collection; to pursue IT system; Institute strong control in monitoring collection and deposit of the money collected.

observations.recommendation: Direct all the concerned officials/employees to immediately settle the unliquidated cash advances; Instruct the City Accountant to (i) deduct from the salaries of the concerned officials/personnel the long outstanding advances if they fail to submit liquidation report at due time; (ii) refrain from granting additional cash advances unless otherwise the previous advance is liquidated; and (iii) coordinate with national agencies top management to direct those officials/employees previously assigned in the City Government to immediately submit liquidation report; Adhere to the regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Refer paragraph No.8, pages 54 to 56 of this report.

observations.recommendation: Adhere to the regulations on real property tax as provided in Section 246 of RA 7160; and advise the City Treasurer to coordinate in the setting up of the Real Property Tax Receivable and Deferred Real Property Tax Income by providing data to the City Accountant as basis in the preparation of JEV in the first day of the year.
observations.managementAction: Accounting Office has already complied Section 246 of RA 7160. Accrual was effected in the beginning of CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: Advise the City Treasurer and Accountant to (i) coordinate in the setting up of receivables on real property taxes on the first day of every year; and (ii) prioritize the reconciliation of affected accounts, particularly the problematic accounts and effect adjustment upon achieving the harmonized records of the two offices.

Encourage and challenge the Inventory Team to complete the physical count of inventories and properties within CY 2015 and immediately prepare the RPCPPE and RPCI; Advise all offices within the City Government, particularly those which are not yet done with the physical count to coordinate with the Inventory Team by preparing the RPCPPE and RPCI for properties assigned and inventories issued to them in order to fast track the undertaking; and Provide a safe and sufficient storage of inventory and property items which should be accessed only by the custodian.
observations.reason: The GSO has already completed the physical count of PPE, but the inventories remained not conducted.

observations.recommendation: Direct all officials/employees involved in the procurement process to stop the practice of advance withdrawal of goods without procurement documents and strictly comply with the regulations pertaining to the purchases of supplies and materials as mentioned above, otherwise may cause appropriate legal action against erring officials/employees; Instruct the City Accountant to comply the regulations on the recording of purchases of supplies and materials as mentioned above; Consider as priority, the provision of an adequate and safe storage room for inventories; and Instruct the General Services Officer to comply the requirement on the preparation of SSMI every end of the month to record the issuance of goods.

observations.recommendation: Direct the City Accountant to (i) thresh out details of PPE booked in bulk; (ii) provide corresponding depreciation to each property involved as soon as the necessary data is already in place; and (iii) prepare Journal Entry Vouchers for the adjustments of accounts involved in the provision of depreciation expense; Require the erring officials/employees justification for the prolong/delayed action on the above deficiency; any unsatisfied justification may cause disciplinary action against them.

Create a task force to conduct the reconciliation of affected accounts composing of personnel from Accounting and General Services Offices as soon as the physical count is complete; Prepare the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to adjust reconciling items identified with sufficient and appropriate supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: Advise the Inventory Committee to consider the physical count as one of the priority activities that should be completed within CY 2015; Advise the City Accountant to coordinate with the General Services Officer for the reconciliation of accounts as soon as the physical count is complete; and Prepare a detailed inventory report on other assets account to support the outstanding balance.

observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer and the City Accountant has conducted initial reconciliation of these accounts.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the City Accountant to (i) exercise due care in the preparation of JEVs using the eNGAS by providing sufficient and necessary data; and (ii) strengthen review of JEVs particularly the completeness of data before approval.

observations.reason: Effective January 2016, the Accounting Office has established an in-house ePPSAS accounting recording. Featured in this program is the Ageing of advances which showed unreliable data. Thereby, manual ageing still conducted.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the concerned officers to adhere strictly the regulations on granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Same above
observations.reason: Refer Paragraph No. 15, page 69 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
(a) stop the practice of allowing the same personnel to initiate, undertake and review/inspection of the projects/activities; and

(b) instruct the concerned officer to revisit the existing control system considering the non-segregation of incompatible duties as discussed above and its attribution of insufficiency of personnel and effect the enhancement to conform with the regulations under Section 123 and 124 of the PD 1445.
observations.reason: Reiterated under paragraph 16, pages 70 to 72 of this report.

observations.recommendation: (i) adhere to the regulations of inventory and property management as provided in Section 114 of NGAS Manual Volume II; (ii) designate personnel to (a) handle the recording of purchases and issuances of inventories and PPE; and (b) maintain the appropriate ledger and stock/property cards – not necessarily to hire personnel for this purpose but assign additional load to a reliable personnel; and (iii) design a systematic and effective control in recording of properties and supplies.
observations.reason: As far as inventories is concern, recording of purchases of inventories is outright expense, thus, no inventory account was recognized which is deviation to PPSAS 12. (Refer discussion under paragraph no. 2, pages 44 to 47 of this report). While PPE accounts were not already back-up with Property Ledger Card in Accounting Office and Property Card in GSO. Thereby, the concerned offices find it difficult to conduct the reconciliation of PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: Comply with pertinent provisions in COA Circular No, 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 pertaining submission of monthly report of resources and utilization of LDRRMF to concerned offices.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, no monthly report on sources and utilization of the same was submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
1.9   We recommended that Management should:


           (a)   create a task  force  to  fast  track  the  completion  of
              reconciliation of  PPE Accounts in GSO and Accounting Office;



           (b)  prepare  the  Journal  Entry  Vouchers   (JEVs)   for   the
              reconciling items supported with sufficient  and  appropriate
              supporting documents; and


           (c) submit immediately the JEV together with the  reconciliation
              statements and sufficient/ appropriate  supporting  documents
              to the Office of the Auditor for review.

2.14  We recommended that Management:


           (a)   direct the City Accountant and General Services Officer in
              coordination with all offices of the City Government  to  (i)
              perform a physical count of inventories in order to establish
              the actual inventory; (ii) consolidate all inventory on-hand;
              and (iii) conduct reconciliation  of  inventory  accounts  to
              arrive at the accurate and actual amounts;


           (b) instruct the City Accountant to (i) comply  PPSAS  12,  that
              is,  record  the  purchases  of  supplies  and  materials  as
              inventory and recognize  expense  when  the  inventories  are
              consumed or distributed; and (ii) prepare JEV to  adjust  the
              reconciling items;


           (c)  allocate  a  budget  for  establishment  of  a  storage  or
              warehouse for the safekeeping of supplies  and  materials  in
              order  to  protect  the  same   against   theft,   fire   and
              deterioration and these should  be  controlled  by  the  City
              General Services Officer; and


           (d)   meantime the warehouse is not yet in place,  instruct  the
              City  Accountant  to  further  study  her  proposal  on   the
              segregation of  custodianship and issuance of inventories  by
              office and if found  feasible  and  compliant  to  PPSAS  12,
              recommend for implementation.

Require the (a) City Treasurer to conduct reconciliation with the Accounting records in order to produce accurate data for the adjustments of affected accounts; (b) City Accountant and City Assessor to coordinate in the reconciliation of affected accounts, particularly the problematic accounts and effect necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction:
The City Treasurer and the City Accountant has conducted initial reconciliation of these accounts.

The Vice Mayor explained that this issue is one of the priorities identified during the CY 2016 Executive and Legislative Agenda to maximize collection.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, accumulated delinquent account was increased to P81,146,245.98 or a difference of P3,213,400.48 compared to last year balance of P77,932,845.50. This account still was not recorded in the books of accounts.

observations.managementAction:
The City General Services Officer initiated the proposal on the establishment of a safe storage structure.  Hopefully the budget will be realized in CY 2018 Annual Budget with an estimated cost of P10 million;

The City Accountant proposes to segregate the custodianship and issuance of inventories per office.  Then, consolidation will be conducted in GSO to support the issuance of JEVs as far as receipt and consumption of inventories.
observations.reason: As of year-end of CY 2016, the storage facility was still not realized, so much so that the practice still existed. Reiterated under paragraph 2, page 44 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
(a) direct the City Accountant to (i) thresh out details of PPE booked in bulk; (ii) provide corresponding depreciation to each property involved as soon as the necessary data are already in place; and (iii) prepare Journal Entry Vouchers for the adjustments of accounts in the provision of depreciation expense; and

observations.recommendation:
1. Require the concerned accountable officers to

(a) prepare a schedule that properly explain/present their accountabilities indicating therein (a) chronological sequence of official receipts (OR) and other accountable forms issued; (b) the corresponding amount collected per accountable form; (c) total amount collected; and (d) other data, either in electronic or hard copies; and

(b) attach the schedule to the Report of Collections and Deposits together with the duplicate copies of official receipts and validated deposit slips.

2. Revisit the existing control on the review of Report of Collections and Deposits and identify loopholes that may cause deficiencies of the said report and recommend measures to enhance the control system on collections.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office has completed the in-house system for collection and remittance/deposit.
observations.reason: The in-house information system developed by the Accounting Office for collections and deposits was still not used by the accountable officers in wet market, integrated bus terminal, and other offices.

observations.managementAction: The City Accountant has already refrained from granting additional cash advances unless otherwise the previous advance is liquidated. Adherence to the regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances was already religiously observed.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, unliquidated cash advances totaled at P54,257,531.25, a increased of P4,281,007.58 compared to last year’s balance. However, the City Accountant still need to enhance its strategies to reduce more this amount by coordinating the national agencies top management to direct those officials/employees previously assigned to the City Government to settle long standing unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation: Direct the concerned officials to conduct a validation of occupants of all the units/stalls and identify those lessees who subleased the same and effect termination/cancellation of the contracts.

observations.recommendation:
(a) instruct the OIC-General Services Officer to (i) officially request the GSIS to conduct evaluation of properties for the consideration in the insurance plan coverage; (ii) coordinate with the City Budget Officer in the estimation of premiums that may be incurred for the insurance plan; and (iii) fast track the completion of the physical inventory report; and

The City Treasurer and Legal Officer have coordinated in the enforcement of collection of delinquent accounts thru issuance of demand letters to lessees.

This also the reason that hinders the inclusion in the Annual Budget the insurance plan premium. It is advised that the GSO make a tracer of the letter request to the GSIS

observations.recommendation:

The City Accountant effected the deduction of the excess amount from the claims of retired personnel only.

The City Government plan to make an appeal with the COA Regional Office within the prescribed period of six months.

Early preparation and approval of School Board Budget was made in CY 2016.

Review of Advances to Officers and Employees recorded in the ePPSAS ledger, the grant of cash advance relative to this activity was already stopped. observations.recommendation:
(a) the Sangguniang Panglunsod: (i) fast track the evaluation and approval of the proposed LDRRMF Investment Plan in order to immediately implement the projects/activities; (ii) in the future similar case, give priority to the review and approval of disaster related programs/projects in order to enable the LGU to be prepared for the possible disaster and avoid massive damages to life and property, otherwise the higher authorities will point to the SP as one of the responsible for the unwanted massive damages; and

(b) the Management: (i) follow up and coordinate with the SP for the immediate provision of required documents and explanation/justification on issues raised; (ii) in the future similar case, plan the proposed projects effectively duly supported with detailed data in order not to cause delay of its review and approval; and advise the LDRRM Council to identify the specific projects/activities for a more effective and efficient Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction:
The SP Presiding Officer claimed that fast tracking process was observed considering that LDRRMF programs/projects is very critical or urgent.  However, approval could not be done immediately due to lack of supporting documents of the identified programs/activities/projects.

On the other hand, Management exhausted all means to fast track its approval by giving necessary supporting documents to the SP for their thorough understanding.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Paragraph No. 32, pages 87 to 90 of this report.

observations.recommendation: Direct the City Accountant and the OIC-City General Services Officer to revisit the existing internal control system particularly the inspection of deliveries and enhance the system by: (i) having a regular reshuffling of Inspectorate Team to avoid closeness/intimacy to the suppliers/contractors; (ii) monitor the activities of the inspectors by conducting a random re-inspection of deliveries to ensure non-recurrence of the deficiencies discussed above and warn them to refrain from doing again otherwise appropriate legal action may be taken; and (iii) require the concerned suppliers to deliver the lacking items or this may be disallowed in audit and warn them to refrain from doing again otherwise appropriate legal action may be taken against them.
observations.reason: Though the City Accountant has exerted efforts to enhance the internal control system, reshuffling of Inspectorate Team was not undertaken.

observations.recommendation:

The City Treasurer and Legal Officer have coordinated in the enforcement of collection of delinquent accounts thru issuance of demand letters to lessees. Final demand letters were also issued awaiting for the grace period to elapse before filing case for ejectment.

observations.reason: Hiring of Manager to undertake the economic enterprise operations was not yet effected.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the City Engineer to: (a) revisit the existing controls in the implementation of project particularly in the right water-cement ratio or the standard slump of the concrete mix during concrete pouring as this is detrimental on the strength of the concrete pavement; (b) identify loopholes and install mechanism to strengthen the supervision and monitoring controls in order to avoid recurrence of the noted deficiency in the future; and (c) direct persons responsible/liable to effect remedial action by installing pressurized concrete epoxy to the affected parts or removable or replacement of the affected blocks at their own expense otherwise this may result to audit disallowance.
observations.managementAction:
The City Engineer to comply with the recommendations –; material testing to be included in the POW, conduct materials testing and submit the result;

For the City Engineer to assign a project engineer and establish systems and procedures in monitoring; Submit a report of the remedial action done.
observations.reason: Remedial action by the City Engineer was not effectively done. Scaling of concrete blocks was worsen. It can be recalled that this project was completed in June 2015 and barely three months the defects were noticeable. Refer photos of the project label as Annex AO.

observations.recommendation:
(a) direct the concerned officers to find ways and means to procure the lacking item as soon as possible, install the equipment in place at the recipient beneficiary premise and conduct hands-on training on the proper use of the equipment.  Also, require the supplier to provide manual of the equipment delivered and its warranty.  Otherwise this may result in audit disallowance amounting to P320,178.00;

(b) impose sanction for failure to deliver the complete set of equipment; (c) advise the BAC to recommend blacklisting of the supplier; and (d) in the future similar case, include in the project proposal the orientation or hands-on training for highly technical equipment, warranty and manual of the equipment.
observations.managementAction:
After more than a year of stocking the equipment at the stairway along the City Agriculture Office, turn-over the equipment was made to the beneficiary.

The concerned officials assured that in future similar case, they will include in the project proposal the orientation or hands-on training for highly technical equipment, warranty and manual of the equipment.
observations.reason: The lacking items were already procured. The equipment was turned over to the MAFVEMCO in January 13, 2017. However, ocular inspection of the equipment in the MAFVEMCO on March 18, 2017, the same still uninstalled and not operational since the laboratory building where the equipment will be installed still not complete. Still no manual to operate and hands-on training provided. Annex AP present the photos of the same.

observations.managementAction:
The Local Poverty Reduction Action Team (LPRAT) is more vigilant in the implementation of BUB projects thru regular effective monitoring and meetings among the committee members for prompt completion of projects in accordance with plans and specifications.

The City Agriculture Office has already maintained a roster of suppliers of agricultural and marine products.
observations.reason: Evaluation of projects under BUB program showed that the LPRAT has improved monitoring system thru regular meetings discussing the status of implementation as well as the prompt submission of liquidation reports to source agencies. However, coordination of LGU officials to the beneficiaries or end-users during the planning of the projects still need to be enhanced and the prompt completion of the projects.

observations.recommendation: (a) direct all the concerned officials/employees to (i) demand from the supplier the immediate delivery of lacking construction materials to project site; and (ii) fast track completion of the projects; (b) advise the Accountant to (i) impose liquidated damages against the claims of the supplier; and (ii) submit timely the liquidation report of the fund transfer to source agency to avoid delay in the succeeding releases; (c) instruct the concerned officer to blacklist the supplier; and (d) in future similar transaction, advise the City Engineer to conduct effective and efficient planning of the projects by (i) preparing appropriate plans/specifications and/or drawings; (ii) project site inspection by technical personnel to avoid delay and adjustments in the implementation of project.
observations.managementAction: The City Engineer ensure that planning of the projects will entails preparing appropriate plans/specifications and/or drawings and project site inspection will be conducted by the technical personnel to avoid delay and adjustments in the implementation of the project.
observations.reason: All of the projects involved herewith were already completed and operational. However, liquidation report for projects implemented in CY 2016 still incurred delay. Report on the imposition of liquidated damages to suppliers was not submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: (a) adhere to the regulations on real property tax as provided in Sections 254 of RA 7160; (b) advise the City Treasurer to (i) conduct more drastic action for the collection of delinquent accounts by issuing notices to defaulting tax payers and warrant of levy before the public auction; (ii) install a sound monitoring control on real property tax; and (c) advise the City Accountant to continue motivating the barangay officials to assist in collecting the delinquent accounts of property taxes located at their concerned barangays by releasing promptly their share of property tax collected duly supported with template on the computation of shares; and (d) direct the City Assessor to coordinate with the City Treasurer in the reclassification of problematic accounts in order to exclude from the delinquent accounts.

observations.recommendation:

observations.managementAction: Monitoring of accounts were conducted and

observations.recommendation:

(i) find an appropriate and sanitized venue for the distribution of goods in order to protect these from contamination from bad elements; (ii) store the goods in enclosed room and assign personnel accountable for easy pinpointing of responsibility in case of losses; and (iii) continue the inspection of goods by City Veterinary representative in order to maintain quality of deliveries in accord with the specifications as indicated in the contract/order.

observations.recommendation: Submit the report of accountability for accountable forms together with sufficient and appropriate documents to support data on the receipt and issuance/cancellation of accountable forms.

observations.recommendation: Require the accountable officers to prepare a schedule that properly explain/present their accountabilities indicating therein (a) chronological sequence of official receipts (OR) and other accountable forms issued; (b) the corresponding amount collected per accountable form; (c) total amount collected; and (d) other data, either in electronic or hard copies; and Attach the schedule to the Report of Collections and Deposits together with the duplicate copies of official receipts and validated deposit slips. But for the whole calendar year 2015, the office has not started any changes to effect the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: Direct all the concerned officials/employees to immediately settle the unliquidated cash advances; Instruct the City Accountant to (i) deduct from the salaries of the concerned officials/personnel the long outstanding advances if they fail to submit liquidation report at due time; and (ii) refrain from granting additional cash advances unless otherwise the previous advance is liquidated; and Adhere to the regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

observations.recommendation: Create a task force to conduct the reconciliation of affected accounts composing of personnel from Accounting and General Services Offices as soon as the physical count is complete; Prepare the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to adjust reconciling items identified with sufficient and appropriate supporting documents.
observations.reason: Reiterated under paragraph 1, pages 42 to 44 of this report.

observations.recommendation: Advise the Inventory Committee to consider the physical count as one of the priority activities that should be completed within CY 2015; Advise the City Accountant to coordinate with the General Services Officer for the reconciliation of accounts as soon as the physical count is complete; and Prepare a detailed inventory report on other assets account to support the outstanding balance.
observations.managementAction: General Services Office.
observations.reason: This account is included in the cleaning of PPE accounts by the GSO and Accounting Office which is still in progress.

observations.recommendation: Adhere to the regulations on payment of salaries through ATM; Advise the City Treasurer and City Accountant to coordinate in the implementation on payment of salaries thru ATM; and Secure a written consent from the job-order and contractual personnel before pursuing the automated payment of salaries/wages.
observations.managementAction: City Personnel Officer and Accountant coordinated to register more job-order personnel to ATM accounts.  As of cut-off date, several JOs were processing their bank accounts thru DBP.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, only 379 or approximately 24 percent job-order personnel who are paid their wages thru ATM.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to coordinate in the submission of disbursement vouchers together with supporting documents as detailed in Annex AU.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant and the City Treasurer have coordinated the complete submission of DVs to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: Conduct an intense planning of infrastructure projects considering the identified causes of delay especially on limited resources; Advise the CEO to conduct a regular monitoring of the implementation of infrastructure project; and Make representation with the Sangguniang Panlungsod and Sangguniang Panglalawigan for the timely approval of the Annual Budget as provided for under Sections 319 and 327 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The City Engineering Office submitted a written explanation indicating therein the reasons of its delay.
observations.reason: Reiterated under paragraph 27, pages 81 to 84 of this report.

observations.recommendation: Advise the City Treasurer to: (i) intensify collection of delinquent accounts by prompting the issuance of notices to defaulting tax payers; (ii) install a sound monitoring control on real property tax; and Advise the City Accountant to motivate the barangay officials to assist in collecting the delinquent accounts of property taxes located at their concerned barangays by prompting the release of share of property tax collected duly supported with template on the computation of shares.
observations.managementAction:
The City Treasurer and the City Accountant has conducted initial reconciliation of these accounts.

The Vice Mayor explained that this issue is one of the priorities identified during the CY 2016 Executive and Legislative Agenda to maximize collection.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, accumulated delinquent account was increased to P81,146,245.98 or a difference of P3,213,400.48 compared to last year balance of P77,932,845.50. This account still was not recorded in the books of accounts. Refer AAR 2015, Finding 1, page 91 of this report.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the City Accountant to (i) exercise due care in the preparation of JEVs using the eNGAS by providing sufficient and necessary data; and (ii) strengthen review of JEVs particularly the completeness of data before approval.
observations.reason: Refer AAR 2015, Finding 5, page 93 of this report.
